metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'hashMD5')] LIKE '%SHA256=c2cfd23fdc6c0e1b1ffa0e545cbe556f18d11b362b4a89ba0713f6ab01c4827f%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'hashMD5')] LIKE '%SHA256=53f8adbd76c0eb16f5e43cadde422474d8a06f9c8f959389c1930042ad8beaa5%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'hashMD5')] LIKE '%SHA256=648c8d2f8001c113d2986dd00b7bbd181593d462bef73522cee212c4f71f95b3%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'hashMD5')] LIKE '%SHA256=ae047e2095e46c3f9c518b2be67ec753f4f0aad23b261a361fcb6144dcdb63b4%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'hashMD5')] LIKE '%SHA256=3153d2baa462978dd22ab33d1c2274ecc88c200225d6a3327f98d5b752d08f5c%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'hashMD5')] LIKE '%SHA256=f49cde976e628012c9db73e1c8d76081944ecf2297cdafeb78bb13290da274c4%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'hashMD5')] LIKE '%SHA256=d2513e58bb03ccc83affde685c6ef987924c37ce6707d8e9857e2524b0d7e90f%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'hashMD5')] LIKE '%SHA256=bb67c7623ba92fe64ffd9816b8d5b3b1ea3013960a30bd4cf6e295b3eb5b1bad%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'hashMD5')] LIKE '%SHA256=b34b3c3a91e3165d1481f0b3ec23eab93a1cfba94345a6cbfe5b18ddbd48eac7%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'hashMD5')] LIKE '%SHA256=f7848034e010d55f15e474ca998f96391e320ff29b00cfcc4c5e536529703e75%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'hashMD5')] LIKE '%SHA256=b6fc9493778cbe3bfc062d73f5cc604bc0ff058bc5e5dc6aac87f3a4008b54b6%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'hashMD5')] LIKE '%SHA256=f5c5e962577e2293c4ad10603816dce7cc273585969615fbf4e4bfa9eaff1688%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'hashMD5')] LIKE '%SHA256=d14c52d9220b606f428a8fe9f7c108b0d6f14cf71e7384749e98e6a95962e68f%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'hashMD5')] LIKE '%SHA256=d3a0e1a79158f3985cd49607ebe0cdfcc49cb9af96b8f43aefd0cdfe2f22e663%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'hashMD5')] LIKE '%SHA256=2fbbfc8299537ff80cadf9d0e27c223fe0ccb9052bf9d8763ad717bbfa521c77%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'hashMD5')] LIKE '%SHA256=19074674c6fbdaa573b3081745e5e26144fdf7a086d14e0e220d1814f1f13078%')))
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%-url%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%tunnel%'))
  OR ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.exe -url%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.exe --url%'))))
  OR indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%-url%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%-no-autoupdate%')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Legitimate usage of Cloudflare Quick Tunnel

Cloudflared Tunnel Connections Cleanup

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: T1102, T1090, T1572 Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects execution of the "cloudflared" tool with the tunnel "cleanup" flag in order to cleanup tunnel connections.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Cloudflared Tunnel Connections Cleanup
-- Sigma ID:     7050bba1-1aed-454e-8f73-3f46f09ce56a
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1102, T1090, T1572
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2023-05-17
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_cloudflared_tunnel_cleanup.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate usage of Cloudflared.
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% tunnel %' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%cleanup %')
    AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%-config %' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%-connector-id %')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Legitimate usage of Cloudflared.

Cloudflared Tunnel Execution

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: T1102, T1090, T1572 Author: Janantha Marasinghe, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects execution of the "cloudflared" tool to connect back to a tunnel. This was seen used by threat actors to maintain persistence and remote access to compromised networks.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Cloudflared Tunnel Execution
-- Sigma ID:     9a019ffc-3580-4c9d-8d87-079f7e8d3fd4
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1102, T1090, T1572
-- Author:       Janantha Marasinghe, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2023-05-17
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_cloudflared_tunnel_run.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate usage of Cloudflared tunnel.
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% tunnel %' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% run %')
    AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%-config %' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%-credentials-contents %' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%-credentials-file %' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%-token %')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Legitimate usage of Cloudflared tunnel.

Change Default File Association Via Assoc

low FSM Severity: 3 MITRE: persistence, T1546.001 Author: Timur Zinniatullin, oscd.community Status: test
Detects file association changes using the builtin "assoc" command. When a file is opened, the default program used to open the file (also called the file association or handler) is checked. File association selections are stored in the Windows Registry and can be edited by users, administrators, or programs that have Registry access or by administrators using the built-in assoc utility. Applications can modify the file association for a given file extension to call an arbitrary program when a file with the given extension is opened.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Change Default File Association Via Assoc
-- Sigma ID:     3d3aa6cd-6272-44d6-8afc-7e88dfef7061
-- Level:        low  |  FSM Severity: 3
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        persistence | T1546.001
-- Author:       Timur Zinniatullin, oscd.community
-- Date:         2019-10-21
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_cmd_assoc_execution.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Admin activity
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%assoc%')
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\cmd.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'Cmd.Exe')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Admin activity

Change Default File Association To Executable Via Assoc

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: persistence, T1546.001 Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects when a program changes the default file association of any extension to an executable. When a file is opened, the default program used to open the file (also called the file association or handler) is checked. File association selections are stored in the Windows Registry and can be edited by users, administrators, or programs that have Registry access or by administrators using the built-in assoc utility. Applications can modify the file association for a given file extension to call an arbitrary program when a file with the given extension is opened.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Change Default File Association To Executable Via Assoc
-- Sigma ID:     ae6f14e6-14de-45b0-9f44-c0986f50dc89
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        persistence | T1546.001
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2022-06-28
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_cmd_assoc_tamper_exe_file_association.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND NOT (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.exe=exefile%'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Copy .DMP/.DUMP Files From Remote Share Via Cmd.EXE

high FSM Severity: 7 Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects usage of the copy builtin cmd command to copy files with the ".dmp"/".dump" extension from a remote share
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Copy .DMP/.DUMP Files From Remote Share Via Cmd.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     044ba588-dff4-4918-9808-3f95e8160606
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        (none)
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2022-09-27
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_cmd_copy_dmp_from_share.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%copy %' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% \\\\\\\\%')
    AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.dmp%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.dump%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.hdmp%')))
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\cmd.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'Cmd.Exe')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Curl Download And Execute Combination

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1218, T1105 Author: Sreeman, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Adversaries can use curl to download payloads remotely and execute them. Curl is included by default in Windows 10 build 17063 and later.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Curl Download And Execute Combination
-- Sigma ID:     21dd6d38-2b18-4453-9404-a0fe4a0cc288
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1218, T1105
-- Author:       Sreeman, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2020-01-13
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_cmd_curl_download_exec_combo.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================
-- UNSUPPORTED_MODIFIER: contains|windash

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (rawEventMsg LIKE '% -c %'
    AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%curl %' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%http%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%-o%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%&%'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

File Deletion Via Del

low FSM Severity: 3 MITRE: T1070.004 Author: frack113 Status: test
Detects execution of the builtin "del"/"erase" commands in order to delete files. Adversaries may delete files left behind by the actions of their intrusion activity. Malware, tools, or other non-native files dropped or created on a system by an adversary may leave traces to indicate to what was done within a network and how. Removal of these files can occur during an intrusion, or as part of a post-intrusion process to minimize the adversary's footprint.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        File Deletion Via Del
-- Sigma ID:     379fa130-190e-4c3f-b7bc-6c8e834485f3
-- Level:        low  |  FSM Severity: 3
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1070.004
-- Author:       frack113
-- Date:         2022-01-15
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_cmd_del_execution.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    False positives levels will differ Depending on the environment. You can use a combination of ParentImage and other keywords from the CommandLine field to filter legitimate activity
-- ============================================================
-- UNSUPPORTED_MODIFIER: contains|windash

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%del %' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%erase %'))
  AND (rawEventMsg LIKE '% -f%' OR rawEventMsg LIKE '% -s%' OR rawEventMsg LIKE '% -q%')
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\cmd.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'Cmd.Exe')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: False positives levels will differ Depending on the environment. You can use a combination of ParentImage and other keywords from the CommandLine field to filter legitimate activity

Greedy File Deletion Using Del

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: T1070.004 Author: frack113 , X__Junior (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects execution of the "del" builtin command to remove files using greedy/wildcard expression. This is often used by malware to delete content of folders that perhaps contains the initial malware infection or to delete evidence.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Greedy File Deletion Using Del
-- Sigma ID:     204b17ae-4007-471b-917b-b917b315c5db
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1070.004
-- Author:       frack113 , X__Junior (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2021-12-02
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_cmd_del_greedy_deletion.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%del %' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%erase %'))
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\\\\\*.au3%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\\\\\*.dll%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\\\\\*.exe%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\\\\\*.js%'))
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\cmd.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'Cmd.Exe')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

File And SubFolder Enumeration Via Dir Command

low FSM Severity: 3 MITRE: discovery, T1217 Author: frack113 Status: test
Detects usage of the "dir" command part of Windows CMD with the "/S" command line flag in order to enumerate files in a specified directory and all subdirectories.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        File And SubFolder Enumeration Via Dir Command
-- Sigma ID:     7c9340a9-e2ee-4e43-94c5-c54ebbea1006
-- Level:        low  |  FSM Severity: 3
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        discovery | T1217
-- Author:       frack113
-- Date:         2021-12-13
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_cmd_dir_execution.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Likely
-- ============================================================
-- UNSUPPORTED_MODIFIER: contains|windash

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (rawEventMsg LIKE '%dir*-s%'
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\cmd.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'Cmd.Exe')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Likely

Potential Dosfuscation Activity

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: execution, T1059 Author: frack113, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects possible payload obfuscation via the commandline
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potential Dosfuscation Activity
-- Sigma ID:     a77c1610-fc73-4019-8e29-0f51efc04a51
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        execution | T1059
-- Author:       frack113, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2022-02-15
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_cmd_dosfuscation.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%^^%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%^|^%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%,;,%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%;;;;%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%;; ;;%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%(,(,%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\%COMSPEC:~%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% c^m^d%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%^c^m^d%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% c^md%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% cm^d%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%^cm^d%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% s^et %' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% s^e^t %' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% se^t %'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Command Line Execution with Suspicious URL and AppData Strings

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: execution, T1059.003, T1059.001, T1105 Author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Jonhnathan Ribeiro, oscd.community Status: test
Detects a suspicious command line execution that includes an URL and AppData string in the command line parameters as used by several droppers (js/vbs > powershell)
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Command Line Execution with Suspicious URL and AppData Strings
-- Sigma ID:     1ac8666b-046f-4201-8aba-1951aaec03a3
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        execution | T1059.003, T1059.001, T1105
-- Author:       Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Jonhnathan Ribeiro, oscd.community
-- Date:         2019-01-16
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_cmd_http_appdata.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    High
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\cmd.exe'
    AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%http%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%://%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\%AppData\%%'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: High

Cmd Launched with Hidden Start Flags to Suspicious Targets

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: T1564.003 Author: Vladan Sekulic, Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems) Status: experimental
Detects cmd.exe executing commands with the "start" utility using "/b" (no window) or "/min" (minimized) flags. To reduce false positives from standard background tasks, detection is restricted to scenarios where the target is a known script extension or located in suspicious temporary/public directories. This technique was observed in Chaos, DarkSide, and Emotet malware campaigns.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Cmd Launched with Hidden Start Flags to Suspicious Targets
-- Sigma ID:     5a6b7c8d-9e0f-1a2b-3c4d-5e6f7a8b9c0d
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       experimental
-- MITRE:        T1564.003
-- Author:       Vladan Sekulic, Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2026-01-24
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_cmd_launched_with_hidden_start_flag.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate administrative scripts running from temporary folders.; Niche software updaters utilizing hidden batch files in ProgramData.
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND 1=1
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Legitimate administrative scripts running from temporary folders.; Niche software updaters utilizing hidden batch files in ProgramData.

Potential Privilege Escalation Using Symlink Between Osk and Cmd

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: persistence, T1546.008 Author: frack113 Status: test
Detects the creation of a symbolic link between "cmd.exe" and the accessibility on-screen keyboard binary (osk.exe) using "mklink". This technique provides an elevated command prompt to the user from the login screen without the need to log in.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potential Privilege Escalation Using Symlink Between Osk and Cmd
-- Sigma ID:     e9b61244-893f-427c-b287-3e708f321c6b
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        persistence | T1546.008
-- Author:       frack113
-- Date:         2022-12-11
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_cmd_mklink_osk_cmd.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%mklink%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\osk.exe%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\cmd.exe%')
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\cmd.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'Cmd.Exe')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

VolumeShadowCopy Symlink Creation Via Mklink

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1003.002, T1003.003 Author: Teymur Kheirkhabarov, oscd.community Status: stable
Shadow Copies storage symbolic link creation using operating systems utilities
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        VolumeShadowCopy Symlink Creation Via Mklink
-- Sigma ID:     40b19fa6-d835-400c-b301-41f3a2baacaf
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       stable
-- MITRE:        T1003.002, T1003.003
-- Author:       Teymur Kheirkhabarov, oscd.community
-- Date:         2019-10-22
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_cmd_mklink_shadow_copies_access_symlink.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate administrator working with shadow copies, access for backup purposes
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%mklink%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%HarddiskVolumeShadowCopy%')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Legitimate administrator working with shadow copies, access for backup purposes

Suspicious File Execution From Internet Hosted WebDav Share

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: execution, T1059.001 Author: pH-T (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects the execution of the "net use" command to mount a WebDAV server and then immediately execute some content in it. As seen being used in malicious LNK files
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Suspicious File Execution From Internet Hosted WebDav Share
-- Sigma ID:     f0507c0f-a3a2-40f5-acc6-7f543c334993
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        execution | T1059.001
-- Author:       pH-T (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2022-09-01
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_cmd_net_use_and_exec_combo.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% net use http%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%& start /b %' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\DavWWWRoot\\%')
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.exe %' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.dll %' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.bat %' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.vbs %' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.ps1 %'))
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\cmd.exe%')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'Cmd.EXE')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Cmd.EXE Missing Space Characters Execution Anomaly

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: execution, T1059.001 Author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects Windows command lines that miss a space before or after the /c flag when running a command using the cmd.exe. This could be a sign of obfuscation of a fat finger problem (typo by the developer).
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Cmd.EXE Missing Space Characters Execution Anomaly
-- Sigma ID:     a16980c2-0c56-4de0-9a79-17971979efdd
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        execution | T1059.001
-- Author:       Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2022-08-23
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_cmd_no_space_execution.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND 1=1
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

NtdllPipe Like Activity Execution

high FSM Severity: 7 Author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects command that type the content of ntdll.dll to a different file or a pipe in order to evade AV / EDR detection. As seen being used in the POC NtdllPipe
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        NtdllPipe Like Activity Execution
-- Sigma ID:     bbc865e4-7fcd-45a6-8ff1-95ced28ec5b2
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        (none)
-- Author:       Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2022-03-05
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_cmd_ntdllpipe_redirect.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%type \%windir\%\\system32\\ntdll.dll%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%type \%systemroot\%\\system32\\ntdll.dll%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%type c:\\windows\\system32\\ntdll.dll%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\\\ntdll.dll > \\\\\\\\.\\\\pipe\\\\%'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Potential CommandLine Path Traversal Via Cmd.EXE

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: execution, T1059.003 Author: xknow @xknow_infosec, Tim Shelton Status: test
Detects potential path traversal attempt via cmd.exe. Could indicate possible command/argument confusion/hijacking
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potential CommandLine Path Traversal Via Cmd.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     087790e3-3287-436c-bccf-cbd0184a7db1
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        execution | T1059.003
-- Author:       xknow @xknow_infosec, Tim Shelton
-- Date:         2020-06-11
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_cmd_path_traversal.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Java tools are known to produce false-positive when loading libraries
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND 1=1
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Java tools are known to produce false-positive when loading libraries

Potentially Suspicious Ping/Copy Command Combination

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: T1070.004 Author: X__Junior (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects uncommon and potentially suspicious one-liner command containing both "ping" and "copy" at the same time, which is usually used by malware.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potentially Suspicious Ping/Copy Command Combination
-- Sigma ID:     ded2b07a-d12f-4284-9b76-653e37b6c8b0
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1070.004
-- Author:       X__Junior (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2023-07-18
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_cmd_ping_copy_combined_execution.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================
-- UNSUPPORTED_MODIFIER: contains|windash

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%ping%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%copy %')
  AND rawEventMsg LIKE '% -n %'
  AND rawEventMsg LIKE '% -y %'
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\cmd.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'Cmd.Exe')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Suspicious Ping/Del Command Combination

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1070.004 Author: Ilya Krestinichev Status: test
Detects a method often used by ransomware. Which combines the "ping" to wait a couple of seconds and then "del" to delete the file in question. Its used to hide the file responsible for the initial infection for example
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Suspicious Ping/Del Command Combination
-- Sigma ID:     54786ddc-5b8a-11ed-9b6a-0242ac120002
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1070.004
-- Author:       Ilya Krestinichev
-- Date:         2022-11-03
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_cmd_ping_del_combined_execution.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================
-- UNSUPPORTED_MODIFIER: contains|windash

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%ping%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%del %')
  AND rawEventMsg LIKE '% -n %'
  AND (rawEventMsg LIKE '% -f %' OR rawEventMsg LIKE '% -q %')
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%Nul%'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Potentially Suspicious CMD Shell Output Redirect

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: T1218 Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects inline Windows shell commands redirecting output via the ">" symbol to a suspicious location. This technique is sometimes used by malicious actors in order to redirect the output of reconnaissance commands such as "hostname" and "dir" to files for future exfiltration.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potentially Suspicious CMD Shell Output Redirect
-- Sigma ID:     8e0bb260-d4b2-4fff-bb8d-3f82118e6892
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1218
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2022-07-12
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_cmd_redirection_susp_folder.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate admin or third party scripts used for diagnostic collection might generate some false positives
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\cmd.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'Cmd.Exe'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Legitimate admin or third party scripts used for diagnostic collection might generate some false positives

Directory Removal Via Rmdir

low FSM Severity: 3 MITRE: T1070.004 Author: frack113 Status: test
Detects execution of the builtin "rmdir" command in order to delete directories. Adversaries may delete files left behind by the actions of their intrusion activity. Malware, tools, or other non-native files dropped or created on a system by an adversary may leave traces to indicate to what was done within a network and how. Removal of these files can occur during an intrusion, or as part of a post-intrusion process to minimize the adversary's footprint.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Directory Removal Via Rmdir
-- Sigma ID:     41ca393d-538c-408a-ac27-cf1e038be80c
-- Level:        low  |  FSM Severity: 3
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1070.004
-- Author:       frack113
-- Date:         2022-01-15
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_cmd_rmdir_execution.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%/s%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%/q%'))
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\cmd.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'Cmd.Exe'))
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%rmdir%'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Copy From VolumeShadowCopy Via Cmd.EXE

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: impact, T1490 Author: Max Altgelt (Nextron Systems), Tobias Michalski (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects the execution of the builtin "copy" command that targets a shadow copy (sometimes used to copy registry hives that are in use)
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Copy From VolumeShadowCopy Via Cmd.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     c73124a7-3e89-44a3-bdc1-25fe4df754b1
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        impact | T1490
-- Author:       Max Altgelt (Nextron Systems), Tobias Michalski (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2021-08-09
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_cmd_shadowcopy_access.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Backup scenarios using the commandline
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%copy %' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\\\\\\\\\?\\\\GLOBALROOT\\\\Device\\\\HarddiskVolumeShadowCopy%')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Backup scenarios using the commandline

Read Contents From Stdin Via Cmd.EXE

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: execution, T1059.003 Author: frack113, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detect the use of "<" to read and potentially execute a file via cmd.exe
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Read Contents From Stdin Via Cmd.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     241e802a-b65e-484f-88cd-c2dc10f9206d
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        execution | T1059.003
-- Author:       frack113, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2023-03-07
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_cmd_stdin_redirect.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%<%')
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'Cmd.Exe'))
  OR (procName LIKE '%\\cmd.exe'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Sticky Key Like Backdoor Execution

critical FSM Severity: 9 MITRE: persistence, T1546.008 Author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), @twjackomo, Jonhnathan Ribeiro, oscd.community Status: test
Detects the usage and installation of a backdoor that uses an option to register a malicious debugger for built-in tools that are accessible in the login screen
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Sticky Key Like Backdoor Execution
-- Sigma ID:     2fdefcb3-dbda-401e-ae23-f0db027628bc
-- Level:        critical  |  FSM Severity: 9
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        persistence | T1546.008
-- Author:       Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), @twjackomo, Jonhnathan Ribeiro, oscd.community
-- Date:         2018-03-15
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_cmd_sticky_key_like_backdoor_execution.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unlikely
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] AS parentImage,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\winlogon.exe')
    AND (procName LIKE '%\\cmd.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\cscript.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\mshta.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\powershell.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\pwsh.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\regsvr32.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\rundll32.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\wscript.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\wt.exe')
    AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%sethc.exe%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%utilman.exe%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%osk.exe%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%Magnify.exe%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%Narrator.exe%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%DisplaySwitch.exe%')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unlikely

Persistence Via Sticky Key Backdoor

critical FSM Severity: 9 MITRE: persistence, T1546.008 Author: Sreeman Status: test
By replacing the sticky keys executable with the local admins CMD executable, an attacker is able to access a privileged windows console session without authenticating to the system. When the sticky keys are "activated" the privilleged shell is launched.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Persistence Via Sticky Key Backdoor
-- Sigma ID:     1070db9a-3e5d-412e-8e7b-7183b616e1b3
-- Level:        critical  |  FSM Severity: 9
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        persistence | T1546.008
-- Author:       Sreeman
-- Date:         2020-02-18
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_cmd_sticky_keys_replace.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unlikely
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%copy %' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%/y %' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%C:\\windows\\system32\\cmd.exe C:\\windows\\system32\\sethc.exe%')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unlikely

Potential Download/Upload Activity Using Type Command

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: T1105 Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects usage of the "type" command to download/upload data from WebDAV server
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potential Download/Upload Activity Using Type Command
-- Sigma ID:     aa0b3a82-eacc-4ec3-9150-b5a9a3e3f82f
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1105
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2022-12-14
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_cmd_type_arbitrary_file_download.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%type \\\\\\\\%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% > %')
  OR indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%type %' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% > \\\\\\\\%')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Unusual Parent Process For Cmd.EXE

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: execution, T1059 Author: Tim Rauch, Elastic (idea) Status: test
Detects suspicious parent process for cmd.exe
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Unusual Parent Process For Cmd.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     4b991083-3d0e-44ce-8fc4-b254025d8d4b
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        execution | T1059
-- Author:       Tim Rauch, Elastic (idea)
-- Date:         2022-09-21
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_cmd_unusual_parent.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] AS parentImage,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\cmd.exe'
    AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\csrss.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\ctfmon.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\dllhost.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\epad.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\FlashPlayerUpdateService.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\GoogleUpdate.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\jucheck.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\jusched.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\LogonUI.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\lsass.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\regsvr32.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\SearchIndexer.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\SearchProtocolHost.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\SIHClient.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\sihost.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\slui.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\spoolsv.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\sppsvc.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\taskhostw.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\unsecapp.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\WerFault.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\wermgr.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\wlanext.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\WUDFHost.exe')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

New Generic Credentials Added Via Cmdkey.EXE

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: T1003.005 Author: frack113, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects usage of "cmdkey.exe" to add generic credentials. As an example, this can be used before connecting to an RDP session via command line interface.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        New Generic Credentials Added Via Cmdkey.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     b1ec66c6-f4d1-4b5c-96dd-af28ccae7727
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1003.005
-- Author:       frack113, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2023-02-03
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_cmdkey_adding_generic_creds.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate usage for administration purposes
-- ============================================================
-- UNSUPPORTED_MODIFIER: contains|windash

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (rawEventMsg LIKE '% -g%'
  AND rawEventMsg LIKE '% -p%'
  AND rawEventMsg LIKE '% -u%'
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\cmdkey.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'cmdkey.exe')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Legitimate usage for administration purposes

Potential Reconnaissance For Cached Credentials Via Cmdkey.EXE

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1003.005 Author: jmallette, Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects usage of cmdkey to look for cached credentials on the system
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potential Reconnaissance For Cached Credentials Via Cmdkey.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     07f8bdc2-c9b3-472a-9817-5a670b872f53
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1003.005
-- Author:       jmallette, Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2019-01-16
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_cmdkey_recon.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate administrative tasks
-- ============================================================
-- UNSUPPORTED_MODIFIER: contains|windash

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (rawEventMsg LIKE '% -l%'
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\cmdkey.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'cmdkey.exe')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Legitimate administrative tasks

Potential Arbitrary File Download Via Cmdl32.EXE

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: execution, T1218, T1202 Author: frack113 Status: test
Detects execution of Cmdl32 with the "/vpn" and "/lan" flags. Attackers can abuse this utility in order to download arbitrary files via a configuration file. Inspect the location and the content of the file passed as an argument in order to determine if it is suspicious.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potential Arbitrary File Download Via Cmdl32.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     f37aba28-a9e6-4045-882c-d5004043b337
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        execution | T1218, T1202
-- Author:       frack113
-- Date:         2021-11-03
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_cmdl32_arbitrary_file_download.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%/vpn%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%/lan%')
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\cmdl32.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'CMDL32.EXE')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

CMSTP Execution Process Creation

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: execution, T1218.003 Author: Nik Seetharaman Status: stable
Detects various indicators of Microsoft Connection Manager Profile Installer execution
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        CMSTP Execution Process Creation
-- Sigma ID:     7d4cdc5a-0076-40ca-aac8-f7e714570e47
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       stable
-- MITRE:        execution | T1218.003
-- Author:       Nik Seetharaman
-- Date:         2018-07-16
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_cmstp_execution_by_creation.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate CMSTP use (unlikely in modern enterprise environments)
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] AS parentImage,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\cmstp.exe')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Legitimate CMSTP use (unlikely in modern enterprise environments)

OpenEDR Spawning Command Shell

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: execution, T1059.003, T1021.004, T1219 Author: @kostastsale Status: experimental
Detects the OpenEDR ssh-shellhost.exe spawning a command shell (cmd.exe) or PowerShell with PTY (pseudo-terminal) capabilities. This may indicate remote command execution through OpenEDR's remote management features, which could be legitimate administrative activity or potential abuse of the remote access tool. Threat actors may leverage OpenEDR's remote shell capabilities to execute commands on compromised systems, facilitating lateral movement or other command-and-control operations.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        OpenEDR Spawning Command Shell
-- Sigma ID:     7f3a9c2d-4e8b-4a7f-9d3e-5c6f8a9b2e1d
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       experimental
-- MITRE:        execution | T1059.003, T1021.004, T1219
-- Author:       @kostastsale
-- Date:         2026-02-19
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_comodo_ssh_shellhost_cmd_spawn.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate use of OpenEDR for remote command execution
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] AS parentImage,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%bash%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%cmd%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%powershell%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%pwsh%'))
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\ITSMService.exe')
    AND procName LIKE '%\\ssh-shellhost.exe'
    AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%--pty%')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Legitimate use of OpenEDR for remote command execution

Arbitrary File Download Via ConfigSecurityPolicy.EXE

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: exfiltration, T1567 Author: frack113 Status: test
Detects the execution of "ConfigSecurityPolicy.EXE", a binary part of Windows Defender used to manage settings in Windows Defender. Users can configure different pilot collections for each of the co-management workloads. It can be abused by attackers in order to upload or download files.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Arbitrary File Download Via ConfigSecurityPolicy.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     1f0f6176-6482-4027-b151-00071af39d7e
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        exfiltration | T1567
-- Author:       frack113
-- Date:         2021-11-26
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_configsecuritypolicy_download_file.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%ConfigSecurityPolicy.exe%'))
  OR (procName LIKE '%\\ConfigSecurityPolicy.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'ConfigSecurityPolicy.exe'))
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%ftp://%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%http://%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%https://%')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Powershell Executed From Headless ConHost Process

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: execution, T1059.001, T1059.003, T1564.003 Author: Matt Anderson (Huntress) Status: test
Detects the use of powershell commands from headless ConHost window. The "--headless" flag hides the windows from the user upon execution.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Powershell Executed From Headless ConHost Process
-- Sigma ID:     056c7317-9a09-4bd4-9067-d051312752ea
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        execution | T1059.001, T1059.003, T1564.003
-- Author:       Matt Anderson (Huntress)
-- Date:         2024-07-23
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_conhost_headless_powershell.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%--headless%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%powershell%')
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\conhost.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'CONHOST.EXE')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Suspicious High IntegrityLevel Conhost Legacy Option

informational FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: T1202 Author: frack113 Status: test
ForceV1 asks for information directly from the kernel space. Conhost connects to the console application. High IntegrityLevel means the process is running with elevated privileges, such as an Administrator context.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Suspicious High IntegrityLevel Conhost Legacy Option
-- Sigma ID:     3037d961-21e9-4732-b27a-637bcc7bf539
-- Level:        informational  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1202
-- Author:       frack113
-- Date:         2022-12-09
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_conhost_legacy_option.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Very Likely, including launching cmd.exe via Run As Administrator
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'integrityLevel')] AS integrityLevel,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'integrityLevel') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'integrityLevel')] IN ('High', 'S-1-16-12288'))
    AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%conhost.exe%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%0xffffffff%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%-ForceV1%'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Very Likely, including launching cmd.exe via Run As Administrator

Conhost.exe CommandLine Path Traversal

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: execution, T1059.003 Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
detects the usage of path traversal in conhost.exe indicating possible command/argument confusion/hijacking
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Conhost.exe CommandLine Path Traversal
-- Sigma ID:     ee5e119b-1f75-4b34-add8-3be976961e39
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        execution | T1059.003
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2022-06-14
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_conhost_path_traversal.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unlikely
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentCommand')] AS parentCommandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentCommand') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentCommand')] LIKE '%conhost%')
    AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%/../../%'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unlikely

Uncommon Child Process Of Conhost.EXE

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: T1202 Author: omkar72 Status: test
Detects uncommon "conhost" child processes. This could be a sign of "conhost" usage as a LOLBIN or potential process injection activity.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Uncommon Child Process Of Conhost.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     7dc2dedd-7603-461a-bc13-15803d132355

-- Status:       test
