Deployment Image Servicing and Management tool. DISM is used to enumerate, install, uninstall, configure, and update features and packages in Windows images
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Dism Remove Online Package
-- Sigma ID:     43e32da2-fdd0-4156-90de-50dfd62636f9
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1562.001
-- Author:       frack113
-- Date:         2022-01-16
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_dism_remove.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate script
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentCommand')] AS parentCommandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\Dism.exe'
    AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%/Online%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%/Disable-Feature%'))
  OR (procName LIKE '%\\DismHost.exe'
    AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentCommand') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentCommand')] LIKE '%/Online%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentCommand')] LIKE '%/Disable-Feature%'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Legitimate script

DLL Sideloading by VMware Xfer Utility

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: persistence, T1574.001 Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects execution of VMware Xfer utility (VMwareXferlogs.exe) from the non-default directory which may be an attempt to sideload arbitrary DLL
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        DLL Sideloading by VMware Xfer Utility
-- Sigma ID:     ebea773c-a8f1-42ad-a856-00cb221966e8
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        persistence | T1574.001
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2022-08-02
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_dll_sideload_vmware_xfer.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unlikely
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\VMwareXferlogs.exe'
  AND NOT (procName LIKE 'C:\\Program Files\\VMware\\%'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unlikely

Dllhost.EXE Execution Anomaly

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1055 Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects a "dllhost" process spawning with no commandline arguments which is very rare to happen and could indicate process injection activity or malware mimicking similar system processes.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Dllhost.EXE Execution Anomaly
-- Sigma ID:     e7888eb1-13b0-4616-bd99-4bc0c2b054b9
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1055
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2022-06-27
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_dllhost_no_cli_execution.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unlikely
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\dllhost.exe'
    AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] IN ('dllhost.exe', 'dllhost')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unlikely

DNS Exfiltration and Tunneling Tools Execution

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: exfiltration, T1048.001, T1071.004, T1132.001 Author: Daniil Yugoslavskiy, oscd.community Status: test
Well-known DNS Exfiltration tools execution
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        DNS Exfiltration and Tunneling Tools Execution
-- Sigma ID:     98a96a5a-64a0-4c42-92c5-489da3866cb0
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        exfiltration | T1048.001, T1071.004, T1132.001
-- Author:       Daniil Yugoslavskiy, oscd.community
-- Date:         2019-10-24
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_dns_exfiltration_tools_execution.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unlikely
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\iodine.exe')
  OR (procName LIKE '%\\dnscat2%')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unlikely

Unusual Child Process of dns.exe

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: persistence, T1133 Author: Tim Rauch, Elastic (idea) Status: test
Detects an unexpected process spawning from dns.exe which may indicate activity related to remote code execution or other forms of exploitation as seen in CVE-2020-1350 (SigRed)
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Unusual Child Process of dns.exe
-- Sigma ID:     a4e3d776-f12e-42c2-8510-9e6ed1f43ec3
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        persistence | T1133
-- Author:       Tim Rauch, Elastic (idea)
-- Date:         2022-09-27
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_dns_susp_child_process.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] AS parentImage,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\dns.exe')
  AND NOT (procName LIKE '%\\conhost.exe'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Potential Discovery Activity Via Dnscmd.EXE

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: discovery, execution Author: @gott_cyber Status: test
Detects an attempt to leverage dnscmd.exe to enumerate the DNS zones of a domain. DNS zones used to host the DNS records for a particular domain.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potential Discovery Activity Via Dnscmd.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     b6457d63-d2a2-4e29-859d-4e7affc153d1
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        discovery, execution
-- Author:       @gott_cyber
-- Date:         2022-07-31
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_dnscmd_discovery.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate administration use
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%/enumrecords%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%/enumzones%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%/ZonePrint%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%/info%'))
  AND procName LIKE '%\\dnscmd.exe')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Legitimate administration use

New DNS ServerLevelPluginDll Installed Via Dnscmd.EXE

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: persistence, T1574.001, T1112 Author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects the installation of a DNS plugin DLL via ServerLevelPluginDll parameter in registry, which can be used to execute code in context of the DNS server (restart required)
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        New DNS ServerLevelPluginDll Installed Via Dnscmd.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     f63b56ee-3f79-4b8a-97fb-5c48007e8573
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        persistence | T1574.001, T1112
-- Author:       Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2017-05-08
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_dnscmd_install_new_server_level_plugin_dll.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\dnscmd.exe'
    AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%/config%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%/serverlevelplugindll%'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Potential Application Whitelisting Bypass via Dnx.EXE

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: T1218, T1027.004 Author: Beyu Denis, oscd.community Status: test
Detects the execution of Dnx.EXE. The Dnx utility allows for the execution of C# code. Attackers might abuse this in order to bypass application whitelisting.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potential Application Whitelisting Bypass via Dnx.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     81ebd28b-9607-4478-bf06-974ed9d53ed7
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1218, T1027.004
-- Author:       Beyu Denis, oscd.community
-- Date:         2019-10-26
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_dnx_execute_csharp_code.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate use of dnx.exe by legitimate user
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND procName LIKE '%\\dnx.exe'
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Legitimate use of dnx.exe by legitimate user

Arbitrary DLL or Csproj Code Execution Via Dotnet.EXE

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: T1218 Author: Beyu Denis, oscd.community Status: test
Detects execution of arbitrary DLLs or unsigned code via a ".csproj" files via Dotnet.EXE.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Arbitrary DLL or Csproj Code Execution Via Dotnet.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     d80d5c81-04ba-45b4-84e4-92eba40e0ad3
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1218
-- Author:       Beyu Denis, oscd.community
-- Date:         2020-10-18
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_dotnet_arbitrary_dll_csproj_execution.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate administrator usage
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND 1=1
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Legitimate administrator usage

Binary Proxy Execution Via Dotnet-Trace.EXE

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: execution, T1218 Author: Jimmy Bayne (@bohops) Status: test
Detects commandline arguments for executing a child process via dotnet-trace.exe
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Binary Proxy Execution Via Dotnet-Trace.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     9257c05b-4a4a-48e5-a670-b7b073cf401b
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        execution | T1218
-- Author:       Jimmy Bayne (@bohops)
-- Date:         2024-01-02
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_dotnet_trace_lolbin_execution.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate usage of the utility in order to debug and trace a program.
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%-- %' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%collect%')
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\dotnet-trace.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'dotnet-trace.dll')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Legitimate usage of the utility in order to debug and trace a program.

Process Memory Dump Via Dotnet-Dump

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: T1218 Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects the execution of "dotnet-dump" with the "collect" flag. The execution could indicate potential process dumping of critical processes such as LSASS.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Process Memory Dump Via Dotnet-Dump
-- Sigma ID:     53d8d3e1-ca33-4012-adf3-e05a4d652e34
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1218
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2023-03-14
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_dotnetdump_memory_dump.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Process dumping is the expected behavior of the tool. So false positives are expected in legitimate usage. The PID/Process Name of the process being dumped needs to be investigated
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%collect%')
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\dotnet-dump.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'dotnet-dump.dll')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Process dumping is the expected behavior of the tool. So false positives are expected in legitimate usage. The PID/Process Name of the process being dumped needs to be investigated

Potential Recon Activity Using DriverQuery.EXE

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: discovery Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detect usage of the "driverquery" utility to perform reconnaissance on installed drivers
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potential Recon Activity Using DriverQuery.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     9fc3072c-dc8f-4bf7-b231-18950000fadd
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        discovery
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2023-01-19
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_driverquery_recon.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate usage by some scripts might trigger this as well
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] AS parentImage,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((procName LIKE '%driverquery.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'drvqry.exe'))
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\cscript.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\mshta.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\regsvr32.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\rundll32.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\wscript.exe')))
  OR ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\AppData\\Local\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\Users\\Public\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\Windows\\Temp\\%'))))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Legitimate usage by some scripts might trigger this as well

DriverQuery.EXE Execution

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: discovery Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detect usage of the "driverquery" utility. Which can be used to perform reconnaissance on installed drivers
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        DriverQuery.EXE Execution
-- Sigma ID:     a20def93-0709-4eae-9bd2-31206e21e6b2
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        discovery
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2023-01-19
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_driverquery_usage.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate use by third party tools in order to investigate installed drivers
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (procName LIKE '%driverquery.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'drvqry.exe'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Legitimate use by third party tools in order to investigate installed drivers

Potentially Over Permissive Permissions Granted Using Dsacls.EXE

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: T1218 Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects usage of Dsacls to grant over permissive permissions
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potentially Over Permissive Permissions Granted Using Dsacls.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     01c42d3c-242d-4655-85b2-34f1739632f7
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1218
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2022-06-20
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_dsacls_abuse_permissions.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate administrators granting over permissive permissions to users
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% /G %')
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\dsacls.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'DSACLS.EXE'))
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%GR%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%GE%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%GW%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%GA%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%WP%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%WD%')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Legitimate administrators granting over permissive permissions to users

Potential Password Spraying Attempt Using Dsacls.EXE

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: T1218 Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects possible password spraying attempts using Dsacls
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potential Password Spraying Attempt Using Dsacls.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     bac9fb54-2da7-44e9-988f-11e9a5edbc0c
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1218
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2022-06-20
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_dsacls_password_spray.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate use of dsacls to bind to an LDAP session
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%/user:%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%/passwd:%')
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\dsacls.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'DSACLS.EXE')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Legitimate use of dsacls to bind to an LDAP session

Domain Trust Discovery Via Dsquery

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: discovery, T1482 Author: E.M. Anhaus, Tony Lambert, oscd.community, omkar72 Status: test
Detects execution of "dsquery.exe" for domain trust discovery
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Domain Trust Discovery Via Dsquery
-- Sigma ID:     3bad990e-4848-4a78-9530-b427d854aac0
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        discovery | T1482
-- Author:       E.M. Anhaus, Tony Lambert, oscd.community, omkar72
-- Date:         2019-10-24
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_dsquery_domain_trust_discovery.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate use of the utilities by legitimate user for legitimate reason
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%trustedDomain%')
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\dsquery.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'dsquery.exe')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Legitimate use of the utilities by legitimate user for legitimate reason

Suspicious Kernel Dump Using Dtrace

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: discovery, T1082 Author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects suspicious way to dump the kernel on Windows systems using dtrace.exe, which is available on Windows systems since Windows 10 19H1
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Suspicious Kernel Dump Using Dtrace
-- Sigma ID:     7124aebe-4cd7-4ccb-8df0-6d6b93c96795
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        discovery | T1082
-- Author:       Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2021-12-28
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_dtrace_kernel_dump.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%syscall:::return%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%lkd(%')
  OR (procName LIKE '%\\dtrace.exe'
    AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%lkd(0)%'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Potential Windows Defender AV Bypass Via Dump64.EXE Rename

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1003.001 Author: Austin Songer @austinsonger, Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects when a user is potentially trying to bypass the Windows Defender AV by renaming a tool to dump64.exe and placing it in the Visual Studio folder. Currently the rule is covering only usage of procdump but other utilities can be added in order to increase coverage.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potential Windows Defender AV Bypass Via Dump64.EXE Rename
-- Sigma ID:     129966c9-de17-4334-a123-8b58172e664d
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1003.001
-- Author:       Austin Songer @austinsonger, Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2021-11-26
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_dump64_defender_av_bypass_rename.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (procName LIKE ':\\Program Files%'
    AND procName LIKE '%\\Microsoft Visual Studio\\%'
    AND procName LIKE '%\\dump64.exe')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

DumpMinitool Execution

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: T1036, T1003.001 Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), Florian Roth (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects the use of "DumpMinitool.exe" a tool that allows the dump of process memory via the use of the "MiniDumpWriteDump"
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        DumpMinitool Execution
-- Sigma ID:     dee0a7a3-f200-4112-a99b-952196d81e42
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1036, T1003.001
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2022-04-06
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_dumpminitool_execution.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% Full%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% Mini%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% WithHeap%'))
  AND ((procName LIKE '%\\DumpMinitool.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\DumpMinitool.x86.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\DumpMinitool.arm64.exe'))
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] IN ('DumpMinitool.exe', 'DumpMinitool.x86.exe', 'DumpMinitool.arm64.exe'))))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Suspicious DumpMinitool Execution

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1036, T1003.001 Author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects suspicious ways to use the "DumpMinitool.exe" binary
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Suspicious DumpMinitool Execution
-- Sigma ID:     eb1c4225-1c23-4241-8dd4-051389fde4ce
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1036, T1003.001
-- Author:       Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2022-04-06
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_dumpminitool_susp_execution.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((procName LIKE '%\\DumpMinitool.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\DumpMinitool.x86.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\DumpMinitool.arm64.exe'))
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] IN ('DumpMinitool.exe', 'DumpMinitool.x86.exe', 'DumpMinitool.arm64.exe')))
  OR indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.txt%')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

New Capture Session Launched Via DXCap.EXE

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: T1218 Author: Beyu Denis, oscd.community, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects the execution of "DXCap.EXE" with the "-c" flag, which allows a user to launch any arbitrary binary or windows package through DXCap itself. This can be abused to potentially bypass application whitelisting.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        New Capture Session Launched Via DXCap.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     60f16a96-db70-42eb-8f76-16763e333590
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1218
-- Author:       Beyu Denis, oscd.community, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2019-10-26
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_dxcap_arbitrary_binary_execution.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate execution of dxcap.exe by legitimate user
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% -c %')
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\DXCap.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'DXCap.exe')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Legitimate execution of dxcap.exe by legitimate user

Esentutl Gather Credentials

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: T1003, T1003.003 Author: sam0x90 Status: test
Conti recommendation to its affiliates to use esentutl to access NTDS dumped file. Trickbot also uses this utilities to get MSEdge info via its module pwgrab.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Esentutl Gather Credentials
-- Sigma ID:     7df1713a-1a5b-4a4b-a071-dc83b144a101
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1003, T1003.003
-- Author:       sam0x90
-- Date:         2021-08-06
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_esentutl_params.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    To be determined
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%esentutl%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% /p%')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: To be determined

Copying Sensitive Files with Credential Data

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1003.002, T1003.003 Author: Teymur Kheirkhabarov, Daniil Yugoslavskiy, oscd.community Status: test
Files with well-known filenames (sensitive files with credential data) copying
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Copying Sensitive Files with Credential Data
-- Sigma ID:     e7be6119-fc37-43f0-ad4f-1f3f99be2f9f
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1003.002, T1003.003
-- Author:       Teymur Kheirkhabarov, Daniil Yugoslavskiy, oscd.community
-- Date:         2019-10-22
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_esentutl_sensitive_file_copy.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Copying sensitive files for legitimate use (eg. backup) or forensic investigation by legitimate incident responder or forensic investigator.
-- ============================================================
-- UNSUPPORTED_MODIFIER: contains|windash

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((rawEventMsg LIKE '%vss%' OR rawEventMsg LIKE '% /m %' OR rawEventMsg LIKE '% /y %')
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\esentutl.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = '\esentutl.exe')))
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\config\\RegBack\\sam%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\config\\RegBack\\security%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\config\\RegBack\\system%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\config\\sam%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\config\\security%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\config\\system %' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\repair\\sam%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\repair\\security%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\repair\\system%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\windows\\ntds\\ntds.dit%'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Copying sensitive files for legitimate use (eg. backup) or forensic investigation by legitimate incident responder or forensic investigator.

Esentutl Steals Browser Information

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: collection, T1005 Author: frack113 Status: test
One way Qbot steals sensitive information is by extracting browser data from Internet Explorer and Microsoft Edge by using the built-in utility esentutl.exe
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Esentutl Steals Browser Information
-- Sigma ID:     6a69f62d-ce75-4b57-8dce-6351eb55b362
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        collection | T1005
-- Author:       frack113
-- Date:         2022-02-13
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_esentutl_webcache.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate use
-- ============================================================
-- UNSUPPORTED_MODIFIER: contains|windash

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (rawEventMsg LIKE '%-r%'
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\esentutl.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'esentutl.exe'))
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\Windows\\WebCache%'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Legitimate use

Security Event Logging Disabled via MiniNt Registry Key - Process

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: persistence, T1562.002, T1112 Author: Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems) Status: experimental
Detects attempts to disable security event logging by adding the `MiniNt` registry key. This key is used to disable the Windows Event Log service, which collects and stores event logs from the operating system and applications. Adversaries may want to disable this service to prevent logging of security events that could be used to detect their activities.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Security Event Logging Disabled via MiniNt Registry Key - Process
-- Sigma ID:     1a4bd6af-99ac-4466-b5b2-7b72b4a05462
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       experimental
-- MITRE:        persistence | T1562.002, T1112
-- Author:       Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2025-04-09
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_event_logging_disable_via_key_minint.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Highly Unlikely
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% add %' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\SYSTEM\\CurrentControlSet\\Control\\MiniNt%')
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\reg.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'reg.exe')))
  OR ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%New-Item %' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%ni %'))
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\SYSTEM\\CurrentControlSet\\Control\\MiniNt%')
  AND ((procName LIKE '%\\powershell.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\pwsh.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\powershell\_ise.exe'))
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] IN ('PowerShell.EXE', 'pwsh.dll'))))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Highly Unlikely

Potentially Suspicious Event Viewer Child Process

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1548.002 Author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects uncommon or suspicious child processes of "eventvwr.exe" which might indicate a UAC bypass attempt
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potentially Suspicious Event Viewer Child Process
-- Sigma ID:     be344333-921d-4c4d-8bb8-e584cf584780
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1548.002
-- Author:       Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2017-03-19
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_eventvwr_susp_child_process.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] AS parentImage,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\eventvwr.exe')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Potentially Suspicious Cabinet File Expansion

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: T1218 Author: Bhabesh Raj, X__Junior (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects the expansion or decompression of cabinet files from potentially suspicious or uncommon locations, e.g. seen in Iranian MeteorExpress related attacks
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potentially Suspicious Cabinet File Expansion
-- Sigma ID:     9f107a84-532c-41af-b005-8d12a607639f
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1218
-- Author:       Bhabesh Raj, X__Junior (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2021-07-30
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_expand_cabinet_files.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    System administrator Usage
-- ============================================================
-- UNSUPPORTED_MODIFIER: contains|windash

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\expand.exe'
    AND rawEventMsg LIKE '%-F:%')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: System administrator Usage

Explorer Process Tree Break

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: T1036 Author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), @gott_cyber Status: test
Detects a command line process that uses explorer.exe to launch arbitrary commands or binaries, which is similar to cmd.exe /c, only it breaks the process tree and makes its parent a new instance of explorer spawning from "svchost"
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Explorer Process Tree Break
-- Sigma ID:     949f1ffb-6e85-4f00-ae1e-c3c5b190d605
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1036
-- Author:       Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), @gott_cyber
-- Date:         2019-06-29
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_explorer_break_process_tree.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================
-- UNSUPPORTED_MODIFIER: contains|windash

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%/factory,{75dff2b7-6936-4c06-a8bb-676a7b00b24b}%')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%explorer.exe%')
    AND rawEventMsg LIKE '% /root,%')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

File Explorer Folder Opened Using Explorer Folder Shortcut Via Shell

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: discovery, T1135 Author: @Kostastsale Status: test
Detects the initial execution of "cmd.exe" which spawns "explorer.exe" with the appropriate command line arguments for opening the "My Computer" folder.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        File Explorer Folder Opened Using Explorer Folder Shortcut Via Shell
-- Sigma ID:     c3d76afc-93df-461e-8e67-9b2bad3f2ac4
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        discovery | T1135
-- Author:       @Kostastsale
-- Date:         2022-12-22
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_explorer_folder_shortcut_via_shell_binary.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] AS parentImage,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\cmd.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\powershell.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\pwsh.exe'))
    AND procName LIKE '%\\explorer.exe'
    AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%shell:mycomputerfolder%'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Explorer NOUACCHECK Flag

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1548.002 Author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects suspicious starts of explorer.exe that use the /NOUACCHECK flag that allows to run all sub processes of that newly started explorer.exe without any UAC checks
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Explorer NOUACCHECK Flag
-- Sigma ID:     534f2ef7-e8a2-4433-816d-c91bccde289b
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1548.002
-- Author:       Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2022-02-23
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_explorer_nouaccheck.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Domain Controller User Logon; Unknown how many legitimate software products use that method
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\explorer.exe'
    AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%/NOUACCHECK%'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Domain Controller User Logon; Unknown how many legitimate software products use that method

Remote File Download Via Findstr.EXE

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: T1218, T1564.004, T1552.001, T1105 Author: Furkan CALISKAN, @caliskanfurkan_, @oscd_initiative, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects execution of "findstr" with specific flags and a remote share path. This specific set of CLI flags would allow "findstr" to download the content of the file located on the remote share as described in the LOLBAS entry.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Remote File Download Via Findstr.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     587254ee-a24b-4335-b3cd-065c0f1f4baa
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1218, T1564.004, T1552.001, T1105
-- Author:       Furkan CALISKAN, @caliskanfurkan_, @oscd_initiative, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2020-10-05
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_findstr_download.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%findstr%'))
  OR (procName LIKE '%findstr.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'FINDSTR.EXE'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Findstr GPP Passwords

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1552.006 Author: frack113 Status: test
Look for the encrypted cpassword value within Group Policy Preference files on the Domain Controller. This value can be decrypted with gpp-decrypt.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Findstr GPP Passwords
-- Sigma ID:     91a2c315-9ee6-4052-a853-6f6a8238f90d
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1552.006
-- Author:       frack113
-- Date:         2021-12-27
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_findstr_gpp_passwords.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%cpassword%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\sysvol\\%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.xml%')
  AND ((procName LIKE '%\\find.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\findstr.exe'))
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] IN ('FIND.EXE', 'FINDSTR.EXE'))))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Findstr Launching .lnk File

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: T1036, T1202, T1027.003 Author: Trent Liffick Status: test
Detects usage of findstr to identify and execute a lnk file as seen within the HHS redirect attack
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Findstr Launching .lnk File
-- Sigma ID:     33339be3-148b-4e16-af56-ad16ec6c7e7b
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1036, T1202, T1027.003
-- Author:       Trent Liffick
-- Date:         2020-05-01
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_findstr_lnk.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.lnk' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.lnk"' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.lnk''))
  AND ((procName LIKE '%\\find.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\findstr.exe'))
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] IN ('FIND.EXE', 'FINDSTR.EXE'))))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

LSASS Process Reconnaissance Via Findstr.EXE

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1552.006 Author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects findstring commands that include the keyword lsass, which indicates recon actviity for the LSASS process PID
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        LSASS Process Reconnaissance Via Findstr.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     fe63010f-8823-4864-a96b-a7b4a0f7b929
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1552.006
-- Author:       Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2022-08-12
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_findstr_lsass.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================
-- UNSUPPORTED_MODIFIER: contains|windash

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%lsass%')
  AND ((procName LIKE '%\\find.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\findstr.exe'))
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] IN ('FIND.EXE', 'FINDSTR.EXE'))))
  OR (rawEventMsg LIKE '% /i "lsass%' OR rawEventMsg LIKE '% /i lsass.exe%' OR rawEventMsg LIKE '%findstr "lsass%' OR rawEventMsg LIKE '%findstr lsass%' OR rawEventMsg LIKE '%findstr.exe "lsass%' OR rawEventMsg LIKE '%findstr.exe lsass%')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Permission Misconfiguration Reconnaissance Via Findstr.EXE

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: T1552.006 Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects usage of findstr with the "EVERYONE" or "BUILTIN" keywords. This was seen being used in combination with "icacls" and other utilities to spot misconfigured files or folders permissions.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Permission Misconfiguration Reconnaissance Via Findstr.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     47e4bab7-c626-47dc-967b-255608c9a920
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1552.006
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2022-08-12
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_findstr_recon_everyone.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%"Everyone"%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%'Everyone'%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%"BUILTIN\\\\"%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%'BUILTIN\\'%'))
  AND ((procName LIKE '%\\find.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\findstr.exe'))
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] IN ('FIND.EXE', 'FINDSTR.EXE'))))
  OR indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%icacls %' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%findstr %' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%Everyone%')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Recon Command Output Piped To Findstr.EXE

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: discovery, T1057 Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), frack113 Status: test
Detects the execution of a potential recon command where the results are piped to "findstr". This is meant to trigger on inline calls of "cmd.exe" via the "/c" or "/k" for example. Attackers often time use this technique to extract specific information they require in their reconnaissance phase.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Recon Command Output Piped To Findstr.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     ccb5742c-c248-4982-8c5c-5571b9275ad3
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        discovery | T1057
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), frack113
-- Date:         2023-07-06
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_findstr_recon_pipe_output.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%ipconfig*|*find%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%net*|*find%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%netstat*|*find%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%ping*|*find%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%systeminfo*|*find%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%tasklist*|*find%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%whoami*|*find%'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Security Tools Keyword Lookup Via Findstr.EXE

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: discovery, T1518.001 Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), frack113 Status: test
Detects execution of "findstr" to search for common names of security tools. Attackers often pipe the results of recon commands such as "tasklist" or "whoami" to "findstr" in order to filter out the results. This detection focuses on the keywords that the attacker might use as a filter.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Security Tools Keyword Lookup Via Findstr.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     4fe074b4-b833-4081-8f24-7dcfeca72b42
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        discovery | T1518.001
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), frack113
-- Date:         2023-10-20
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_findstr_security_keyword_lookup.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% avira' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% avira"' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% cb' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% cb"' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% cylance' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% cylance"' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% defender' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% defender"' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% kaspersky' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% kaspersky"' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% kes' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% kes"' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% mc' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% mc"' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% sec' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% sec"' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% sentinel' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% sentinel"' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% symantec' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% symantec"' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% virus' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% virus"'))
  AND ((procName LIKE '%\\find.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\findstr.exe'))
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] IN ('FIND.EXE', 'FINDSTR.EXE'))))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Insensitive Subfolder Search Via Findstr.EXE

low FSM Severity: 3 MITRE: T1218, T1564.004, T1552.001, T1105 Author: Furkan CALISKAN, @caliskanfurkan_, @oscd_initiative, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects execution of findstr with the "s" and "i" flags for a "subfolder" and "insensitive" search respectively. Attackers sometimes leverage this built-in utility to search the system for interesting files or filter through results of commands.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Insensitive Subfolder Search Via Findstr.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     04936b66-3915-43ad-a8e5-809eadfd1141
-- Level:        low  |  FSM Severity: 3
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1218, T1564.004, T1552.001, T1105
-- Author:       Furkan CALISKAN, @caliskanfurkan_, @oscd_initiative, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2020-10-05
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_findstr_subfolder_search.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Administrative or software activity
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%findstr%'))
  OR (procName LIKE '%findstr.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'FINDSTR.EXE'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Administrative or software activity

Sysmon Discovery Via Default Driver Altitude Using Findstr.EXE

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: discovery, T1518.001 Author: frack113 Status: test
Detects usage of "findstr" with the argument "385201". Which could indicate potential discovery of an installed Sysinternals Sysmon service using the default driver altitude (even if the name is changed).
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Sysmon Discovery Via Default Driver Altitude Using Findstr.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     37db85d1-b089-490a-a59a-c7b6f984f480
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        discovery | T1518.001
-- Author:       frack113
-- Date:         2021-12-16
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_findstr_sysmon_discovery_via_default_altitude.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% 385201%')
  AND ((procName LIKE '%\\find.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\findstr.exe'))
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] IN ('FIND.EXE', 'FINDSTR.EXE'))))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Finger.EXE Execution

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1105 Author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), omkar72, oscd.community Status: test
Detects execution of the "finger.exe" utility. Finger.EXE or "TCPIP Finger Command" is an old utility that is still present on modern Windows installation. It Displays information about users on a specified remote computer (typically a UNIX computer) that is running the finger service or daemon. Due to the old nature of this utility and the rareness of machines having the finger service. Any execution of "finger.exe" can be considered "suspicious" and worth investigating.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Finger.EXE Execution
-- Sigma ID:     af491bca-e752-4b44-9c86-df5680533dbc
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1105
-- Author:       Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), omkar72, oscd.community
-- Date:         2021-02-24
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_finger_execution.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Admin activity (unclear what they do nowadays with finger.exe)
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'finger.exe'))
  OR (procName LIKE '%\\finger.exe')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Admin activity (unclear what they do nowadays with finger.exe)

Filter Driver Unloaded Via Fltmc.EXE

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: T1070, T1562, T1562.002 Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detect filter driver unloading activity via fltmc.exe
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Filter Driver Unloaded Via Fltmc.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     4931188c-178e-4ee7-a348-39e8a7a56821
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1070, T1562, T1562.002
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2023-02-13
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_fltmc_unload_driver.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND 1=1
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Sysmon Driver Unloaded Via Fltmc.EXE

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1070, T1562, T1562.002 Author: Kirill Kiryanov, oscd.community Status: test
Detects possible Sysmon filter driver unloaded via fltmc.exe
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Sysmon Driver Unloaded Via Fltmc.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     4d7cda18-1b12-4e52-b45c-d28653210df8
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1070, T1562, T1562.002
-- Author:       Kirill Kiryanov, oscd.community
-- Date:         2019-10-23
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_fltmc_unload_driver_sysmon.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unlikely
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%unload%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%sysmon%')
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\fltMC.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'fltMC.exe')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unlikely

Forfiles.EXE Child Process Masquerading

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1036 Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), Anish Bogati Status: test
Detects the execution of "forfiles" from a non-default location, in order to potentially spawn a custom "cmd.exe" from the current working directory.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Forfiles.EXE Child Process Masquerading
-- Sigma ID:     f53714ec-5077-420e-ad20-907ff9bb2958
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1036
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), Anish Bogati
-- Date:         2024-01-05
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_forfiles_child_process_masquerading.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentCommand')] AS parentCommandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentCommand') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentCommand')] LIKE '%.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentCommand')] LIKE '%.exe"'))
    AND procName LIKE '%\\cmd.exe'
    AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '/c echo "%'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Forfiles Command Execution

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: execution, T1059 Author: Tim Rauch, Elastic, E.M. Anhaus (originally from Atomic Blue Detections, Endgame), oscd.community Status: test
Detects the execution of "forfiles" with the "/c" flag. While this is an expected behavior of the tool, it can be abused in order to proxy execution through it with any binary. Can be used to bypass application whitelisting.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Forfiles Command Execution
-- Sigma ID:     9aa5106d-bce3-4b13-86df-3a20f1d5cf0b
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
