-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'hashMD5')] AS hashes,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'hashMD5') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'hashMD5')] LIKE '%IMPHASH=136F0A8572C058A96436C82E541E4C41%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'hashMD5')] LIKE '%IMPHASH=589657C64DDE88533186C39F82FA1F50%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'hashMD5')] LIKE '%IMPHASH=6BFE09EFCB4FFDE061EBDBAFC4DB84CF%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'hashMD5')] LIKE '%IMPHASH=7D490037BF450877E6D0287BDCFF8D2E%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'hashMD5')] LIKE '%IMPHASH=8AB93B061287C79F3088C5BC7E7D97ED%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'hashMD5')] LIKE '%IMPHASH=A53A02B997935FD8EEDCB5F7ABAB9B9F%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'hashMD5')] LIKE '%IMPHASH=BA434A7A729EEC20E136CA4C32D6C740%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'hashMD5')] LIKE '%IMPHASH=BD1D1547DA13C0FCB6C15E86217D5EB8%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'hashMD5')] LIKE '%IMPHASH=E96A73C7BF33A464C510EDE582318BF2%'))
  OR (procName LIKE '%\\WCE.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\WCE64.exe')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

HackTool - winPEAS Execution

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: discovery, T1082, T1087, T1046 Author: Georg Lauenstein (sure[secure]) Status: test
WinPEAS is a script that search for possible paths to escalate privileges on Windows hosts. The checks are explained on book.hacktricks.xyz
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        HackTool - winPEAS Execution
-- Sigma ID:     98b53e78-ebaf-46f8-be06-421aafd176d9
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        discovery | T1082, T1087, T1046
-- Author:       Georg Lauenstein (sure[secure])
-- Date:         2022-09-19
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_hktl_winpeas.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unlikely
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentCommand')] AS parentCommandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%https://github.com/carlospolop/PEASS-ng/releases/latest/download/%')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% applicationsinfo%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% browserinfo%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% eventsinfo%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% fileanalysis%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% filesinfo%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% processinfo%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% servicesinfo%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% windowscreds%'))
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentCommand') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentCommand')] LIKE '% -linpeas'))
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% -linpeas'))
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'winPEAS.exe'))
  OR ((procName LIKE '%\\winPEASany\_ofs.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\winPEASany.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\winPEASx64\_ofs.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\winPEASx64.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\winPEASx86\_ofs.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\winPEASx86.exe'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unlikely

HackTool - WinPwn Execution

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: discovery, execution, T1046, T1082, T1106, T1518, T1548.002, T1552.001, T1555, T1555.003 Author: Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel Status: test
Detects commandline keywords indicative of potential usge of the tool WinPwn. A tool for Windows and Active Directory reconnaissance and exploitation.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        HackTool - WinPwn Execution
-- Sigma ID:     d557dc06-62e8-4468-a8e8-7984124908ce
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        discovery, execution | T1046, T1082, T1106, T1518, T1548.002, T1552.001, T1555, T1555.003
-- Author:       Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel
-- Date:         2023-12-04
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_hktl_winpwn.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%Offline\_Winpwn%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%WinPwn %' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%WinPwn.exe%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%WinPwn.ps1%'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

HackTool - Wmiexec Default Powershell Command

high FSM Severity: 7 Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects the execution of PowerShell with a specific flag sequence that is used by the Wmiexec script
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        HackTool - Wmiexec Default Powershell Command
-- Sigma ID:     022eaba8-f0bf-4dd9-9217-4604b0bb3bb0
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        (none)
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2023-03-08
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_hktl_wmiexec_default_powershell.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unlikely
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%-NoP -NoL -sta -NonI -W Hidden -Exec Bypass -Enc%')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unlikely

HackTool - WSASS Execution

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1003.001 Author: Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems) Status: experimental
Detects execution of WSASS, a tool used to dump LSASS memory on Windows systems by leveraging WER's (Windows Error Reporting) WerFaultSecure.EXE to bypass PPL (Protected Process Light) protections.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        HackTool - WSASS Execution
-- Sigma ID:     589ac73f-8e12-409c-964e-31a2f5775ae2
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       experimental
-- MITRE:        T1003.001
-- Author:       Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2025-11-23
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_hktl_wsass.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unlikely
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'hashMD5')] AS hashes,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (match(metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')], '(?i)\.exe[\"\']?\s+.{12,64}[\"\']?\s+\d{2,10}'))
    AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%werfaultsecure%'))
  OR indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'hashMD5') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'hashMD5')] LIKE '%IMPHASH=32F5095C9BBDCACF28FD4060EB4DFC42%')
  OR procName LIKE '%\\wsass.exe'
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unlikely

HackTool - XORDump Execution

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1036, T1003.001 Author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects suspicious use of XORDump process memory dumping utility
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        HackTool - XORDump Execution
-- Sigma ID:     66e563f9-1cbd-4a22-a957-d8b7c0f44372
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1036, T1003.001
-- Author:       Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2022-01-28
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_hktl_xordump.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Another tool that uses the command line switches of XORdump
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\xordump.exe')
  OR ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% -process lsass.exe %' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% -m comsvcs %' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% -m dbghelp %' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% -m dbgcore %')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Another tool that uses the command line switches of XORdump

Suspicious ZipExec Execution

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: execution, T1218, T1202 Author: frack113 Status: test
ZipExec is a Proof-of-Concept (POC) tool to wrap binary-based tools into a password-protected zip file.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Suspicious ZipExec Execution
-- Sigma ID:     90dcf730-1b71-4ae7-9ffc-6fcf62bd0132
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        execution | T1218, T1202
-- Author:       frack113
-- Date:         2021-11-07
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_hktl_zipexec.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%/generic:Microsoft\_Windows\_Shell\_ZipFolder:filename=%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.zip%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%/pass:%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%/user:%')
  OR indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%/delete%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%Microsoft\_Windows\_Shell\_ZipFolder:filename=%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.zip%')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Suspicious Execution of Hostname

low FSM Severity: 3 MITRE: discovery, T1082 Author: frack113 Status: test
Use of hostname to get information
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Suspicious Execution of Hostname
-- Sigma ID:     7be5fb68-f9ef-476d-8b51-0256ebece19e
-- Level:        low  |  FSM Severity: 3
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        discovery | T1082
-- Author:       frack113
-- Date:         2022-01-01
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_hostname_execution.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND procName LIKE '%\\HOSTNAME.EXE'
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Hypervisor-protected Code Integrity (HVCI) Related Registry Tampering Via CommandLine

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1562.001 Author: Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems) Status: experimental
Detects the tampering of Hypervisor-protected Code Integrity (HVCI) related registry values via command line tool reg.exe. HVCI uses virtualization-based security to protect code integrity by ensuring that only trusted code can run in kernel mode. Adversaries may tamper with HVCI to load malicious or unsigned drivers, which can be used to escalate privileges, maintain persistence, or evade security mechanisms.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Hypervisor-protected Code Integrity (HVCI) Related Registry Tampering Via CommandLine
-- Sigma ID:     6225c53a-a96e-4235-b28f-8d7997cd96eb
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       experimental
-- MITRE:        T1562.001
-- Author:       Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2026-01-26
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_hvci_registry_tampering.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate system administration tasks that require disabling HVCI for troubleshooting purposes when certain drivers or applications are incompatible with it.
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%add %' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%New-ItemProperty %' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%Set-ItemProperty %' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%si %'))
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\DeviceGuard%')
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%EnableVirtualizationBasedSecurity%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%HypervisorEnforcedCodeIntegrity%'))
  AND ((procName LIKE '%\\powershell.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\pwsh.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\reg.exe'))
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] IN ('PowerShell.EXE', 'pwsh.dll', 'reg.exe'))))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Legitimate system administration tasks that require disabling HVCI for troubleshooting purposes when certain drivers or applications are incompatible with it.

Suspicious HWP Sub Processes

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: execution, T1566.001, T1203, T1059.003 Author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects suspicious Hangul Word Processor (Hanword) sub processes that could indicate an exploitation
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Suspicious HWP Sub Processes
-- Sigma ID:     023394c4-29d5-46ab-92b8-6a534c6f447b
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        execution | T1566.001, T1203, T1059.003
-- Author:       Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2019-10-24
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_hwp_exploits.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] AS parentImage,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\Hwp.exe')
    AND procName LIKE '%\\gbb.exe')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Potential Fake Instance Of Hxtsr.EXE Executed

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: T1036 Author: Sreeman Status: test
HxTsr.exe is a Microsoft compressed executable file called Microsoft Outlook Communications. HxTsr.exe is part of Outlook apps, because it resides in a hidden "WindowsApps" subfolder of "C:\Program Files". Any instances of hxtsr.exe not in this folder may be malware camouflaging itself as HxTsr.exe
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potential Fake Instance Of Hxtsr.EXE Executed
-- Sigma ID:     4e762605-34a8-406d-b72e-c1a089313320
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1036
-- Author:       Sreeman
-- Date:         2020-04-17
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_hxtsr_masquerading.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND procName LIKE '%\\hxtsr.exe'
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Use Icacls to Hide File to Everyone

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: T1564.001 Author: frack113 Status: test
Detect use of icacls to deny access for everyone in Users folder sometimes used to hide malicious files
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Use Icacls to Hide File to Everyone
-- Sigma ID:     4ae81040-fc1c-4249-bfa3-938d260214d9
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1564.001
-- Author:       frack113
-- Date:         2022-07-18
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_icacls_deny.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%/deny%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%*S-1-1-0:%')
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'iCACLS.EXE'))
  OR (procName LIKE '%\\icacls.exe'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

File Download And Execution Via IEExec.EXE

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1105 Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects execution of the IEExec utility to download and execute files
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        File Download And Execution Via IEExec.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     9801abb8-e297-4dbf-9fbd-57dde0e830ad
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1105
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2022-05-16
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_ieexec_download.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%http://%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%https://%'))
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\IEExec.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'IEExec.exe')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Self Extracting Package Creation Via Iexpress.EXE From Potentially Suspicious Location

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1218 Author: Joseliyo Sanchez, @Joseliyo_Jstnk, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects the use of iexpress.exe to create binaries via Self Extraction Directive (SED) files located in potentially suspicious locations. This behavior has been observed in-the-wild by different threat actors.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Self Extracting Package Creation Via Iexpress.EXE From Potentially Suspicious Location
-- Sigma ID:     b2b048b0-7857-4380-b0fb-d3f0ab820b71
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1218
-- Author:       Joseliyo Sanchez, @Joseliyo_Jstnk, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2024-02-05
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_iexpress_susp_execution.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Administrators building packages using iexpress.exe
-- ============================================================
-- UNSUPPORTED_MODIFIER: contains|windash

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (rawEventMsg LIKE '% /n %'
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\iexpress.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'IEXPRESS.exe'))
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%:\\ProgramData\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%:\\Temp\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%:\\Windows\\System32\\Tasks\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%:\\Windows\\Tasks\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%:\\Windows\\Temp\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\%')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Administrators building packages using iexpress.exe

Disable Windows IIS HTTP Logging

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1562.002 Author: frack113 Status: test
Disables HTTP logging on a Windows IIS web server as seen by Threat Group 3390 (Bronze Union)
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Disable Windows IIS HTTP Logging
-- Sigma ID:     e4ed6030-ffe5-4e6a-8a8a-ab3c1ab9d94e
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1562.002
-- Author:       frack113
-- Date:         2022-01-09
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_iis_appcmd_http_logging.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%set%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%config%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%section:httplogging%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%dontLog:true%')
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\appcmd.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'appcmd.exe')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Microsoft IIS Service Account Password Dumped

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1003 Author: Tim Rauch, Janantha Marasinghe, Elastic (original idea) Status: test
Detects the Internet Information Services (IIS) command-line tool, AppCmd, being used to list passwords
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Microsoft IIS Service Account Password Dumped
-- Sigma ID:     2d3cdeec-c0db-45b4-aa86-082f7eb75701
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1003
-- Author:       Tim Rauch, Janantha Marasinghe, Elastic (original idea)
-- Date:         2022-11-08
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_iis_appcmd_service_account_password_dumped.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% /config%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% /xml%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% -config%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% -xml%'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

IIS Native-Code Module Command Line Installation

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: persistence, T1505.003 Author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects suspicious IIS native-code module installations via command line
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        IIS Native-Code Module Command Line Installation
-- Sigma ID:     9465ddf4-f9e4-4ebd-8d98-702df3a93239
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        persistence | T1505.003
-- Author:       Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2019-12-11
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_iis_appcmd_susp_module_install.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown as it may vary from organisation to organisation how admins use to install IIS modules
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND 1=1
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown as it may vary from organisation to organisation how admins use to install IIS modules

Suspicious IIS URL GlobalRules Rewrite Via AppCmd

medium FSM Severity: 5 Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects usage of "appcmd" to create new global URL rewrite rules. This behaviour has been observed being used by threat actors to add new rules so they can access their webshells.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Suspicious IIS URL GlobalRules Rewrite Via AppCmd
-- Sigma ID:     7c8af9b2-dcae-41a2-a9db-b28c288b5f08
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        (none)
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2023-01-22
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_iis_appcmd_susp_rewrite_rule.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate usage of appcmd to add new URL rewrite rules
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%set%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%config%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%section:system.webServer/rewrite/globalRules%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%commit:%')
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\appcmd.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'appcmd.exe')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Legitimate usage of appcmd to add new URL rewrite rules

Microsoft IIS Connection Strings Decryption

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1003 Author: Tim Rauch, Elastic (idea) Status: test
Detects use of aspnet_regiis to decrypt Microsoft IIS connection strings. An attacker with Microsoft IIS web server access via a webshell or alike can decrypt and dump any hardcoded connection strings, such as the MSSQL service account password using aspnet_regiis command.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Microsoft IIS Connection Strings Decryption
-- Sigma ID:     97dbf6e2-e436-44d8-abee-4261b24d3e41
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1003
-- Author:       Tim Rauch, Elastic (idea)
-- Date:         2022-09-28
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_iis_connection_strings_decryption.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%connectionStrings%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% -pdf%')
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\aspnet\_regiis.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'aspnet_regiis.exe')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

IIS WebServer Log Deletion via CommandLine Utilities

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: T1070 Author: Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems) Status: experimental
Detects attempts to delete Internet Information Services (IIS) log files via command line utilities, which is a common defense evasion technique used by attackers to cover their tracks. Threat actors often abuse vulnerabilities in web applications hosted on IIS servers to gain initial access and later delete IIS logs to evade detection.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        IIS WebServer Log Deletion via CommandLine Utilities
-- Sigma ID:     0649be4a-aeb0-45b0-b89e-7f1668f6d9c0
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       experimental
-- MITRE:        T1070
-- Author:       Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2025-09-02
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_iis_logs_deletion.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Deletion of IIS logs that are older than a certain retention period as part of regular maintenance activities.; Legitimate schedule tasks or scripts that clean up log files regularly.
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%del %' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%erase %' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%rm %' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%remove-item %' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%rmdir %'))
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\inetpub\\logs\\%')
  AND ((procName LIKE '%\\cmd.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\powershell\_ise.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\powershell.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\pwsh.exe'))
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] IN ('cmd.exe', 'powershell.exe', 'powershell_ise.exe', 'pwsh.dll'))))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Deletion of IIS logs that are older than a certain retention period as part of regular maintenance activities.; Legitimate schedule tasks or scripts that clean up log files regularly.

Suspicious IIS Module Registration

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: persistence, T1505.004 Author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Microsoft (idea) Status: test
Detects a suspicious IIS module registration as described in Microsoft threat report on IIS backdoors
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Suspicious IIS Module Registration
-- Sigma ID:     043c4b8b-3a54-4780-9682-081cb6b8185c
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        persistence | T1505.004
-- Author:       Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Microsoft (idea)
-- Date:         2022-08-04
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_iis_susp_module_registration.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Administrative activity
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] AS parentImage,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\w3wp.exe')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Administrative activity

C# IL Code Compilation Via Ilasm.EXE

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: T1127 Author: frack113, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects the use of "Ilasm.EXE" in order to compile C# intermediate (IL) code to EXE or DLL.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        C# IL Code Compilation Via Ilasm.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     850d55f9-6eeb-4492-ad69-a72338f65ba4
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1127
-- Author:       frack113, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2022-05-07
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_ilasm_il_code_compilation.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% /dll%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% /exe%'))
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\ilasm.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'ilasm.exe')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

ImagingDevices Unusual Parent/Child Processes

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: execution Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects unusual parent or children of the ImagingDevices.exe (Windows Contacts) process as seen being used with Bumblebee activity
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        ImagingDevices Unusual Parent/Child Processes
-- Sigma ID:     f11f2808-adb4-46c0-802a-8660db50fa99
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        execution
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2022-09-27
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_imagingdevices_unusual_parents.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] AS parentImage,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\ImagingDevices.exe')
  OR ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\WmiPrvSE.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\svchost.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\dllhost.exe'))
    AND procName LIKE '%\\ImagingDevices.exe')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Arbitrary File Download Via IMEWDBLD.EXE

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: execution, T1218 Author: Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel Status: test
Detects usage of "IMEWDBLD.exe" to download arbitrary files
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Arbitrary File Download Via IMEWDBLD.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     863218bd-c7d0-4c52-80cd-0a96c09f54af
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        execution | T1218
-- Author:       Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel
-- Date:         2023-11-09
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_imewbdld_download.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%http://%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%https://%'))
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\IMEWDBLD.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'imewdbld.exe')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

InfDefaultInstall.exe .inf Execution

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: T1218 Author: frack113 Status: test
Executes SCT script using scrobj.dll from a command in entered into a specially prepared INF file.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        InfDefaultInstall.exe .inf Execution
-- Sigma ID:     ce7cf472-6fcc-490a-9481-3786840b5d9b
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1218
-- Author:       frack113
-- Date:         2021-07-13
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_infdefaultinstall_execute_sct_scripts.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%InfDefaultInstall.exe %' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.inf%')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

File Download Via InstallUtil.EXE

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: T1218 Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects use of .NET InstallUtil.exe in order to download arbitrary files. The files will be written to "%LOCALAPPDATA%\Microsoft\Windows\INetCache\IE\"
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        File Download Via InstallUtil.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     75edd216-1939-4c73-8d61-7f3a0d85b5cc
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1218
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2022-08-19
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_installutil_download.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%ftp://%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%http://%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%https://%'))
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\InstallUtil.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'InstallUtil.exe')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Suspicious Execution of InstallUtil Without Log

medium FSM Severity: 5 Author: frack113 Status: test
Uses the .NET InstallUtil.exe application in order to execute image without log
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Suspicious Execution of InstallUtil Without Log
-- Sigma ID:     d042284c-a296-4988-9be5-f424fadcc28c
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        (none)
-- Author:       frack113
-- Date:         2022-01-23
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_instalutil_no_log_execution.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\InstallUtil.exe'
    AND procName LIKE '%Microsoft.NET\\Framework%'
    AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%/logfile= %' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%/LogToConsole=false%'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Suspicious Shells Spawn by Java Utility Keytool

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: persistence Author: Andreas Hunkeler (@Karneades) Status: test
Detects suspicious shell spawn from Java utility keytool process (e.g. adselfservice plus exploitation)
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Suspicious Shells Spawn by Java Utility Keytool
-- Sigma ID:     90fb5e62-ca1f-4e22-b42e-cc521874c938
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        persistence
-- Author:       Andreas Hunkeler (@Karneades)
-- Date:         2021-12-22
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_java_keytool_susp_child_process.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] AS parentImage,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\keytool.exe')
    AND (procName LIKE '%\\cmd.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\sh.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\bash.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\powershell.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\pwsh.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\schtasks.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\certutil.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\whoami.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\bitsadmin.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\wscript.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\cscript.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\scrcons.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\regsvr32.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\hh.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\wmic.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\mshta.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\rundll32.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\forfiles.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\scriptrunner.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\mftrace.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\AppVLP.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\systeminfo.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\reg.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\query.exe'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Suspicious Child Process Of Manage Engine ServiceDesk

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1102 Author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects suspicious child processes of the "Manage Engine ServiceDesk Plus" Java web service
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Suspicious Child Process Of Manage Engine ServiceDesk
-- Sigma ID:     cea2b7ea-792b-405f-95a1-b903ea06458f
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1102
-- Author:       Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2023-01-18
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_java_manageengine_susp_child_process.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate sub processes started by Manage Engine ServiceDesk Pro
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] AS parentImage,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\ManageEngine\\ServiceDesk\\%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\java.exe%')
    AND (procName LIKE '%\\AppVLP.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\bash.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\bitsadmin.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\calc.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\certutil.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\cscript.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\curl.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\forfiles.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\mftrace.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\mshta.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\net.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\net1.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\notepad.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\powershell.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\pwsh.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\query.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\reg.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\schtasks.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\scrcons.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\sh.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\systeminfo.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\whoami.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\wmic.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\wscript.exe'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Legitimate sub processes started by Manage Engine ServiceDesk Pro

Java Running with Remote Debugging

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: execution, T1203 Author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects a JAVA process running with remote debugging allowing more than just localhost to connect
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Java Running with Remote Debugging
-- Sigma ID:     8f88e3f6-2a49-48f5-a5c4-2f7eedf78710
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        execution | T1203
-- Author:       Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2019-01-16
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_java_remote_debugging.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND NOT ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%address=127.0.0.1%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%address=localhost%')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Suspicious Processes Spawned by Java.EXE

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: persistence Author: Andreas Hunkeler (@Karneades), Florian Roth Status: test
Detects suspicious processes spawned from a Java host process which could indicate a sign of exploitation (e.g. log4j)
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Suspicious Processes Spawned by Java.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     0d34ed8b-1c12-4ff2-828c-16fc860b766d
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        persistence
-- Author:       Andreas Hunkeler (@Karneades), Florian Roth
-- Date:         2021-12-17
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_java_susp_child_process.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate calls to system binaries; Company specific internal usage
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] AS parentImage,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\java.exe')
    AND (procName LIKE '%\\AppVLP.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\bitsadmin.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\certutil.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\cscript.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\curl.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\forfiles.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\hh.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\mftrace.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\mshta.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\net.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\net1.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\query.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\reg.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\regsvr32.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\rundll32.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\schtasks.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\scrcons.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\scriptrunner.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\sh.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\systeminfo.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\whoami.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\wmic.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\wscript.exe'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Legitimate calls to system binaries; Company specific internal usage

Suspicious SysAidServer Child

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: T1210 Author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects suspicious child processes of SysAidServer (as seen in MERCURY threat actor intrusions)
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Suspicious SysAidServer Child
-- Sigma ID:     60bfeac3-0d35-4302-8efb-1dd16f715bc6
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1210
-- Author:       Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2022-08-26
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_java_sysaidserver_susp_child_process.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] AS parentImage,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentCommand')] AS parentCommandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\java.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\javaw.exe'))
    AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentCommand') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentCommand')] LIKE '%SysAidServer%'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

JScript Compiler Execution

low FSM Severity: 3 MITRE: T1127 Author: frack113 Status: test
Detects the execution of the "jsc.exe" (JScript Compiler). Attacker might abuse this in order to compile JScript files on the fly and bypassing application whitelisting.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        JScript Compiler Execution
-- Sigma ID:     52788a70-f1da-40dd-8fbd-73b5865d6568
-- Level:        low  |  FSM Severity: 3
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1127
-- Author:       frack113
-- Date:         2022-05-02
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_jsc_execution.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate use to compile JScript by developers.
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\jsc.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'jsc.exe'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Legitimate use to compile JScript by developers.

Kavremover Dropped Binary LOLBIN Usage
