SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% run run-cmd %')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Windows Kernel Debugger Execution

medium FSM Severity: 5 Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects execution of the Windows Kernel Debugger "kd.exe".
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Windows Kernel Debugger Execution
-- Sigma ID:     27ee9438-90dc-4bef-904b-d3ef927f5e7e
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        (none)
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2023-05-15
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_kd_execution.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Rare occasions of legitimate cases where kernel debugging is necessary in production. Investigation is required
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\kd.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'kd.exe'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Rare occasions of legitimate cases where kernel debugging is necessary in production. Investigation is required

Attempts of Kerberos Coercion Via DNS SPN Spoofing

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: collection, persistence, T1557.001, T1187 Author: Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems) Status: experimental
Detects the presence of "UWhRC....AAYBAAAA" pattern in command line. The pattern "1UWhRCAAAAA..BAAAA" is a base64-encoded signature that corresponds to a marshaled CREDENTIAL_TARGET_INFORMATION structure. Attackers can use this technique to coerce authentication from victim systems to attacker-controlled hosts. It is one of the strong indicators of a Kerberos coercion attack, where adversaries manipulate DNS records to spoof Service Principal Names (SPNs) and redirect authentication requests like in CVE-2025-33073. If you see this pattern in the command line, it is likely an attempt to add spoofed Service Principal Names (SPNs) to DNS records, or checking for the presence of such records through the `nslookup` command.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Attempts of Kerberos Coercion Via DNS SPN Spoofing
-- Sigma ID:     0ed99dda-6a35-11ef-8c99-0242ac120002
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       experimental
-- MITRE:        collection, persistence | T1557.001, T1187
-- Author:       Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2025-06-20
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_kerberos_coercion_via_dns_spn_spoofing.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%UWhRCA%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%BAAAA%')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Potentially Suspicious Child Process of KeyScrambler.exe

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: persistence, execution, T1203, T1574.001 Author: Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel Status: test
Detects potentially suspicious child processes of KeyScrambler.exe
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potentially Suspicious Child Process of KeyScrambler.exe
-- Sigma ID:     ca5583e9-8f80-46ac-ab91-7f314d13b984
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        persistence, execution | T1203, T1574.001
-- Author:       Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel
-- Date:         2024-05-13
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_keyscrambler_susp_child_process.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] AS parentImage,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (((procName LIKE '%\\cmd.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\cscript.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\mshta.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\powershell.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\pwsh.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\regsvr32.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\rundll32.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\wscript.exe'))
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] IN ('Cmd.Exe', 'cscript.exe', 'mshta.exe', 'PowerShell.EXE', 'pwsh.dll', 'regsvr32.exe', 'RUNDLL32.EXE', 'wscript.exe')))
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\KeyScrambler.exe'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Computer Password Change Via Ksetup.EXE

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: execution Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects password change for the computer's domain account or host principal via "ksetup.exe"
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Computer Password Change Via Ksetup.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     de16d92c-c446-4d53-8938-10aeef41c8b6
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        execution
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2023-04-06
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_ksetup_password_change_computer.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% /setcomputerpassword %')
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\ksetup.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'ksetup.exe')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Logged-On User Password Change Via Ksetup.EXE

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: execution Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects password change for the logged-on user's via "ksetup.exe"
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Logged-On User Password Change Via Ksetup.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     c9783e20-4793-4164-ba96-d9ee483992c4
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        execution
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2023-04-06
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_ksetup_password_change_user.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% /ChangePassword %')
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\ksetup.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'ksetup.exe')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Active Directory Structure Export Via Ldifde.EXE

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: exfiltration Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects the execution of "ldifde.exe" in order to export organizational Active Directory structure.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Active Directory Structure Export Via Ldifde.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     4f7a6757-ff79-46db-9687-66501a02d9ec
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        exfiltration
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2023-03-14
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_ldifde_export.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND 1=1
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Import LDAP Data Interchange Format File Via Ldifde.EXE

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: T1218, T1105 Author: @gott_cyber Status: test
Detects the execution of "Ldifde.exe" with the import flag "-i". The can be abused to include HTTP-based arguments which will allow the arbitrary download of files from a remote server.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Import LDAP Data Interchange Format File Via Ldifde.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     6f535e01-ca1f-40be-ab8d-45b19c0c8b7f
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1218, T1105
-- Author:       @gott_cyber
-- Date:         2022-09-02
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_ldifde_file_load.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Since the content of the files are unknown, false positives are expected
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%-i%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%-f%')
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\ldifde.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'ldifde.exe')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Since the content of the files are unknown, false positives are expected

Uncommon Link.EXE Parent Process

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: T1218 Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects an uncommon parent process of "LINK.EXE". Link.EXE in Microsoft incremental linker. Its a utility usually bundled with Visual Studio installation. Multiple utilities often found in the same folder (editbin.exe, dumpbin.exe, lib.exe, etc) have a hardcode call to the "LINK.EXE" binary without checking its validity. This would allow an attacker to sideload any binary with the name "link.exe" if one of the aforementioned tools get executed from a different location. By filtering the known locations of such utilities we can spot uncommon parent process of LINK.EXE that might be suspicious or malicious.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Uncommon Link.EXE Parent Process
-- Sigma ID:     6e968eb1-5f05-4dac-94e9-fd0c5cb49fd6
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1218
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2022-08-22
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_link_uncommon_parent_process.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\link.exe'
    AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%LINK /%'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Rebuild Performance Counter Values Via Lodctr.EXE

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: execution Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects the execution of "lodctr.exe" to rebuild the performance counter registry values. This can be abused by attackers by providing a malicious config file to overwrite performance counter configuration to confuse and evade monitoring and security solutions.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Rebuild Performance Counter Values Via Lodctr.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     cc9d3712-6310-4320-b2df-7cb408274d53
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        execution
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2023-06-15
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_lodctr_performance_counter_tampering.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate usage by an administrator
-- ============================================================
-- UNSUPPORTED_MODIFIER: contains|windash

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (rawEventMsg LIKE '% -r%'
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\lodctr.exe'
    AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'LODCTR.EXE')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Legitimate usage by an administrator

Suspicious Windows Trace ETW Session Tamper Via Logman.EXE

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1562.001, T1070.001 Author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects the execution of "logman" utility in order to disable or delete Windows trace sessions
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Suspicious Windows Trace ETW Session Tamper Via Logman.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     cd1f961e-0b96-436b-b7c6-38da4583ec00
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1562.001, T1070.001
-- Author:       Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2021-02-11
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_logman_disable_eventlog.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate deactivation by administrative staff; Installer tools that disable services, e.g. before log collection agent installation
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%stop %' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%delete %'))
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\logman.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'Logman.exe'))
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%Circular Kernel Context Logger%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%EventLog-%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%SYSMON TRACE%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%SysmonDnsEtwSession%')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Legitimate deactivation by administrative staff; Installer tools that disable services, e.g. before log collection agent installation

LOLBAS Data Exfiltration by DataSvcUtil.exe

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: exfiltration, T1567 Author: Ialle Teixeira @teixeira0xfffff, Austin Songer @austinsonger Status: test
Detects when a user performs data exfiltration by using DataSvcUtil.exe
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        LOLBAS Data Exfiltration by DataSvcUtil.exe
-- Sigma ID:     e290b10b-1023-4452-a4a9-eb31a9013b3a
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        exfiltration | T1567
-- Author:       Ialle Teixeira @teixeira0xfffff, Austin Songer @austinsonger
-- Date:         2021-09-30
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_lolbin_data_exfiltration_by_using_datasvcutil.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    DataSvcUtil.exe being used may be performed by a system administrator.; Verify whether the user identity, user agent, and/or hostname should be making changes in your environment.; DataSvcUtil.exe being executed from unfamiliar users should be investigated. If known behavior is causing false positives, it can be exempted from the rule.
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%/in:%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%/out:%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%/uri:%'))
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\DataSvcUtil.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'DataSvcUtil.exe')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: DataSvcUtil.exe being used may be performed by a system administrator.; Verify whether the user identity, user agent, and/or hostname should be making changes in your environment.; DataSvcUtil.exe being executed from unfamiliar users should be investigated. If known behavior is causing false positives, it can be exempted from the rule.

Devtoolslauncher.exe Executes Specified Binary

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1218 Author: Beyu Denis, oscd.community (rule), @_felamos (idea) Status: test
The Devtoolslauncher.exe executes other binary
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Devtoolslauncher.exe Executes Specified Binary
-- Sigma ID:     cc268ac1-42d9-40fd-9ed3-8c4e1a5b87e6
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1218
-- Author:       Beyu Denis, oscd.community (rule), @_felamos (idea)
-- Date:         2019-10-12
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_lolbin_devtoolslauncher.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate use of devtoolslauncher.exe by legitimate user
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\devtoolslauncher.exe'
    AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%LaunchForDeploy%'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Legitimate use of devtoolslauncher.exe by legitimate user

Suspicious Diantz Alternate Data Stream Execution

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: T1564.004 Author: frack113 Status: test
Compress target file into a cab file stored in the Alternate Data Stream (ADS) of the target file.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Suspicious Diantz Alternate Data Stream Execution
-- Sigma ID:     6b369ced-4b1d-48f1-b427-fdc0de0790bd
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1564.004
-- Author:       frack113
-- Date:         2021-11-26
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_lolbin_diantz_ads.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Very Possible
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%diantz.exe%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.cab%')
    AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (match(metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')], ':[^\\]')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Very Possible

Suspicious Diantz Download and Compress Into a CAB File

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: T1105 Author: frack113 Status: test
Download and compress a remote file and store it in a cab file on local machine.

-- Sigma ID:     185d7418-f250-42d0-b72e-0c8b70661e93

-- Status:       test

  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%diantz.exe%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% \\\\\\\\%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.cab%')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Suspicious Extrac32 Execution

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: T1105 Author: frack113 Status: test
Download or Copy file with Extrac32
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Suspicious Extrac32 Execution
-- Sigma ID:     aa8e035d-7be4-48d3-a944-102aec04400d
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1105
-- Author:       frack113
-- Date:         2021-11-26
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_lolbin_extrac32.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.cab%')
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%extrac32.exe%'))
  OR (procName LIKE '%\\extrac32.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'extrac32.exe'))
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%/C%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%/Y%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% \\\\\\\\%')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown
