Potential Register_App.Vbs LOLScript Abuse

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: T1218 Author: Austin Songer @austinsonger Status: test
Detects potential abuse of the "register_app.vbs" script that is part of the Windows SDK. The script offers the capability to register new VSS/VDS Provider as a COM+ application. Attackers can use this to install malicious DLLs for persistence and execution.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potential Register_App.Vbs LOLScript Abuse
-- Sigma ID:     28c8f68b-098d-45af-8d43-8089f3e35403
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1218
-- Author:       Austin Songer @austinsonger
-- Date:         2021-11-05
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_lolscript_register_app.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Other VB scripts that leverage the same starting command line flags
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.vbs -register %')
  AND ((procName LIKE '%\\cscript.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\wscript.exe'))
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] IN ('cscript.exe', 'wscript.exe'))))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Other VB scripts that leverage the same starting command line flags

Potential Credential Dumping Via LSASS Process Clone

critical FSM Severity: 9 MITRE: T1003, T1003.001 Author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Samir Bousseaden Status: test
Detects a suspicious LSASS process process clone that could be a sign of credential dumping activity
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potential Credential Dumping Via LSASS Process Clone
-- Sigma ID:     c8da0dfd-4ed0-4b68-962d-13c9c884384e
-- Level:        critical  |  FSM Severity: 9
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1003, T1003.001
-- Author:       Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Samir Bousseaden
-- Date:         2021-11-27
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_lsass_process_clone.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] AS parentImage,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\Windows\\System32\\lsass.exe')
    AND procName LIKE '%\\Windows\\System32\\lsass.exe')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Potential Mftrace.EXE Abuse

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: T1127 Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects child processes of the "Trace log generation tool for Media Foundation Tools" (Mftrace.exe) which can abused to execute arbitrary binaries.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potential Mftrace.EXE Abuse
-- Sigma ID:     3d48c9d3-1aa6-418d-98d3-8fd3c01a564e
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1127
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2022-06-09
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_mftrace_child_process.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate use for tracing purposes
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] AS parentImage,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\mftrace.exe')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Legitimate use for tracing purposes

Windows Default Domain GPO Modification via GPME

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: T1484.001 Author: TropChaud Status: experimental
Detects the use of the Group Policy Management Editor (GPME) to modify Default Domain or Default Domain Controllers Group Policy Objects (GPOs). Adversaries may leverage GPME to make stealthy changes in these default GPOs to deploy malicious GPOs configurations across the domain without raising suspicion.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Windows Default Domain GPO Modification via GPME
-- Sigma ID:     dcff7e85-d01f-4eb5-badd-84e2e6be8294
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       experimental
-- MITRE:        T1484.001
-- Author:       TropChaud
-- Date:         2025-11-22
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_mmc_default_domain_gpo_modification_via_gpme.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate use of GPME to modify GPOs
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%31B2F340-016D-11D2-945F-00C04FB984F9%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%6AC1786C-016F-11D2-945F-00C04FB984F9%'))
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%gpme.msc%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%gpobject:%')
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\mmc.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'MMC.exe')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Legitimate use of GPME to modify GPOs

MMC20 Lateral Movement

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: execution, T1021.003 Author: @2xxeformyshirt (Security Risk Advisors) - rule; Teymur Kheirkhabarov (idea) Status: test
Detects MMC20.Application Lateral Movement; specifically looks for the spawning of the parent MMC.exe with a command line of "-Embedding" as a child of svchost.exe
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        MMC20 Lateral Movement
-- Sigma ID:     f1f3bf22-deb2-418d-8cce-e1a45e46a5bd
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        execution | T1021.003
-- Author:       @2xxeformyshirt (Security Risk Advisors) - rule; Teymur Kheirkhabarov (idea)
-- Date:         2020-03-04
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_mmc_mmc20_lateral_movement.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unlikely
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] AS parentImage,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\svchost.exe')
    AND procName LIKE '%\\mmc.exe'
    AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%-Embedding%'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unlikely

MMC Executing Files with Reversed Extensions Using RTLO Abuse

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: execution, T1204.002, T1218.014, T1036.002 Author: Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems) Status: experimental
Detects malicious behavior where the MMC utility (`mmc.exe`) executes files with reversed extensions caused by Right-to-Left Override (RLO) abuse, disguising them as document formats.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        MMC Executing Files with Reversed Extensions Using RTLO Abuse
-- Sigma ID:     9cfe4b27-1e56-48b4-b7a8-d46851c91a44
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       experimental
-- MITRE:        execution | T1204.002, T1218.014, T1036.002
-- Author:       Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2025-02-05
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_mmc_rlo_abuse_pattern.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate administrative actions using MMC to execute misnamed `.msc` files.; Unconventional but non-malicious usage of RLO or reversed extensions.
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%cod.msc%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%fdp.msc%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%ftr.msc%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%lmth.msc%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%slx.msc%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%tdo.msc%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%xcod.msc%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%xslx.msc%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%xtpp.msc%'))
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\mmc.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'MMC.exe')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Legitimate administrative actions using MMC to execute misnamed `.msc` files.; Unconventional but non-malicious usage of RLO or reversed extensions.

MMC Spawning Windows Shell

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1021.003 Author: Karneades, Swisscom CSIRT Status: test
Detects a Windows command line executable started from MMC
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        MMC Spawning Windows Shell
-- Sigma ID:     05a2ab7e-ce11-4b63-86db-ab32e763e11d
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1021.003
-- Author:       Karneades, Swisscom CSIRT
-- Date:         2019-08-05
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_mmc_susp_child_process.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    (none)
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] AS parentImage,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\mmc.exe')
  AND ((procName LIKE '%\\cmd.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\powershell.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\pwsh.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\wscript.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\cscript.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\sh.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\bash.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\reg.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\regsvr32.exe'))
  OR (procName LIKE '%\\BITSADMIN%'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;

CodePage Modification Via MODE.COM To Russian Language

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: T1036 Author: Joseliyo Sanchez, @Joseliyo_Jstnk Status: test
Detects a CodePage modification using the "mode.com" utility to Russian language. This behavior has been used by threat actors behind Dharma ransomware.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        CodePage Modification Via MODE.COM To Russian Language
-- Sigma ID:     12fbff88-16b5-4b42-9754-cd001a789fb3
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1036
-- Author:       Joseliyo Sanchez, @Joseliyo_Jstnk
-- Date:         2024-01-17
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_mode_codepage_russian.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Russian speaking people changing the CodePage
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% con %' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% cp %' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% select=%')
    AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%=1251' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%=866')))
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\mode.com')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'MODE.COM')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Russian speaking people changing the CodePage

Potential Suspicious Mofcomp Execution

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1218 Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects execution of the "mofcomp" utility as a child of a suspicious shell or script running utility or by having a suspicious path in the commandline. The "mofcomp" utility parses a file containing MOF statements and adds the classes and class instances defined in the file to the WMI repository. Attackers abuse this utility to install malicious MOF scripts
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potential Suspicious Mofcomp Execution
-- Sigma ID:     1dd05363-104e-4b4a-b963-196a534b03a1
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1218
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2022-07-12
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_mofcomp_execution.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND 1=1
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Potential Mpclient.DLL Sideloading Via Defender Binaries

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: persistence, T1574.001 Author: Bhabesh Raj Status: test
Detects potential sideloading of "mpclient.dll" by Windows Defender processes ("MpCmdRun" and "NisSrv") from their non-default directory.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potential Mpclient.DLL Sideloading Via Defender Binaries
-- Sigma ID:     7002aa10-b8d4-47ae-b5ba-51ab07e228b9
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        persistence | T1574.001
-- Author:       Bhabesh Raj
-- Date:         2022-08-01
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_mpcmdrun_dll_sideload_defender.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unlikely
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\MpCmdRun.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\NisSrv.exe')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unlikely

File Download Via Windows Defender MpCmpRun.EXE

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1218, T1105 Author: Matthew Matchen Status: test
Detects the use of Windows Defender MpCmdRun.EXE to download files
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        File Download Via Windows Defender MpCmpRun.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     46123129-1024-423e-9fae-43af4a0fa9a5
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1218, T1105
-- Author:       Matthew Matchen
-- Date:         2020-09-04
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_mpcmdrun_download_arbitrary_file.yml
-- Unmapped:     Description
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================
-- UNMAPPED_FIELD: Description

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%DownloadFile%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%url%')
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'MpCmdRun.exe'))
  OR (procName LIKE '%\\MpCmdRun.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%MpCmdRun.exe%'))
  OR (rawEventMsg = 'Microsoft Malware Protection Command Line Utility'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Windows Defender Definition Files Removed

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1562.001 Author: frack113 Status: test
Adversaries may disable security tools to avoid possible detection of their tools and activities by removing Windows Defender Definition Files
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Windows Defender Definition Files Removed
-- Sigma ID:     9719a8aa-401c-41af-8108-ced7ec9cd75c
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1562.001
-- Author:       frack113
-- Date:         2021-07-07
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_mpcmdrun_remove_windows_defender_definition.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% -RemoveDefinitions%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% -All%')
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\MpCmdRun.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'MpCmdRun.exe')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Suspicious Msbuild Execution By Uncommon Parent Process

medium FSM Severity: 5 Author: frack113 Status: test
Detects suspicious execution of 'Msbuild.exe' by a uncommon parent process
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Suspicious Msbuild Execution By Uncommon Parent Process
-- Sigma ID:     33be4333-2c6b-44f4-ae28-102cdbde0a31
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        (none)
-- Author:       frack113
-- Date:         2022-11-17
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_msbuild_susp_parent_process.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] AS parentImage,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((procName LIKE '%\\MSBuild.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'MSBuild.exe'))
  AND NOT ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\devenv.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\cmd.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\msbuild.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\python.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\explorer.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\nuget.exe'))))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

MSDT Execution Via Answer File

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: execution, T1218 Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects execution of "msdt.exe" using an answer file which is simulating the legitimate way of calling msdt via "pcwrun.exe" (For example from the compatibility tab).
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        MSDT Execution Via Answer File
-- Sigma ID:     9c8c7000-3065-44a8-a555-79bcba5d9955
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        execution | T1218
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2022-06-13
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_msdt_answer_file_exec.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Possible undocumented parents of "msdt" other than "pcwrun".
-- ============================================================
-- UNSUPPORTED_MODIFIER: contains|windash

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\msdt.exe'
    AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\WINDOWS\\diagnostics\\index\\PCWDiagnostic.xml%')
    AND rawEventMsg LIKE '% -af %')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Possible undocumented parents of "msdt" other than "pcwrun".

Potential Arbitrary Command Execution Using Msdt.EXE

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1202 Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects processes leveraging the "ms-msdt" handler or the "msdt.exe" binary to execute arbitrary commands as seen in the follina (CVE-2022-30190) vulnerability
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potential Arbitrary Command Execution Using Msdt.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     258fc8ce-8352-443a-9120-8a11e4857fa5
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1202
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2022-05-29
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_msdt_arbitrary_command_execution.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((procName LIKE '%\\msdt.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'msdt.exe'))
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%IT\_BrowseForFile=%'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Suspicious Cabinet File Execution Via Msdt.EXE

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: T1202 Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), GossiTheDog, frack113 Status: test
Detects execution of msdt.exe using the "cab" flag which could indicates suspicious diagcab files with embedded answer files leveraging CVE-2022-30190
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Suspicious Cabinet File Execution Via Msdt.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     dc4576d4-7467-424f-9eee-fd2b02855fe0
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1202
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), GossiTheDog, frack113
-- Date:         2022-06-21
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_msdt_susp_cab_options.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate usage of ".diagcab" files
-- ============================================================
-- UNSUPPORTED_MODIFIER: contains|windash

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (rawEventMsg LIKE '% -cab %'
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\msdt.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'msdt.exe')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Legitimate usage of ".diagcab" files

Suspicious MSDT Parent Process

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1036, T1218 Author: Nextron Systems Status: test
Detects msdt.exe executed by a suspicious parent as seen in CVE-2022-30190 / Follina exploitation
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Suspicious MSDT Parent Process
-- Sigma ID:     7a74da6b-ea76-47db-92cc-874ad90df734
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1036, T1218
-- Author:       Nextron Systems
-- Date:         2022-06-01
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_msdt_susp_parent.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] AS parentImage,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((procName LIKE '%\\msdt.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'msdt.exe'))
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\cmd.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\cscript.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\mshta.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\powershell.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\pwsh.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\regsvr32.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\rundll32.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\schtasks.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\wmic.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\wscript.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\wsl.exe')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Arbitrary File Download Via MSEDGE_PROXY.EXE

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: execution, T1218 Author: Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel Status: test
Detects usage of "msedge_proxy.exe" to download arbitrary files
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Arbitrary File Download Via MSEDGE_PROXY.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     e84d89c4-f544-41ca-a6af-4b92fd38b023
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        execution | T1218
-- Author:       Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel
-- Date:         2023-11-09
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_msedge_proxy_download.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%http://%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%https://%'))
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\msedge\_proxy.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'msedge_proxy.exe')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Remotely Hosted HTA File Executed Via Mshta.EXE

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: execution, T1218.005 Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects execution of the "mshta" utility with an argument containing the "http" keyword, which could indicate that an attacker is executing a remotely hosted malicious hta file
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Remotely Hosted HTA File Executed Via Mshta.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     b98d0db6-511d-45de-ad02-e82a98729620
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        execution | T1218.005
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2022-08-08
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_mshta_http.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%http://%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%https://%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%ftp://%'))
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\mshta.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'MSHTA.EXE')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Wscript Shell Run In CommandLine

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: execution, T1059 Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects the presence of the keywords "Wscript", "Shell" and "Run" in the command, which could indicate a suspicious activity
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Wscript Shell Run In CommandLine
-- Sigma ID:     2c28c248-7f50-417a-9186-a85b223010ee
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        execution | T1059
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2022-08-31
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_mshta_inline_vbscript.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Inline scripting can be used by some rare third party applications or administrators. Investigate and apply additional filters accordingly
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%Wscript.%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.Shell%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.Run%')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Inline scripting can be used by some rare third party applications or administrators. Investigate and apply additional filters accordingly

Suspicious JavaScript Execution Via Mshta.EXE

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1218.005 Author: E.M. Anhaus (originally from Atomic Blue Detections, Endgame), oscd.community Status: test
Detects execution of javascript code using "mshta.exe".
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Suspicious JavaScript Execution Via Mshta.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     67f113fa-e23d-4271-befa-30113b3e08b1
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1218.005
-- Author:       E.M. Anhaus (originally from Atomic Blue Detections, Endgame), oscd.community
-- Date:         2019-10-24
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_mshta_javascript.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%javascript%')
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\mshta.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'MSHTA.EXE')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Potential LethalHTA Technique Execution

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1218.005 Author: Markus Neis Status: test
Detects potential LethalHTA technique where the "mshta.exe" is spawned by an "svchost.exe" process
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potential LethalHTA Technique Execution
-- Sigma ID:     ed5d72a6-f8f4-479d-ba79-02f6a80d7471
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1218.005
-- Author:       Markus Neis
-- Date:         2018-06-07
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_mshta_lethalhta_technique.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] AS parentImage,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\svchost.exe')
    AND procName LIKE '%\\mshta.exe')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Suspicious MSHTA Child Process

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1218.005 Author: Michael Haag Status: test
Detects a suspicious process spawning from an "mshta.exe" process, which could be indicative of a malicious HTA script execution
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Suspicious MSHTA Child Process
-- Sigma ID:     03cc0c25-389f-4bf8-b48d-11878079f1ca
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1218.005
-- Author:       Michael Haag
-- Date:         2019-01-16
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_mshta_susp_child_processes.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Printer software / driver installations; HP software
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] AS parentImage,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (((procName LIKE '%\\cmd.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\powershell.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\pwsh.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\wscript.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\cscript.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\sh.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\bash.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\reg.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\regsvr32.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\bitsadmin.exe'))
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] IN ('Cmd.Exe', 'PowerShell.EXE', 'pwsh.dll', 'wscript.exe', 'cscript.exe', 'Bash.exe', 'reg.exe', 'REGSVR32.EXE', 'bitsadmin.exe')))
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\mshta.exe'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Printer software / driver installations; HP software

MSHTA Execution with Suspicious File Extensions

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: execution, T1140, T1218.005, T1059.007 Author: Diego Perez (@darkquassar), Markus Neis, Swisscom (Improve Rule), Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects execution of mshta.exe with file types that looks like they do not typically represent HTA (HTML Application) content, such as .png, .jpg, .zip, .pdf, and others, which are often polyglots. MSHTA is a legitimate Windows utility for executing HTML Applications containing VBScript or JScript. Threat actors often abuse this lolbin utility to download and execute malicious scripts disguised as benign files or hosted under misleading extensions to evade detection.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        MSHTA Execution with Suspicious File Extensions
-- Sigma ID:     cc7abbd0-762b-41e3-8a26-57ad50d2eea3
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        execution | T1140, T1218.005, T1059.007
-- Author:       Diego Perez (@darkquassar), Markus Neis, Swisscom (Improve Rule), Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2019-02-22
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_mshta_susp_execution.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    False positives depend on scripts and administrative tools used in the monitored environment
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.7z%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.avi%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.bat%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.bmp%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.conf%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.csv%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.dll%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.doc%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.gif%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.gz%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.ini%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.jpe%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.jpg%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.json%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.lnk%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.log%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.mkv%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.mp3%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.mp4%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.pdf%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.png%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.ppt%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.rar%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.rtf%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.svg%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.tar%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.tmp%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.txt%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.xls%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.xml%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.yaml%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.yml%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.zip%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%vbscript%'))
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\mshta.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'mshta.exe')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: False positives depend on scripts and administrative tools used in the monitored environment

Suspicious Mshta.EXE Execution Patterns

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: execution, T1106 Author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects suspicious mshta process execution patterns
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Suspicious Mshta.EXE Execution Patterns
-- Sigma ID:     e32f92d1-523e-49c3-9374-bdb13b46a3ba
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        execution | T1106
-- Author:       Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2021-07-17
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_mshta_susp_pattern.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] AS parentImage,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((procName LIKE '%\\mshta.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'MSHTA.EXE'))
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\cmd.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\cscript.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\powershell.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\pwsh.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\regsvr32.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\rundll32.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\wscript.exe'))
    AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\AppData\\Local\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%C:\\ProgramData\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%C:\\Users\\Public\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%C:\\Windows\\Temp\\%'))))
  OR ((procName LIKE '%\\mshta.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'MSHTA.EXE'))
  AND NOT (((procName LIKE 'C:\\Windows\\System32\\%' OR procName LIKE 'C:\\Windows\\SysWOW64\\%'))
  OR ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.htm%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.hta%')))
  OR ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%mshta.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%mshta')))))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

DllUnregisterServer Function Call Via Msiexec.EXE

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: T1218.007 Author: frack113 Status: test
Detects MsiExec loading a DLL and calling its DllUnregisterServer function
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        DllUnregisterServer Function Call Via Msiexec.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     84f52741-8834-4a8c-a413-2eb2269aa6c8
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1218.007
-- Author:       frack113
-- Date:         2022-04-24
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_msiexec_dll.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================
-- UNSUPPORTED_MODIFIER: contains|windash

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.dll%')
  AND rawEventMsg LIKE '% -z %'
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\msiexec.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = '\msiexec.exe')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Suspicious MsiExec Embedding Parent

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: T1218.007 Author: frack113 Status: test
Adversaries may abuse msiexec.exe to proxy the execution of malicious payloads
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Suspicious MsiExec Embedding Parent
-- Sigma ID:     4a2a2c3e-209f-4d01-b513-4155a540b469
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1218.007
-- Author:       frack113
-- Date:         2022-04-16
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_msiexec_embedding.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentCommand')] AS parentCommandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((procName LIKE '%\\powershell.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\pwsh.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\cmd.exe')
    AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentCommand') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentCommand')] LIKE '%MsiExec.exe%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentCommand')] LIKE '%-Embedding %'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Suspicious Msiexec Execute Arbitrary DLL

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: T1218.007 Author: frack113 Status: test
Adversaries may abuse msiexec.exe to proxy execution of malicious payloads. Msiexec.exe is the command-line utility for the Windows Installer and is thus commonly associated with executing installation packages (.msi)
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Suspicious Msiexec Execute Arbitrary DLL
-- Sigma ID:     6f4191bb-912b-48a8-9ce7-682769541e6d
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1218.007
-- Author:       frack113
-- Date:         2022-01-16
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_msiexec_execute_dll.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate script
-- ============================================================
-- UNSUPPORTED_MODIFIER: contains|windash

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\msiexec.exe'
    AND rawEventMsg LIKE '% -y%')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Legitimate script

Msiexec Quiet Installation

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: T1218.007 Author: frack113 Status: test
Adversaries may abuse msiexec.exe to proxy execution of malicious payloads. Msiexec.exe is the command-line utility for the Windows Installer and is thus commonly associated with executing installation packages (.msi)
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Msiexec Quiet Installation
-- Sigma ID:     79a87aa6-e4bd-42fc-a5bb-5e6fbdcd62f5
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1218.007
-- Author:       frack113
-- Date:         2022-01-16
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_msiexec_install_quiet.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    WindowsApps installing updates via the quiet flag
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND 1=1
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: WindowsApps installing updates via the quiet flag

Suspicious Msiexec Quiet Install From Remote Location

Detects usage of Msiexec.exe to install packages hosted remotely quietly

  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND 1=1
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
