Potential MsiExec Masquerading

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1036.005 Author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects the execution of msiexec.exe from an uncommon directory
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potential MsiExec Masquerading
-- Sigma ID:     e22a6eb2-f8a5-44b5-8b44-a2dbd47b1144
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1036.005
-- Author:       Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2019-11-14
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_msiexec_masquerading.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((procName LIKE '%\\msiexec.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = '\msiexec.exe'))
  AND NOT ((procName LIKE 'C:\\Windows\\System32\\%' OR procName LIKE 'C:\\Windows\\SysWOW64\\%' OR procName LIKE 'C:\\Windows\\WinSxS\\%')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

MsiExec Web Install

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: T1218.007, T1105 Author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects suspicious msiexec process starts with web addresses as parameter
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        MsiExec Web Install
-- Sigma ID:     f7b5f842-a6af-4da5-9e95-e32478f3cd2f
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1218.007, T1105
-- Author:       Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2018-02-09
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_msiexec_web_install.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    False positives depend on scripts and administrative tools used in the monitored environment
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% msiexec%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%://%')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: False positives depend on scripts and administrative tools used in the monitored environment

Windows MSIX Package Support Framework AI_STUBS Execution

low FSM Severity: 3 MITRE: execution, T1218, T1553.005, T1204.002 Author: Michael Haag, Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems) Status: experimental
Detects execution of Advanced Installer MSIX Package Support Framework (PSF) components, specifically AI_STUBS executables with original filename 'popupwrapper.exe'. This activity may indicate malicious MSIX packages build with Advanced Installer leveraging the Package Support Framework to bypass application control restrictions.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Windows MSIX Package Support Framework AI_STUBS Execution
-- Sigma ID:     af5732ed-764e-489d-826d-0447c8b36242
-- Level:        low  |  FSM Severity: 3
-- Status:       experimental
-- MITRE:        execution | T1218, T1553.005, T1204.002
-- Author:       Michael Haag, Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2025-11-03
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_msix_ai_stub_execution.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate applications packaged with Advanced Installer using Package Support Framework
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((procName LIKE '%\\AI\_STUBS\\AiStubX64Elevated.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\AI\_STUBS\\AiStubX86Elevated.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\AI\_STUBS\\AiStubX64.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\AI\_STUBS\\AiStubX86.exe')
    AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'popupwrapper.exe'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Legitimate applications packaged with Advanced Installer using Package Support Framework

Arbitrary File Download Via MSOHTMED.EXE

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: execution, T1218 Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects usage of "MSOHTMED" to download arbitrary files
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Arbitrary File Download Via MSOHTMED.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     459f2f98-397b-4a4a-9f47-6a5ec2f1c69d
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        execution | T1218
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2022-08-19
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_msohtmed_download.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%ftp://%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%http://%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%https://%'))
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\MSOHTMED.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'MsoHtmEd.exe')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Arbitrary File Download Via MSPUB.EXE

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: execution, T1218 Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects usage of "MSPUB" (Microsoft Publisher) to download arbitrary files
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Arbitrary File Download Via MSPUB.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     3b3c7f55-f771-4dd6-8a6e-08d057a17caf
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        execution | T1218
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2022-08-19
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_mspub_download.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%ftp://%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%http://%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%https://%'))
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\MSPUB.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'MSPUB.exe')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Potential Process Injection Via Msra.EXE

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1055 Author: Alexander McDonald Status: test
Detects potential process injection via Microsoft Remote Asssistance (Msra.exe) by looking at suspicious child processes spawned from the aforementioned process. It has been a target used by many threat actors and used for discovery and persistence tactics
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potential Process Injection Via Msra.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     744a188b-0415-4792-896f-11ddb0588dbc
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1055
-- Author:       Alexander McDonald
-- Date:         2022-06-24
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_msra_process_injection.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate use of Msra.exe
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] AS parentImage,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentCommand')] AS parentCommandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\msra.exe')
    AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentCommand') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentCommand')] LIKE '%msra.exe')
    AND (procName LIKE '%\\arp.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\cmd.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\net.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\netstat.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\nslookup.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\route.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\schtasks.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\whoami.exe'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Legitimate use of Msra.exe

Detection of PowerShell Execution via Sqlps.exe

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: execution, T1059.001, T1127 Author: Agro (@agro_sev) oscd.community Status: test
This rule detects execution of a PowerShell code through the sqlps.exe utility, which is included in the standard set of utilities supplied with the MSSQL Server. Script blocks are not logged in this case, so this utility helps to bypass protection mechanisms based on the analysis of these logs.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Detection of PowerShell Execution via Sqlps.exe
-- Sigma ID:     0152550d-3a26-4efd-9f0e-54a0b28ae2f3
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        execution | T1059.001, T1127
-- Author:       Agro (@agro_sev) oscd.community
-- Date:         2020-10-10
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_mssql_sqlps_susp_execution.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Direct PS command execution through SQLPS.exe is uncommon, childprocess sqlps.exe spawned by sqlagent.exe is a legitimate action.
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] AS parentImage,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\sqlps.exe')
  OR ((procName LIKE '%\\sqlps.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'sqlps.exe'))
  AND NOT (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\sqlagent.exe')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Direct PS command execution through SQLPS.exe is uncommon, childprocess sqlps.exe spawned by sqlagent.exe is a legitimate action.

SQL Client Tools PowerShell Session Detection

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: execution, T1059.001, T1127 Author: Agro (@agro_sev) oscd.communitly Status: test
This rule detects execution of a PowerShell code through the sqltoolsps.exe utility, which is included in the standard set of utilities supplied with the Microsoft SQL Server Management studio. Script blocks are not logged in this case, so this utility helps to bypass protection mechanisms based on the analysis of these logs.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        SQL Client Tools PowerShell Session Detection
-- Sigma ID:     a746c9b8-a2fb-4ee5-a428-92bee9e99060
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        execution | T1059.001, T1127
-- Author:       Agro (@agro_sev) oscd.communitly
-- Date:         2020-10-13
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_mssql_sqltoolsps_susp_execution.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Direct PS command execution through SQLToolsPS.exe is uncommon, childprocess sqltoolsps.exe spawned by smss.exe is a legitimate action.
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] AS parentImage,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((procName LIKE '%\\sqltoolsps.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\sqltoolsps.exe'))
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = '\sqltoolsps.exe'))
  AND NOT (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\smss.exe')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Direct PS command execution through SQLToolsPS.exe is uncommon, childprocess sqltoolsps.exe spawned by smss.exe is a legitimate action.

Suspicious Child Process Of SQL Server

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: persistence, T1505.003, T1190 Author: FPT.EagleEye Team, wagga Status: test
Detects suspicious child processes of the SQLServer process. This could indicate potential RCE or SQL Injection.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Suspicious Child Process Of SQL Server
-- Sigma ID:     869b9ca7-9ea2-4a5a-8325-e80e62f75445
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        persistence | T1505.003, T1190
-- Author:       FPT.EagleEye Team, wagga
-- Date:         2020-12-11
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_mssql_susp_child_process.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    (none)
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] AS parentImage,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\sqlservr.exe')
    AND (procName LIKE '%\\bash.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\bitsadmin.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\cmd.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\netstat.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\nltest.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\ping.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\powershell.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\pwsh.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\regsvr32.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\rundll32.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\sh.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\systeminfo.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\tasklist.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\wsl.exe'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;

Suspicious Child Process Of Veeam Dabatase

critical FSM Severity: 9 MITRE: persistence Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects suspicious child processes of the Veeam service process. This could indicate potential RCE or SQL Injection.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Suspicious Child Process Of Veeam Dabatase
-- Sigma ID:     d55b793d-f847-4eea-b59a-5ab09908ac90
-- Level:        critical  |  FSM Severity: 9
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        persistence
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2023-05-04
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_mssql_veaam_susp_child_processes.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    (none)
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] AS parentImage,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentCommand')] AS parentCommandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\sqlservr.exe')
    AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentCommand') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentCommand')] LIKE '%VEEAMSQL%'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;

Potential MSTSC Shadowing Activity

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1563.002 Author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects RDP session hijacking by using MSTSC shadowing
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potential MSTSC Shadowing Activity
-- Sigma ID:     6ba5a05f-b095-4f0a-8654-b825f4f16334
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1563.002
-- Author:       Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2020-01-24
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_mstsc_rdp_hijack_shadowing.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%noconsentprompt%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%shadow:%')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

New Remote Desktop Connection Initiated Via Mstsc.EXE

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: T1021.001 Author: frack113 Status: test
Detects the usage of "mstsc.exe" with the "/v" flag to initiate a connection to a remote server. Adversaries may use valid accounts to log into a computer using the Remote Desktop Protocol (RDP). The adversary may then perform actions as the logged-on user.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        New Remote Desktop Connection Initiated Via Mstsc.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     954f0af7-62dd-418f-b3df-a84bc2c7a774
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1021.001
-- Author:       frack113
-- Date:         2022-01-07
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_mstsc_remote_connection.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    WSL (Windows Sub System For Linux)
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND 1=1
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: WSL (Windows Sub System For Linux)

Mstsc.EXE Execution With Local RDP File

low FSM Severity: 3 MITRE: T1219.002 Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), Christopher Peacock @securepeacock Status: test
Detects potential RDP connection via Mstsc using a local ".rdp" file
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Mstsc.EXE Execution With Local RDP File
-- Sigma ID:     5fdce3ac-e7f9-4ecd-a3aa-a4d78ebbf0af
-- Level:        low  |  FSM Severity: 3
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1219.002
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), Christopher Peacock @securepeacock
-- Date:         2023-04-18
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_mstsc_run_local_rdp_file.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Likely with legitimate usage of ".rdp" files
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND 1=1
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Likely with legitimate usage of ".rdp" files

Suspicious Mstsc.EXE Execution With Local RDP File

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1219.002 Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects potential RDP connection via Mstsc using a local ".rdp" file located in suspicious locations.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Suspicious Mstsc.EXE Execution With Local RDP File
-- Sigma ID:     6e22722b-dfb1-4508-a911-49ac840b40f8
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1219.002
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2023-04-18
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_mstsc_run_local_rdp_file_susp_location.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Likelihood is related to how often the paths are used in the environment
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.rdp' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.rdp"'))
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\mstsc.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'mstsc.exe'))
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%:\\Users\\Public\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%:\\Windows\\System32\\spool\\drivers\\color%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%:\\Windows\\System32\\Tasks\_Migrated %' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%:\\Windows\\Tasks\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%:\\Windows\\Temp\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%:\\Windows\\Tracing\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\Downloads\\%')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Likelihood is related to how often the paths are used in the environment

Mstsc.EXE Execution From Uncommon Parent

high FSM Severity: 7 Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects potential RDP connection via Mstsc using a local ".rdp" file located in suspicious locations.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Mstsc.EXE Execution From Uncommon Parent
-- Sigma ID:     ff3b6b39-e765-42f9-bb2c-ea6761e0e0f6
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        (none)
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2023-04-18
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_mstsc_run_local_rpd_file_susp_parent.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unlikely
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] AS parentImage,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((procName LIKE '%\\mstsc.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'mstsc.exe'))
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\brave.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\CCleanerBrowser.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\chrome.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\chromium.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\firefox.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\iexplore.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\microsoftedge.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\msedge.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\opera.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\vivaldi.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\whale.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\outlook.exe')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unlikely

Msxsl.EXE Execution

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: T1220 Author: Timur Zinniatullin, oscd.community Status: test
Detects the execution of the MSXSL utility. This can be used to execute Extensible Stylesheet Language (XSL) files. These files are commonly used to describe the processing and rendering of data within XML files. Adversaries can abuse this functionality to execute arbitrary files while potentially bypassing application whitelisting defenses.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Msxsl.EXE Execution
-- Sigma ID:     9e50a8b3-dd05-4eb8-9153-bdb6b79d50b0
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1220
-- Author:       Timur Zinniatullin, oscd.community
-- Date:         2019-10-21
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_msxsl_execution.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Msxsl is not installed by default and is deprecated, so unlikely on most systems.
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND procName LIKE '%\\msxsl.exe'
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Msxsl is not installed by default and is deprecated, so unlikely on most systems.

Remote XSL Execution Via Msxsl.EXE

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1220 Author: Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel Status: test
Detects the execution of the "msxsl" binary with an "http" keyword in the command line. This might indicate a potential remote execution of XSL files.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Remote XSL Execution Via Msxsl.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     75d0a94e-6252-448d-a7be-d953dff527bb
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1220
-- Author:       Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel
-- Date:         2023-11-09
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_msxsl_remote_execution.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Msxsl is not installed by default and is deprecated, so unlikely on most systems.
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\msxsl.exe'
    AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%http%'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Msxsl is not installed by default and is deprecated, so unlikely on most systems.

Suspicious Group And Account Reconnaissance Activity Using Net.EXE

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: discovery, T1087.001, T1087.002 Author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), omkar72, @svch0st, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects suspicious reconnaissance command line activity on Windows systems using Net.EXE Check if the user that executed the commands is suspicious (e.g. service accounts, LOCAL_SYSTEM)
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Suspicious Group And Account Reconnaissance Activity Using Net.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     d95de845-b83c-4a9a-8a6a-4fc802ebf6c0
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        discovery | T1087.001, T1087.002
-- Author:       Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), omkar72, @svch0st, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2019-01-16
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_net_groups_and_accounts_recon.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Inventory tool runs; Administrative activity
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (((procName LIKE '%\\net.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\net1.exe'))
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] IN ('net.exe', 'net1.exe')))
  AND NOT (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% /add%')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Inventory tool runs; Administrative activity

Unmount Share Via Net.EXE

low FSM Severity: 3 MITRE: T1070.005 Author: oscd.community, @redcanary, Zach Stanford @svch0st Status: test
Detects when when a mounted share is removed. Adversaries may remove share connections that are no longer useful in order to clean up traces of their operation
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Unmount Share Via Net.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     cb7c4a03-2871-43c0-9bbb-18bbdb079896
-- Level:        low  |  FSM Severity: 3
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1070.005
-- Author:       oscd.community, @redcanary, Zach Stanford @svch0st
-- Date:         2020-10-08
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_net_share_unmount.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Administrators or Power users may remove their shares via cmd line
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%share%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%/delete%')
  AND ((procName LIKE '%\\net.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\net1.exe'))
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] IN ('net.exe', 'net1.exe'))))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Administrators or Power users may remove their shares via cmd line

Start Windows Service Via Net.EXE

low FSM Severity: 3 MITRE: execution, T1569.002 Author: Timur Zinniatullin, Daniil Yugoslavskiy, oscd.community Status: test
Detects the usage of the "net.exe" command to start a service using the "start" flag
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Start Windows Service Via Net.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     2a072a96-a086-49fa-bcb5-15cc5a619093
-- Level:        low  |  FSM Severity: 3
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        execution | T1569.002
-- Author:       Timur Zinniatullin, Daniil Yugoslavskiy, oscd.community
-- Date:         2019-10-21
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_net_start_service.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate administrator or user executes a service for legitimate reasons.
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% start %')
  AND ((procName LIKE '%\\net.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\net1.exe'))
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] IN ('net.exe', 'net1.exe'))))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Legitimate administrator or user executes a service for legitimate reasons.

Stop Windows Service Via Net.EXE

low FSM Severity: 3 MITRE: impact, T1489 Author: Jakob Weinzettl, oscd.community, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects the stopping of a Windows service via the "net" utility.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Stop Windows Service Via Net.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     88872991-7445-4a22-90b2-a3adadb0e827
-- Level:        low  |  FSM Severity: 3
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        impact | T1489
-- Author:       Jakob Weinzettl, oscd.community, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2023-03-05
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_net_stop_service.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    There are many legitimate reasons to stop a service. This rule isn't looking for any suspicious behaviour in particular. Filter legitimate activity accordingly
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% stop %')
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] IN ('net.exe', 'net1.exe')))
  OR ((procName LIKE '%\\net.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\net1.exe')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: There are many legitimate reasons to stop a service. This rule isn't looking for any suspicious behaviour in particular. Filter legitimate activity accordingly

Windows Admin Share Mount Via Net.EXE

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: T1021.002 Author: oscd.community, Teymur Kheirkhabarov @HeirhabarovT, Zach Stanford @svch0st, wagga Status: test
Detects when an admin share is mounted using net.exe
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Windows Admin Share Mount Via Net.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     3abd6094-7027-475f-9630-8ab9be7b9725
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1021.002
-- Author:       oscd.community, Teymur Kheirkhabarov @HeirhabarovT, Zach Stanford @svch0st, wagga
-- Date:         2020-10-05
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_net_use_mount_admin_share.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Administrators
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% use %' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% \\\\\\\\*\\\\*$%')
  AND ((procName LIKE '%\\net.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\net1.exe'))
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] IN ('net.exe', 'net1.exe'))))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Administrators

Windows Internet Hosted WebDav Share Mount Via Net.EXE

Detects when an internet hosted webdav share is mounted using the "net.exe" utility

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% use %' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% http%')
  AND ((procName LIKE '%\\net.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\net1.exe'))
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0

Windows Share Mount Via Net.EXE

low FSM Severity: 3 MITRE: T1021.002 Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects when a share is mounted using the "net.exe" utility
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Windows Share Mount Via Net.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     f117933c-980c-4f78-b384-e3d838111165
-- Level:        low  |  FSM Severity: 3
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1021.002
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2023-02-02
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_net_use_mount_share.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate activity by administrators and scripts
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% use %' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% \\\\\\\\%'))
  AND ((procName LIKE '%\\net.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\net1.exe'))
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] IN ('net.exe', 'net1.exe'))))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Legitimate activity by administrators and scripts

System Network Connections Discovery Via Net.EXE

low FSM Severity: 3 MITRE: discovery, T1049 Author: frack113 Status: test
Adversaries may attempt to get a listing of network connections to or from the compromised system they are currently accessing or from remote systems by querying for information over the network.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        System Network Connections Discovery Via Net.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     1c67a717-32ba-409b-a45d-0fb704a73a81
-- Level:        low  |  FSM Severity: 3
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        discovery | T1049
-- Author:       frack113
-- Date:         2021-12-10
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_net_use_network_connections_discovery.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% use' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% sessions')))
  OR ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% use %' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% sessions %')))
  AND ((procName LIKE '%\\net.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\net1.exe'))
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] IN ('net.exe', 'net1.exe'))))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Password Provided In Command Line Of Net.EXE

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: persistence, T1021.002, T1078 Author: Tim Shelton (HAWK.IO) Status: test
Detects a when net.exe is called with a password in the command line
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Password Provided In Command Line Of Net.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     d4498716-1d52-438f-8084-4a603157d131
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        persistence | T1021.002, T1078
-- Author:       Tim Shelton (HAWK.IO)
-- Date:         2021-12-09
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_net_use_password_plaintext.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND 1=1
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

New User Created Via Net.EXE

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: persistence, T1136.001 Author: Endgame, JHasenbusch (adapted to Sigma for oscd.community) Status: test
Identifies the creation of local users via the net.exe command.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        New User Created Via Net.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     cd219ff3-fa99-45d4-8380-a7d15116c6dc
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        persistence | T1136.001
-- Author:       Endgame, JHasenbusch (adapted to Sigma for oscd.community)
-- Date:         2018-10-30
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_net_user_add.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate user creation.; Better use event IDs for user creation rather than command line rules.
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%user%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%add%')
  AND ((procName LIKE '%\\net.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\net1.exe'))
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] IN ('net.exe', 'net1.exe'))))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Legitimate user creation.; Better use event IDs for user creation rather than command line rules.

New User Created Via Net.EXE With Never Expire Option

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: persistence, T1136.001 Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects creation of local users via the net.exe command with the option "never expire"
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        New User Created Via Net.EXE With Never Expire Option
-- Sigma ID:     b9f0e6f5-09b4-4358-bae4-08408705bd5c
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        persistence | T1136.001
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2022-07-12
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_net_user_add_never_expire.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unlikely
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%user%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%add%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%expires:never%')
  AND ((procName LIKE '%\\net.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\net1.exe'))
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] IN ('net.exe', 'net1.exe'))))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unlikely

Suspicious Manipulation Of Default Accounts Via Net.EXE

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: collection, T1560.001 Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects suspicious manipulations of default accounts such as 'administrator' and 'guest'. For example 'enable' or 'disable' accounts or change the password...etc
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Suspicious Manipulation Of Default Accounts Via Net.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     5b768e71-86f2-4879-b448-81061cbae951
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        collection | T1560.001
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2022-09-01
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_net_user_default_accounts_manipulation.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Some false positives could occur with the admin or guest account. It depends on the scripts being used by the admins in your env. If you experience a lot of FP you could reduce the level to medium
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND NOT (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%guest%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%/active no%'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Some false positives could occur with the admin or guest account. It depends on the scripts being used by the admins in your env. If you experience a lot of FP you could reduce the level to medium

Share And Session Enumeration Using Net.EXE

low FSM Severity: 3 MITRE: discovery, T1018 Author: Endgame, JHasenbusch (ported for oscd.community) Status: stable
Detects attempts to enumerate file shares, printer shares and sessions using "net.exe" with the "view" flag.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Share And Session Enumeration Using Net.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     62510e69-616b-4078-b371-847da438cc03
-- Level:        low  |  FSM Severity: 3
-- Status:       stable
-- MITRE:        discovery | T1018
-- Author:       Endgame, JHasenbusch (ported for oscd.community)
-- Date:         2018-10-30
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_net_view_share_and_sessions_enum.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate use of net.exe utility by legitimate user
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND NOT (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\\\\\\\%'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Legitimate use of net.exe utility by legitimate user

New Firewall Rule Added Via Netsh.EXE

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: T1562.004 Author: Markus Neis, Sander Wiebing Status: test
Detects the addition of a new rule to the Windows firewall via netsh
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        New Firewall Rule Added Via Netsh.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     cd5cfd80-aa5f-44c0-9c20-108c4ae12e3c
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1562.004
-- Author:       Markus Neis, Sander Wiebing
-- Date:         2019-01-29
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_netsh_fw_add_rule.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate administration activity; Software installations
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND 1=1
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Legitimate administration activity; Software installations

Suspicious Program Location Whitelisted In Firewall Via Netsh.EXE

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1562.004 Author: Sander Wiebing, Jonhnathan Ribeiro, Daniil Yugoslavskiy, oscd.community Status: test
Detects Netsh command execution that whitelists a program located in a suspicious location in the Windows Firewall
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Suspicious Program Location Whitelisted In Firewall Via Netsh.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     a35f5a72-f347-4e36-8895-9869b0d5fc6d
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1562.004
-- Author:       Sander Wiebing, Jonhnathan Ribeiro, Daniil Yugoslavskiy, oscd.community
-- Date:         2020-05-25
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_netsh_fw_allow_program_in_susp_location.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%firewall%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%add%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%allowedprogram%'))
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%advfirewall%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%firewall%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%add%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%rule%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%action=allow%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%program=%'))
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\netsh.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'netsh.exe'))
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%:\\$Recycle.bin\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%:\\RECYCLER.BIN\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%:\\RECYCLERS.BIN\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%:\\SystemVolumeInformation\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%:\\Temp\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%:\\Users\\Default\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%:\\Users\\Desktop\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%:\\Users\\Public\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%:\\Windows\\addins\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%:\\Windows\\cursors\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%:\\Windows\\debug\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%:\\Windows\\drivers\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%:\\Windows\\fonts\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%:\\Windows\\help\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%:\\Windows\\system32\\tasks\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%:\\Windows\\Tasks\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%:\\Windows\\Temp\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\Downloads\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\Local Settings\\Temporary Internet Files\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\Temporary Internet Files\\Content.Outlook\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\%Public\%\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\%TEMP\%%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\%TMP\%%')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

RDP Connection Allowed Via Netsh.EXE

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1562.004 Author: Sander Wiebing Status: test
Detects usage of the netsh command to open and allow connections to port 3389 (RDP). As seen used by Sarwent Malware
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        RDP Connection Allowed Via Netsh.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     01aeb693-138d-49d2-9403-c4f52d7d3d62
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1562.004
-- Author:       Sander Wiebing
-- Date:         2020-05-23
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_netsh_fw_allow_rdp.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate administration activity
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%firewall %' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%add %' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%tcp %' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%3389%')
    AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%portopening%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%allow%')))
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\netsh.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'netsh.exe')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Legitimate administration activity

  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR

Detects netsh commands that turns off the Windows firewall

  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\netsh.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'netsh.exe'))

Netsh Allow Group Policy on Microsoft Defender Firewall

Adversaries may modify system firewalls in order to bypass controls limiting network usage

-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Netsh Allow Group Policy on Microsoft Defender Firewall
-- Sigma ID:     347906f3-e207-4d18-ae5b-a9403d6bcdef
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1562.004
-- Author:       frack113
-- Date:         2022-01-09
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_netsh_fw_enable_group_rule.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate administration activity
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%advfirewall%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%firewall%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%set%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%rule%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%group=%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%new%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%enable=Yes%')
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\netsh.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'netsh.exe')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Legitimate administration activity

Firewall Configuration Discovery Via Netsh.EXE

low FSM Severity: 3 MITRE: discovery, T1016 Author: frack113, Christopher Peacock '@securepeacock', SCYTHE '@scythe_io' Status: test
Adversaries may look for details about the network configuration and settings of systems they access or through information discovery of remote systems
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Firewall Configuration Discovery Via Netsh.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     0e4164da-94bc-450d-a7be-a4b176179f1f
-- Level:        low  |  FSM Severity: 3
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        discovery | T1016
-- Author:       frack113, Christopher Peacock '@securepeacock', SCYTHE '@scythe_io'
-- Date:         2021-12-07
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_netsh_fw_rules_discovery.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Administrative activity
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%netsh%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%show %' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%firewall %')
    AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%config %' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%state %' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%rule %' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%name=all%')))
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\netsh.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'netsh.exe')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Administrative activity

  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,

Potential Persistence Via Netsh Helper DLL

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: persistence, T1546.007 Author: Victor Sergeev, oscd.community Status: test
Detects the execution of netsh with "add helper" flag in order to add a custom helper DLL. This technique can be abused to add a malicious helper DLL that can be used as a persistence proxy that gets called when netsh.exe is executed.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potential Persistence Via Netsh Helper DLL
-- Sigma ID:     56321594-9087-49d9-bf10-524fe8479452
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        persistence | T1546.007
-- Author:       Victor Sergeev, oscd.community

-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_netsh_helper_dll_persistence.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%add%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%helper%')
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'netsh.exe'))
  OR (procName LIKE '%\\netsh.exe'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

New Network Trace Capture Started Via Netsh.EXE

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: discovery, T1040 Author: Kutepov Anton, oscd.community Status: test
Detects the execution of netsh with the "trace" flag in order to start a network capture

-- ============================================================
-- Title:        New Network Trace Capture Started Via Netsh.EXE

  reptDevIpAddrV4,

  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
