New Port Forwarding Rule Added Via Netsh.EXE

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: T1090 Author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), omkar72, oscd.community, Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel Status: test
Detects the execution of netsh commands that configure a new port forwarding (PortProxy) rule
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        New Port Forwarding Rule Added Via Netsh.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     322ed9ec-fcab-4f67-9a34-e7c6aef43614
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1090
-- Author:       Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), omkar72, oscd.community, Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel
-- Date:         2019-01-29
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_netsh_port_forwarding.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate administration activity; WSL2 network bridge PowerShell script used for WSL/Kubernetes/Docker (e.g. https://github.com/microsoft/WSL/issues/4150#issuecomment-504209723)
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\netsh.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'netsh.exe'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Legitimate administration activity; WSL2 network bridge PowerShell script used for WSL/Kubernetes/Docker (e.g. https://github.com/microsoft/WSL/issues/4150#issuecomment-504209723)

RDP Port Forwarding Rule Added Via Netsh.EXE

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1090 Author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), oscd.community Status: test
Detects the execution of netsh to configure a port forwarding of port 3389 (RDP) rule
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        RDP Port Forwarding Rule Added Via Netsh.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     782d6f3e-4c5d-4b8c-92a3-1d05fed72e63
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1090
-- Author:       Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), oscd.community
-- Date:         2019-01-29
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_netsh_port_forwarding_3389.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate administration activity
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% i%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% p%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%=3389%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% c%')
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\netsh.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'netsh.exe')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Legitimate administration activity

Harvesting Of Wifi Credentials Via Netsh.EXE

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: discovery, T1040 Author: Andreas Hunkeler (@Karneades), oscd.community Status: test
Detect the harvesting of wifi credentials using netsh.exe
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Harvesting Of Wifi Credentials Via Netsh.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     42b1a5b8-353f-4f10-b256-39de4467faff
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        discovery | T1040
-- Author:       Andreas Hunkeler (@Karneades), oscd.community
-- Date:         2020-04-20
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_netsh_wifi_credential_harvesting.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%wlan%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% s%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% p%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% k%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%=clear%')
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\netsh.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'netsh.exe')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Nltest.EXE Execution

low FSM Severity: 3 MITRE: discovery, T1016, T1018, T1482 Author: Arun Chauhan Status: test
Detects nltest commands that can be used for information discovery
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Nltest.EXE Execution
-- Sigma ID:     903076ff-f442-475a-b667-4f246bcc203b
-- Level:        low  |  FSM Severity: 3
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        discovery | T1016, T1018, T1482
-- Author:       Arun Chauhan
-- Date:         2023-02-03
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_nltest_execution.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate administration activity
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\nltest.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'nltestrk.exe'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Legitimate administration activity

Potential Recon Activity Via Nltest.EXE

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: discovery, T1016, T1482 Author: Craig Young, oscd.community, Georg Lauenstein Status: test
Detects nltest commands that can be used for information discovery
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potential Recon Activity Via Nltest.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     5cc90652-4cbd-4241-aa3b-4b462fa5a248
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        discovery | T1016, T1482
-- Author:       Craig Young, oscd.community, Georg Lauenstein
-- Date:         2021-07-24
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_nltest_recon.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate administration use but user and host must be investigated
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((procName LIKE '%\\nltest.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'nltestrk.exe'))
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%server%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%query%'))
  OR ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%/user%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%all\_trusts%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%dclist:%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%dnsgetdc:%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%domain\_trusts%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%dsgetdc:%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%parentdomain%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%trusted\_domains%'))))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Legitimate administration use but user and host must be investigated

Potential Arbitrary Code Execution Via Node.EXE

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1127 Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects the execution node.exe which is shipped with multiple software such as VMware, Adobe...etc. In order to execute arbitrary code. For example to establish reverse shell as seen in Log4j attacks...etc
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potential Arbitrary Code Execution Via Node.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     6640f31c-01ad-49b5-beb5-83498a5cd8bd
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1127
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2022-09-09
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_node_abuse.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unlikely
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\node.exe'
    AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% -e %' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% --eval %')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unlikely

Node Process Executions

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: execution, T1127, T1059.007 Author: Max Altgelt (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects the execution of other scripts using the Node executable packaged with Adobe Creative Cloud
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Node Process Executions
-- Sigma ID:     df1f26d3-bea7-4700-9ea2-ad3e990cf90e
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        execution | T1127, T1059.007
-- Author:       Max Altgelt (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2022-04-06
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_node_adobe_creative_cloud_abuse.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\Adobe Creative Cloud Experience\\libs\\node.exe'
  AND NOT (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%Adobe Creative Cloud Experience\\js%')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Notepad Password Files Discovery

low FSM Severity: 3 MITRE: discovery, T1083 Author: The DFIR Report Status: experimental
Detects the execution of Notepad to open a file that has the string "password" which may indicate unauthorized access to credentials or suspicious activity.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Notepad Password Files Discovery
-- Sigma ID:     3b4e950b-a3ea-44d3-877e-432071990709
-- Level:        low  |  FSM Severity: 3
-- Status:       experimental
-- MITRE:        discovery | T1083
-- Author:       The DFIR Report
-- Date:         2025-02-21
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_notepad_local_passwd_discovery.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate use of opening files from remote hosts by administrators or users. However, storing passwords in text readable format could potentially be a violation of the organization's policy. Any match should be investigated further.
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] AS parentImage,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\explorer.exe')
    AND procName LIKE '%\\notepad.exe'
    AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%password*.txt' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%password*.csv' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%password*.doc' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%password*.xls')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Legitimate use of opening files from remote hosts by administrators or users. However, storing passwords in text readable format could potentially be a violation of the organization's policy. Any match should be investigated further.

Network Reconnaissance Activity

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: discovery, T1087, T1082 Author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects a set of suspicious network related commands often used in recon stages
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Network Reconnaissance Activity
-- Sigma ID:     e6313acd-208c-44fc-a0ff-db85d572e90e
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        discovery | T1087, T1082
-- Author:       Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2022-02-07
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_nslookup_domain_discovery.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    False positives depend on scripts and administrative tools used in the monitored environment
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%nslookup%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\_ldap.\_tcp.dc.\_msdcs.%')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: False positives depend on scripts and administrative tools used in the monitored environment

Nslookup PowerShell Download Cradle - ProcessCreation

medium FSM Severity: 5 Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects suspicious powershell download cradle using nslookup. This cradle uses nslookup to extract payloads from DNS records
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Nslookup PowerShell Download Cradle - ProcessCreation
-- Sigma ID:     1b3b01c7-84e9-4072-86e5-fc285a41ff23
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        (none)
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2022-09-05
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_nslookup_poweshell_download.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] AS parentImage,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\powershell.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\pwsh.exe'))
    AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% -q=txt %' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% -querytype=txt %')))
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\nslookup.exe%')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = '\nslookup.exe')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Suspicious Usage Of Active Directory Diagnostic Tool (ntdsutil.exe)

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: T1003.003 Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects execution of ntdsutil.exe to perform different actions such as restoring snapshots...etc.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Suspicious Usage Of Active Directory Diagnostic Tool (ntdsutil.exe)
-- Sigma ID:     a58353df-af43-4753-bad0-cd83ef35eef5
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1003.003
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2022-09-14
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_ntdsutil_susp_usage.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate usage to restore snapshots; Legitimate admin activity
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%snapshot%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%mount %'))
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%ac%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% i%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% ntds%'))
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\ntdsutil.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'ntdsutil.exe')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Legitimate usage to restore snapshots; Legitimate admin activity

Invocation of Active Directory Diagnostic Tool (ntdsutil.exe)

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: T1003.003 Author: Thomas Patzke Status: test
Detects execution of ntdsutil.exe, which can be used for various attacks against the NTDS database (NTDS.DIT)
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Invocation of Active Directory Diagnostic Tool (ntdsutil.exe)
-- Sigma ID:     2afafd61-6aae-4df4-baed-139fa1f4c345
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1003.003
-- Author:       Thomas Patzke
-- Date:         2019-01-16
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_ntdsutil_usage.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    NTDS maintenance
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND procName LIKE '%\\ntdsutil.exe'
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: NTDS maintenance

Driver/DLL Installation Via Odbcconf.EXE

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: T1218.008 Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects execution of "odbcconf" with "INSTALLDRIVER" which installs a new ODBC driver. Attackers abuse this to install and run malicious DLLs.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Driver/DLL Installation Via Odbcconf.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     3f5491e2-8db8-496b-9e95-1029fce852d4
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1218.008
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2023-05-22
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_odbcconf_driver_install.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate driver DLLs being registered via "odbcconf" will generate false positives. Investigate the path of the DLL and its contents to determine if the action is authorized.
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%INSTALLDRIVER %' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.dll%')
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\odbcconf.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'odbcconf.exe')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Legitimate driver DLLs being registered via "odbcconf" will generate false positives. Investigate the path of the DLL and its contents to determine if the action is authorized.

Suspicious Driver/DLL Installation Via Odbcconf.EXE

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1218.008 Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects execution of "odbcconf" with the "INSTALLDRIVER" action where the driver doesn't contain a ".dll" extension. This is often used as a defense evasion method.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Suspicious Driver/DLL Installation Via Odbcconf.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     cb0fe7c5-f3a3-484d-aa25-d350a7912729
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1218.008
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2023-05-23
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_odbcconf_driver_install_susp.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unlikely
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND 1=1
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unlikely

Odbcconf.EXE Suspicious DLL Location

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1218.008 Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects execution of "odbcconf" where the path of the DLL being registered is located in a potentially suspicious location.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Odbcconf.EXE Suspicious DLL Location
-- Sigma ID:     6b65c28e-11f3-46cb-902a-68f2cafaf474
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1218.008
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2023-05-22
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_odbcconf_exec_susp_locations.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unlikely
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%:\\PerfLogs\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%:\\ProgramData\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%:\\Temp\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%:\\Users\\Public\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%:\\Windows\\Registration\\CRMLog%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%:\\Windows\\System32\\com\\dmp\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%:\\Windows\\System32\\FxsTmp\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%:\\Windows\\System32\\Microsoft\\Crypto\\RSA\\MachineKeys\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%:\\Windows\\System32\\spool\\drivers\\color\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%:\\Windows\\System32\\spool\\PRINTERS\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%:\\Windows\\System32\\spool\\SERVERS\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%:\\Windows\\System32\\Tasks\_Migrated\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%:\\Windows\\System32\\Tasks\\Microsoft\\Windows\\SyncCenter\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%:\\Windows\\SysWOW64\\com\\dmp\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%:\\Windows\\SysWOW64\\FxsTmp\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%:\\Windows\\SysWOW64\\Tasks\\Microsoft\\Windows\\PLA\\System\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%:\\Windows\\SysWOW64\\Tasks\\Microsoft\\Windows\\SyncCenter\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%:\\Windows\\Tasks\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%:\\Windows\\Temp\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%:\\Windows\\Tracing\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\AppData\\Roaming\\%'))
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\odbcconf.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'odbcconf.exe')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unlikely

New DLL Registered Via Odbcconf.EXE

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: T1218.008 Author: Kirill Kiryanov, Beyu Denis, Daniil Yugoslavskiy, oscd.community, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects execution of "odbcconf" with "REGSVR" in order to register a new DLL (equivalent to running regsvr32). Attackers abuse this to install and run malicious DLLs.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        New DLL Registered Via Odbcconf.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     9f0a8bf3-a65b-440a-8c1e-5cb1547c8e70
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1218.008
-- Author:       Kirill Kiryanov, Beyu Denis, Daniil Yugoslavskiy, oscd.community, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2023-05-22
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_odbcconf_register_dll_regsvr.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate DLLs being registered via "odbcconf" will generate false positives. Investigate the path of the DLL and its content to determine if the action is authorized.
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%REGSVR %' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.dll%')
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\odbcconf.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'odbcconf.exe')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Legitimate DLLs being registered via "odbcconf" will generate false positives. Investigate the path of the DLL and its content to determine if the action is authorized.

Potentially Suspicious DLL Registered Via Odbcconf.EXE

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1218.008 Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects execution of "odbcconf" with the "REGSVR" action where the DLL in question doesn't contain a ".dll" extension. Which is often used as a method to evade defenses.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potentially Suspicious DLL Registered Via Odbcconf.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     ba4cfc11-d0fa-4d94-bf20-7c332c412e76
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1218.008
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2023-05-22
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_odbcconf_register_dll_regsvr_susp.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unlikely
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND 1=1
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unlikely

Response File Execution Via Odbcconf.EXE

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: T1218.008 Author: Kirill Kiryanov, Beyu Denis, Daniil Yugoslavskiy, oscd.community, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects execution of "odbcconf" with the "-f" flag in order to load a response file which might contain a malicious action.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Response File Execution Via Odbcconf.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     5f03babb-12db-4eec-8c82-7b4cb5580868
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1218.008
-- Author:       Kirill Kiryanov, Beyu Denis, Daniil Yugoslavskiy, oscd.community, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2023-05-22
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_odbcconf_response_file.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    The rule is looking for any usage of response file, which might generate false positive when this function is used legitimately. Investigate the contents of the ".rsp" file to determine if it is malicious and apply additional filters if necessary.
-- ============================================================
-- UNSUPPORTED_MODIFIER: contains|windash

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (rawEventMsg LIKE '% -f %'
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\odbcconf.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'odbcconf.exe'))
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.rsp%'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: The rule is looking for any usage of response file, which might generate false positive when this function is used legitimately. Investigate the contents of the ".rsp" file to determine if it is malicious and apply additional filters if necessary.

Suspicious Response File Execution Via Odbcconf.EXE

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1218.008 Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects execution of "odbcconf" with the "-f" flag in order to load a response file with a non-".rsp" extension.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Suspicious Response File Execution Via Odbcconf.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     2d32dd6f-3196-4093-b9eb-1ad8ab088ca5
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1218.008
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2023-05-22
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_odbcconf_response_file_susp.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unlikely
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND 1=1
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unlikely

Uncommon Child Process Spawned By Odbcconf.EXE

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: T1218.008 Author: Harjot Singh @cyb3rjy0t Status: test
Detects an uncommon child process of "odbcconf.exe" binary which normally shouldn't have any child processes.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Uncommon Child Process Spawned By Odbcconf.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     8e3c7994-131e-4ba5-b6ea-804d49113a26
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1218.008
-- Author:       Harjot Singh @cyb3rjy0t
-- Date:         2023-05-22
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_odbcconf_uncommon_child_process.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    In rare occurrences where "odbcconf" crashes. It might spawn a "werfault" process; Other child processes will depend on the DLL being registered by actions like "regsvr". In case where the DLLs have external calls (which should be rare). Other child processes might spawn and additional filters need to be applied.
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] AS parentImage,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\odbcconf.exe')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: In rare occurrences where "odbcconf" crashes. It might spawn a "werfault" process; Other child processes will depend on the DLL being registered by actions like "regsvr". In case where the DLLs have external calls (which should be rare). Other child processes might spawn and additional filters need to be applied.

Potential Arbitrary File Download Using Office Application

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1202 Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), Beyu Denis, oscd.community Status: test
Detects potential arbitrary file download using a Microsoft Office application
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potential Arbitrary File Download Using Office Application
-- Sigma ID:     4ae3e30b-b03f-43aa-87e3-b622f4048eed
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1202
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), Beyu Denis, oscd.community
-- Date:         2022-05-17
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_office_arbitrary_cli_download.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%http://%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%https://%'))
  AND ((procName LIKE '%\\EXCEL.EXE' OR procName LIKE '%\\POWERPNT.EXE' OR procName LIKE '%\\WINWORD.exe'))
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] IN ('Excel.exe', 'POWERPNT.EXE', 'WinWord.exe'))))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Potential Excel.EXE DCOM Lateral Movement Via ActivateMicrosoftApp

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1021.003 Author: Aaron Stratton Status: test
Detects suspicious child processes of Excel which could be an indicator of lateral movement leveraging the "ActivateMicrosoftApp" Excel DCOM object.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potential Excel.EXE DCOM Lateral Movement Via ActivateMicrosoftApp
-- Sigma ID:     551d9c1f-816c-445b-a7a6-7a3864720d60
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1021.003
-- Author:       Aaron Stratton
-- Date:         2023-11-13
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_office_excel_dcom_lateral_movement.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] AS parentImage,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] IN ('foxprow.exe', 'schdplus.exe', 'winproj.exe')))
  OR ((procName LIKE '%\\foxprow.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\schdplus.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\winproj.exe'))
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\excel.exe'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Potentially Suspicious Office Document Executed From Trusted Location

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1202 Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects the execution of an Office application that points to a document that is located in a trusted location. Attackers often used this to avoid macro security and execute their malicious code.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potentially Suspicious Office Document Executed From Trusted Location
-- Sigma ID:     f99abdf0-6283-4e71-bd2b-b5c048a94743
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1202
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2023-06-21
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_office_exec_from_trusted_locations.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND 1=1
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

OneNote.EXE Execution of Malicious Embedded Scripts

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1218.001 Author: @kostastsale Status: test
Detects the execution of malicious OneNote documents that contain embedded scripts. When a user clicks on a OneNote attachment and then on the malicious link inside the ".one" file, it exports and executes the malicious embedded script from specific directories.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        OneNote.EXE Execution of Malicious Embedded Scripts
-- Sigma ID:     84b1706c-932a-44c4-ae28-892b28a25b94
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1218.001
-- Author:       @kostastsale
-- Date:         2023-02-02
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_office_onenote_embedded_script_execution.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unlikely
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] AS parentImage,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\onenote.exe')
    AND (procName LIKE '%\\cmd.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\cscript.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\mshta.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\powershell.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\pwsh.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\wscript.exe')
    AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\exported\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\onenoteofflinecache\_files\\%')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unlikely

Suspicious Microsoft OneNote Child Process

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1566, T1566.001 Author: Tim Rauch (Nextron Systems), Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), Elastic (idea) Status: test
Detects suspicious child processes of the Microsoft OneNote application. This may indicate an attempt to execute malicious embedded objects from a .one file.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Suspicious Microsoft OneNote Child Process
-- Sigma ID:     c27515df-97a9-4162-8a60-dc0eeb51b775
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1566, T1566.001
-- Author:       Tim Rauch (Nextron Systems), Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), Elastic (idea)
-- Date:         2022-10-21
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_office_onenote_susp_child_processes.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    File located in the AppData folder with trusted signature
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] AS parentImage,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\onenote.exe')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: File located in the AppData folder with trusted signature

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\Outlook\\Security\\EnableUnsafeClientMailRules%')

Detects a suspicious program execution in Outlook temp folder

LIMIT 1000;

-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Suspicious Outlook Child Process
-- Sigma ID:     208748f7-881d-47ac-a29c-07ea84bf691d
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        execution | T1204.002
-- Author:       Michael Haag, Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Markus Neis, Elastic, FPT.EagleEye Team
-- Date:         2022-02-28
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_office_outlook_susp_child_processes.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] AS parentImage,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\OUTLOOK.EXE')
    AND (procName LIKE '%\\AppVLP.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\bash.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\cmd.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\cscript.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\forfiles.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\hh.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\mftrace.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\msbuild.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\msdt.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\mshta.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\msiexec.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\powershell.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\pwsh.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\regsvr32.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\schtasks.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\scrcons.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\scriptrunner.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\sh.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\svchost.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\wmic.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\wscript.exe'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Suspicious Remote Child Process From Outlook

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: execution, T1059, T1202 Author: Markus Neis, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects a suspicious child process spawning from Outlook where the image is located in a remote location (SMB/WebDav shares).
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Suspicious Remote Child Process From Outlook
-- Sigma ID:     e212d415-0e93-435f-9e1a-f29005bb4723
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        execution | T1059, T1202
-- Author:       Markus Neis, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2018-12-27
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_office_outlook_susp_child_processes_remote.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] AS parentImage,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\outlook.exe')
    AND procName LIKE '\\\\\\\\%')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Suspicious Binary In User Directory Spawned From Office Application

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: execution, T1204.002 Author: Jason Lynch Status: test
Detects an executable in the users directory started from one of the Microsoft Office suite applications (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Publisher, Visio)
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Suspicious Binary In User Directory Spawned From Office Application
-- Sigma ID:     aa3a6f94-890e-4e22-b634-ffdfd54792cc
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        execution | T1204.002
-- Author:       Jason Lynch
-- Date:         2019-04-02
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_office_spawn_exe_from_users_directory.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] AS parentImage,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\WINWORD.EXE' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\EXCEL.EXE' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\POWERPNT.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\MSPUB.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\VISIO.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\MSACCESS.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\EQNEDT32.exe'))
    AND procName LIKE 'C:\\users\\%'
    AND procName LIKE '%.exe')
  AND NOT (procName LIKE '%\\Teams.exe'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Suspicious Microsoft Office Child Process

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: execution, T1047, T1204.002, T1218.010 Author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Markus Neis, FPT.EagleEye Team, Vadim Khrykov, Cyb3rEng, Michael Haag, Christopher Peacock @securepeacock, @scythe_io Status: test
Detects a suspicious process spawning from one of the Microsoft Office suite products (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Publisher, Visio, etc.)
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Suspicious Microsoft Office Child Process
-- Sigma ID:     438025f9-5856-4663-83f7-52f878a70a50
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        execution | T1047, T1204.002, T1218.010
-- Author:       Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Markus Neis, FPT.EagleEye Team, Vadim Khrykov, Cyb3rEng, Michael Haag, Christopher Peacock @securepeacock, @scythe_io
-- Date:         2018-04-06
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_office_susp_child_processes.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] AS parentImage,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\EQNEDT32.EXE' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\EXCEL.EXE' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\MSACCESS.EXE' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\MSPUB.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\ONENOTE.EXE' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\POWERPNT.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\VISIO.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\WINWORD.EXE' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\wordpad.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\wordview.exe'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Potential Arbitrary DLL Load Using Winword

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: T1202 Author: Victor Sergeev, oscd.community Status: test
Detects potential DLL sideloading using the Microsoft Office winword process via the '/l' flag.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potential Arbitrary DLL Load Using Winword
-- Sigma ID:     f7375e28-5c14-432f-b8d1-1db26c832df3
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1202
-- Author:       Victor Sergeev, oscd.community
-- Date:         2020-10-09
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_office_winword_dll_load.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%/l %' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.dll%')
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\WINWORD.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'WinWord.exe')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Potential Mpclient.DLL Sideloading Via OfflineScannerShell.EXE Execution

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: T1218 Author: frack113 Status: test
Detects execution of Windows Defender "OfflineScannerShell.exe" from its non standard directory. The "OfflineScannerShell.exe" binary is vulnerable to DLL side loading and will load any DLL named "mpclient.dll" from the current working directory.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potential Mpclient.DLL Sideloading Via OfflineScannerShell.EXE Execution
-- Sigma ID:     02b18447-ea83-4b1b-8805-714a8a34546a
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1218
-- Author:       frack113
-- Date:         2022-03-06
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_offlinescannershell_mpclient_sideloading.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\OfflineScannerShell.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'OfflineScannerShell.exe'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

PDQ Deploy Remote Adminstartion Tool Execution

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: execution, T1072 Author: frack113 Status: test
Detect use of PDQ Deploy remote admin tool
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        PDQ Deploy Remote Adminstartion Tool Execution
-- Sigma ID:     d679950c-abb7-43a6-80fb-2a480c4fc450
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        execution | T1072
-- Author:       frack113
-- Date:         2022-10-01
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_pdqdeploy_execution.yml
-- Unmapped:     Description
-- False Pos:    Legitimate use
-- ============================================================
-- UNMAPPED_FIELD: Description

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'product')] AS product,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'company')] AS company,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (rawEventMsg = 'PDQ Deploy Console')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'product') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'product')] = 'PDQ Deploy'))
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'company') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'company')] = 'PDQ.com'))
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'PDQDeployConsole.exe'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Legitimate use

Potentially Suspicious Execution Of PDQDeployRunner

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: execution Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects suspicious execution of "PDQDeployRunner" which is part of the PDQDeploy service stack that is responsible for executing commands and packages on a remote machines
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potentially Suspicious Execution Of PDQDeployRunner
-- Sigma ID:     12b8e9f5-96b2-41e1-9a42-8c6779a5c184
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        execution
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2022-07-22
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_pdqdeploy_runner_susp_children.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate use of the PDQDeploy tool to execute these commands
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] AS parentImage,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (((procName LIKE '%\\bash.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\certutil.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\cmd.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\csc.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\cscript.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\dllhost.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\mshta.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\msiexec.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\regsvr32.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\rundll32.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\scriptrunner.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\wmic.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\wscript.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\wsl.exe'))
  OR ((procName LIKE '%:\\ProgramData\\%' OR procName LIKE '%:\\Users\\Public\\%' OR procName LIKE '%:\\Windows\\TEMP\\%' OR procName LIKE '%\\AppData\\Local\\Temp%'))
  OR ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% -decode %' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% -enc %' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% -encodedcommand %' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% -w hidden%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%DownloadString%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%FromBase64String%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%http%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%iex %' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%Invoke-%')))
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\PDQDeployRunner-%'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Legitimate use of the PDQDeploy tool to execute these commands

Perl Inline Command Execution

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: execution, T1059 Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects execution of perl using the "-e"/"-E" flags. This is could be used as a way to launch a reverse shell or execute live perl code.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Perl Inline Command Execution
-- Sigma ID:     f426547a-e0f7-441a-b63e-854ac5bdf54d
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        execution | T1059
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2023-01-02
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_perl_inline_command_execution.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% -e%')
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\perl.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'perl.exe')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Php Inline Command Execution

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: execution, T1059 Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects execution of php using the "-r" flag. This is could be used as a way to launch a reverse shell or execute live php code.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Php Inline Command Execution
-- Sigma ID:     d81871ef-5738-47ab-9797-7a9c90cd4bfb
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        execution | T1059
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2023-01-02
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_php_inline_command_execution.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% -r%')
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\php.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'php.exe')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Ping Hex IP

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1140, T1027 Author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects a ping command that uses a hex encoded IP address
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Ping Hex IP
-- Sigma ID:     1a0d4aba-7668-4365-9ce4-6d79ab088dfd
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1140, T1027
-- Author:       Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2018-03-23
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_ping_hex_ip.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unlikely, because no sane admin pings IP addresses in a hexadecimal form
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\ping.exe'
    AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (match(metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')], '0x[a-fA-F0-9]{8}')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unlikely, because no sane admin pings IP addresses in a hexadecimal form

PktMon.EXE Execution

Detects execution of PktMon, a tool that captures network packets.

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\pktmon.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'PktMon.exe'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;

Suspicious Plink Port Forwarding

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1572, T1021.001 Author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects suspicious Plink tunnel port forwarding to a local port
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Suspicious Plink Port Forwarding
-- Sigma ID:     48a61b29-389f-4032-b317-b30de6b95314
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1572, T1021.001
-- Author:       Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2021-01-19
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_plink_port_forwarding.yml
-- Unmapped:     Description
-- False Pos:    Administrative activity using a remote port forwarding to a local port
-- ============================================================
-- UNMAPPED_FIELD: Description

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (rawEventMsg = 'Command-line SSH, Telnet, and Rlogin client'
    AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% -R %'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Administrative activity using a remote port forwarding to a local port

Potential RDP Tunneling Via Plink

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1572 Author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Execution of plink to perform data exfiltration and tunneling
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potential RDP Tunneling Via Plink
-- Sigma ID:     f38ce0b9-5e97-4b47-a211-7dc8d8b871da
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1572
-- Author:       Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2022-08-04
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_plink_susp_tunneling.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\plink.exe'
    AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%:127.0.0.1:3389%'))
  OR ((procName LIKE '%\\plink.exe'
    AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%:3389%'))
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% -P 443%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% -P 22%')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Suspicious Powercfg Execution To Change Lock Screen Timeout

medium FSM Severity: 5 Author: frack113 Status: test
Detects suspicious execution of 'Powercfg.exe' to change lock screen timeout
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Suspicious Powercfg Execution To Change Lock Screen Timeout
-- Sigma ID:     f8d6a15e-4bc8-4c27-8e5d-2b10f0b73e5b
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        (none)
-- Author:       frack113
-- Date:         2022-11-18
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_powercfg_execution.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((procName LIKE '%\\powercfg.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'PowerCfg.exe'))
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%/setacvalueindex %' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%SCHEME\_CURRENT%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%SUB\_VIDEO%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%VIDEOCONLOCK%'))
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%-change %' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%-standby-timeout-%')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

AADInternals PowerShell Cmdlets Execution - ProccessCreation

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: execution, reconnaissance, discovery, impact Author: Austin Songer (@austinsonger), Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects ADDInternals Cmdlet execution. A tool for administering Azure AD and Office 365. Which can be abused by threat actors to attack Azure AD or Office 365.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        AADInternals PowerShell Cmdlets Execution - ProccessCreation
-- Sigma ID:     c86500e9-a645-4680-98d7-f882c70c1ea3
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        execution, reconnaissance, discovery, impact
-- Author:       Austin Songer (@austinsonger), Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2022-12-23
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_powershell_aadinternals_cmdlets_execution.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate use of the library for administrative activity
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%Add-AADInt%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%ConvertTo-AADInt%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%Disable-AADInt%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%Enable-AADInt%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%Export-AADInt%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%Find-AADInt%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%Get-AADInt%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%Grant-AADInt%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%Initialize-AADInt%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%Install-AADInt%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%Invoke-AADInt%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%Join-AADInt%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%New-AADInt%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%Open-AADInt%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%Read-AADInt%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%Register-AADInt%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%Remove-AADInt%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%Reset-AADInt%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%Resolve-AADInt%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%Restore-AADInt%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%Save-AADInt%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%Search-AADInt%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%Send-AADInt%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%Set-AADInt%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%Start-AADInt%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%Unprotect-AADInt%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%Update-AADInt%'))
  AND ((procName LIKE '%\\powershell.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\powershell\_ise.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\pwsh.exe'))
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] IN ('PowerShell.Exe', 'pwsh.dll'))))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Legitimate use of the library for administrative activity

Potential Active Directory Enumeration Using AD Module - ProcCreation

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: reconnaissance, discovery, impact Author: frack113 Status: test
