-- Title:        Direct Autorun Keys Modification
-- Sigma ID:     24357373-078f-44ed-9ac4-6d334a668a11
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        persistence | T1547.001
-- Author:       Victor Sergeev, Daniil Yugoslavskiy, oscd.community, Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2019-10-25
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_reg_direct_asep_registry_keys_modification.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate software automatically (mostly, during installation) sets up autorun keys for legitimate reasons.; Legitimate administrator sets up autorun keys for legitimate reasons.; Discord
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%add%')
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\software\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Run%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\software\\WOW6432Node\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Run%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\software\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Policies\\Explorer\\Run%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\software\\Microsoft\\Windows NT\\CurrentVersion\\Winlogon\\Userinit%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\software\\Microsoft\\Windows NT\\CurrentVersion\\Winlogon\\Shell%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\software\\Microsoft\\Windows NT\\CurrentVersion\\Windows%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\system\\CurrentControlSet\\Control\\SafeBoot\\AlternateShell%'))
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\reg.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'reg.exe')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Legitimate software automatically (mostly, during installation) sets up autorun keys for legitimate reasons.; Legitimate administrator sets up autorun keys for legitimate reasons.; Discord

Disabling Windows Defender WMI Autologger Session via Reg.exe

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1562.001 Author: Matt Anderson (Huntress) Status: experimental
Detects the use of reg.exe to disable the Event Tracing for Windows (ETW) Autologger session for Windows Defender API and Audit events. By setting the 'Start' value to '0' for the 'DefenderApiLogger' or 'DefenderAuditLogger' session, an attacker can prevent these critical security events from being logged, effectively blinding monitoring tools that rely on this data. This is a powerful defense evasion technique.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Disabling Windows Defender WMI Autologger Session via Reg.exe
-- Sigma ID:     a1b2c3d4-e5f6-a7b8-c9d0-e1f2a3b4c5d6
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       experimental
-- MITRE:        T1562.001
-- Author:       Matt Anderson (Huntress)
-- Date:         2025-07-09
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_reg_disable_defender_wmi_autologger.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Highly unlikely
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND 1=1
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Highly unlikely

Security Service Disabled Via Reg.EXE

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1562.001 Author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), John Lambert (idea), elhoim Status: test
Detects execution of "reg.exe" to disable security services such as Windows Defender.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Security Service Disabled Via Reg.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     5e95028c-5229-4214-afae-d653d573d0ec
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1562.001
-- Author:       Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), John Lambert (idea), elhoim
-- Date:         2021-07-14
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_reg_disable_sec_services.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unlikely
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%d 4%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%v Start%')
    AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\AppIDSvc%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\MsMpSvc%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\NisSrv%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\SecurityHealthService%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\Sense%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\UsoSvc%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\WdBoot%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\WdFilter%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\WdNisDrv%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\WdNisSvc%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\WinDefend%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\wscsvc%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\wuauserv%')))
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%reg%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%add%'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unlikely

Dumping of Sensitive Hives Via Reg.EXE

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1003.002, T1003.004, T1003.005 Author: Teymur Kheirkhabarov, Endgame, JHasenbusch, Daniil Yugoslavskiy, oscd.community, frack113 Status: test
Detects the usage of "reg.exe" in order to dump sensitive registry hives. This includes SAM, SYSTEM and SECURITY hives.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Dumping of Sensitive Hives Via Reg.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     fd877b94-9bb5-4191-bb25-d79cbd93c167
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1003.002, T1003.004, T1003.005
-- Author:       Teymur Kheirkhabarov, Endgame, JHasenbusch, Daniil Yugoslavskiy, oscd.community, frack113
-- Date:         2019-10-22
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_reg_dumping_sensitive_hives.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Dumping hives for legitimate purpouse i.e. backup or forensic investigation
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% save %' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% export %' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% ˢave %' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% eˣport %'))
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\system%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\sam%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\security%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\ˢystem%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\syˢtem%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\ˢyˢtem%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\ˢam%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\ˢecurity%'))
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%hklm%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%hk˪m%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%hkey\_local\_machine%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%hkey\_˪ocal\_machine%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%hkey\_loca˪\_machine%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%hkey\_˪oca˪\_machine%'))
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\reg.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'reg.exe')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Dumping hives for legitimate purpouse i.e. backup or forensic investigation

Windows Recall Feature Enabled Via Reg.EXE

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: collection, T1113 Author: Sajid Nawaz Khan Status: test
Detects the enabling of the Windows Recall feature via registry manipulation. Windows Recall can be enabled by deleting the existing "DisableAIDataAnalysis" value, or setting it to 0. Adversaries may enable Windows Recall as part of post-exploitation discovery and collection activities. This rule assumes that Recall is already explicitly disabled on the host, and subsequently enabled by the adversary.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Windows Recall Feature Enabled Via Reg.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     817f252c-5143-4dae-b418-48c3e9f63728
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        collection | T1113
-- Author:       Sajid Nawaz Khan
-- Date:         2024-06-02
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_reg_enable_windows_recall.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate use/activation of Windows Recall
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((procName LIKE '%\\reg.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'reg.exe'))
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%Microsoft\\Windows\\WindowsAI%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%DisableAIDataAnalysis%'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Legitimate use/activation of Windows Recall

Enumeration for Credentials in Registry

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: T1552.002 Author: frack113 Status: test
Adversaries may search the Registry on compromised systems for insecurely stored credentials. The Windows Registry stores configuration information that can be used by the system or other programs. Adversaries may query the Registry looking for credentials and passwords that have been stored for use by other programs or services
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Enumeration for Credentials in Registry
-- Sigma ID:     e0b0c2ab-3d52-46d9-8cb7-049dc775fbd1
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1552.002
-- Author:       frack113
-- Date:         2021-12-20
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_reg_enumeration_for_credentials_in_registry.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((procName LIKE '%\\reg.exe'
    AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% query %' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%/t %' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%REG\_SZ%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%/s%'))
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%/f %' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%HKLM%'))
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%/f %' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%HKCU%'))
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%HKCU\\Software\\SimonTatham\\PuTTY\\Sessions%')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Potential Suspicious Registry File Imported Via Reg.EXE

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: persistence, T1112 Author: frack113, Nasreddine Bencherchali Status: test
Detects the import of '.reg' files from suspicious paths using the 'reg.exe' utility
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potential Suspicious Registry File Imported Via Reg.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     62e0298b-e994-4189-bc87-bc699aa62d97
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        persistence | T1112
-- Author:       frack113, Nasreddine Bencherchali
-- Date:         2022-08-01
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_reg_import_from_suspicious_paths.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate import of keys
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% import %')
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\reg.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'reg.exe'))
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%C:\\Users\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\%temp\%%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\%tmp\%%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\%appdata\%%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%C:\\Windows\\Temp\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%C:\\ProgramData\\%')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Legitimate import of keys

RestrictedAdminMode Registry Value Tampering - ProcCreation

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: persistence, T1112 Author: frack113 Status: test
Detects changes to the "DisableRestrictedAdmin" registry value in order to disable or enable RestrictedAdmin mode. RestrictedAdmin mode prevents the transmission of reusable credentials to the remote system to which you connect using Remote Desktop. This prevents your credentials from being harvested during the initial connection process if the remote server has been compromise
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        RestrictedAdminMode Registry Value Tampering - ProcCreation
-- Sigma ID:     28ac00d6-22d9-4a3c-927f-bbd770104573
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        persistence | T1112
-- Author:       frack113
-- Date:         2023-01-13
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_reg_lsa_disable_restricted_admin.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\System\\CurrentControlSet\\Control\\Lsa%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%DisableRestrictedAdmin%')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

LSA PPL Protection Disabled Via Reg.EXE

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1562.010 Author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects the usage of the "reg.exe" utility to disable PPL protection on the LSA process
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        LSA PPL Protection Disabled Via Reg.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     8c0eca51-0f88-4db2-9183-fdfb10c703f9
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1562.010
-- Author:       Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2022-03-22
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_reg_lsa_ppl_protection_disabled.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unlikely
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%SYSTEM\\CurrentControlSet\\Control\\Lsa%')
    AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% add %' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% /d 0%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% /v RunAsPPL %'))
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\reg.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'reg.exe')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unlikely

Suspicious Query of MachineGUID

low FSM Severity: 3 MITRE: discovery, T1082 Author: frack113 Status: test
Use of reg to get MachineGuid information

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\reg.exe'
    AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Cryptography%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%/v %' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%MachineGuid%'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC

Modify Group Policy Settings

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: T1484.001 Author: frack113 Status: test
Detect malicious GPO modifications can be used to implement many other malicious behaviors.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Modify Group Policy Settings
-- Sigma ID:     ada4b0c4-758b-46ac-9033-9004613a150d
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1484.001
-- Author:       frack113
-- Date:         2022-08-19
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_reg_modify_group_policy_settings.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate use
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%GroupPolicyRefreshTimeDC%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%GroupPolicyRefreshTimeOffsetDC%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%GroupPolicyRefreshTime%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%GroupPolicyRefreshTimeOffset%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%EnableSmartScreen%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%ShellSmartScreenLevel%'))
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\SOFTWARE\\Policies\\Microsoft\\Windows\\System%')
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\reg.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'reg.exe')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Legitimate use

Enable LM Hash Storage - ProcCreation

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: persistence, T1112 Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects changes to the "NoLMHash" registry value in order to allow Windows to store LM Hashes. By setting this registry value to "0" (DWORD), Windows will be allowed to store a LAN manager hash of your password in Active Directory and local SAM databases.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Enable LM Hash Storage - ProcCreation
-- Sigma ID:     98dedfdd-8333-49d4-9f23-d7018cccae53
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        persistence | T1112
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2023-12-15
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_reg_nolmhash.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\System\\CurrentControlSet\\Control\\Lsa%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%NoLMHash%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% 0%')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Potential Configuration And Service Reconnaissance Via Reg.EXE

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: discovery, T1012, T1007 Author: Timur Zinniatullin, oscd.community Status: test
Detects the usage of "reg.exe" in order to query reconnaissance information from the registry. Adversaries may interact with the Windows registry to gather information about credentials, the system, configuration, and installed software.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potential Configuration And Service Reconnaissance Via Reg.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     970007b7-ce32-49d0-a4a4-fbef016950bd
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        discovery | T1012, T1007
-- Author:       Timur Zinniatullin, oscd.community
-- Date:         2019-10-21
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_reg_query_registry.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Discord
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%query%')
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\reg.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'reg.exe'))
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%currentVersion\\windows%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%winlogon\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%currentVersion\\shellServiceObjectDelayLoad%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%currentVersion\\run%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%currentVersion\\policies\\explorer\\run%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%currentcontrolset\\services%')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Discord

Potential Tampering With RDP Related Registry Keys Via Reg.EXE

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: persistence, T1021.001, T1112 Author: pH-T (Nextron Systems), @Kostastsale, TheDFIRReport Status: test
Detects the execution of "reg.exe" for enabling/disabling the RDP service on the host by tampering with the 'CurrentControlSet\Control\Terminal Server' values
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potential Tampering With RDP Related Registry Keys Via Reg.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     0d5675be-bc88-4172-86d3-1e96a4476536
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        persistence | T1021.001, T1112
-- Author:       pH-T (Nextron Systems), @Kostastsale, TheDFIRReport
-- Date:         2022-02-12
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_reg_rdp_keys_tamper.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND 1=1
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Suspicious ScreenSave Change by Reg.exe

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: persistence, T1546.002 Author: frack113 Status: test
Adversaries may establish persistence by executing malicious content triggered by user inactivity. Screensavers are programs that execute after a configurable time of user inactivity and consist of Portable Executable (PE) files with a .scr file extension
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Suspicious ScreenSave Change by Reg.exe
-- Sigma ID:     0fc35fc3-efe6-4898-8a37-0b233339524f
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        persistence | T1546.002
-- Author:       frack113
-- Date:         2021-08-19
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_reg_screensaver.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    GPO
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\reg.exe'
    AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%HKEY\_CURRENT\_USER\\Control Panel\\Desktop%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%HKCU\\Control Panel\\Desktop%')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: GPO

Changing Existing Service ImagePath Value Via Reg.EXE

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: persistence, T1574.011 Author: frack113 Status: test
Adversaries may execute their own malicious payloads by hijacking the Registry entries used by services. Adversaries may use flaws in the permissions for registry to redirect from the originally specified executable to one that they control, in order to launch their own code at Service start. Windows stores local service configuration information in the Registry under HKLM\SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Changing Existing Service ImagePath Value Via Reg.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     9b0b7ac3-6223-47aa-a3fd-e8f211e637db
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        persistence | T1574.011
-- Author:       frack113
-- Date:         2021-12-30
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_reg_service_imagepath_change.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================
-- UNSUPPORTED_MODIFIER: contains|windash

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((procName LIKE '%\\reg.exe'
    AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%add %' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%SYSTEM\\CurrentControlSet\\Services\\%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% ImagePath %'))
  AND rawEventMsg LIKE '% -d %')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Detected Windows Software Discovery

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: discovery, T1518 Author: Nikita Nazarov, oscd.community Status: test
Adversaries may attempt to enumerate software for a variety of reasons, such as figuring out what security measures are present or if the compromised system has a version of software that is vulnerable.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Detected Windows Software Discovery
-- Sigma ID:     e13f668e-7f95-443d-98d2-1816a7648a7b
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        discovery | T1518
-- Author:       Nikita Nazarov, oscd.community
-- Date:         2020-10-16
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_reg_software_discovery.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate administration activities
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\reg.exe'
    AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%query%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\software\\%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%/v%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%svcversion%'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Legitimate administration activities

Reg Add Suspicious Paths

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: persistence, T1112, T1562.001 Author: frack113, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects when an adversary uses the reg.exe utility to add or modify new keys or subkeys
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Reg Add Suspicious Paths
-- Sigma ID:     b7e2a8d4-74bb-4b78-adc9-3f92af2d4829
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        persistence | T1112, T1562.001
-- Author:       frack113, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2022-08-19
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_reg_susp_paths.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Rare legitimate add to registry via cli (to these locations)
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\AppDataLow\\Software\\Microsoft\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\Policies\\Microsoft\\Windows\\OOBE%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\Policies\\Microsoft\\Windows NT\\CurrentVersion\\Winlogon%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Windows NT\\Currentversion\\Winlogon%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\CurrentControlSet\\Control\\SecurityProviders\\WDigest%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\Microsoft\\Windows Defender\\%'))
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\reg.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'reg.exe')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Rare legitimate add to registry via cli (to these locations)

System Language Discovery via Reg.Exe

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: discovery, T1614.001 Author: Marco Pedrinazzi (@pedrinazziM) (InTheCyber) Status: experimental
Detects the usage of Reg.Exe to query system language settings. Attackers may discover the system language to determine the geographic location of victims, customize payloads for specific regions, or avoid targeting certain locales to evade detection.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        System Language Discovery via Reg.Exe
-- Sigma ID:     c43a5405-e8e1-4221-9ac9-dbe3fa14e886
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       experimental
-- MITRE:        discovery | T1614.001
-- Author:       Marco Pedrinazzi (@pedrinazziM) (InTheCyber)
-- Date:         2026-01-09
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_reg_system_language_discovery.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%query%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%Control\\Nls\\Language%')
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\reg.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'reg.exe')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Disabled Volume Snapshots

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1562.001 Author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects commands that temporarily turn off Volume Snapshots
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Disabled Volume Snapshots
-- Sigma ID:     dee4af55-1f22-4e1d-a9d2-4bdc7ecb472a
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1562.001
-- Author:       Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2021-01-28
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_reg_volsnap_disable.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate administration
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\Services\\VSS\\Diag%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%/d Disabled%')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Legitimate administration

Suspicious Windows Defender Registry Key Tampering Via Reg.EXE

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1562.001 Author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects the usage of "reg.exe" to tamper with different Windows Defender registry keys in order to disable some important features related to protection and detection
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Suspicious Windows Defender Registry Key Tampering Via Reg.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     452bce90-6fb0-43cc-97a5-affc283139b3
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1562.001
-- Author:       Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2022-03-22
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_reg_windows_defender_tamper.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Rare legitimate use by administrators to test software (should always be investigated)
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND 1=1
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Rare legitimate use by administrators to test software (should always be investigated)

Write Protect For Storage Disabled

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: T1562 Author: Sreeman Status: test
Detects applications trying to modify the registry in order to disable any write-protect property for storage devices. This could be a precursor to a ransomware attack and has been an observed technique used by cypherpunk group.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Write Protect For Storage Disabled
-- Sigma ID:     75f7a0e2-7154-4c4d-9eae-5cdb4e0a5c13
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1562
-- Author:       Sreeman
-- Date:         2021-06-11
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_reg_write_protect_for_storage_disabled.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\System\\CurrentControlSet\\Control%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%Write Protection%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%0%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%storage%')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

RegAsm.EXE Execution Without CommandLine Flags or Files

low FSM Severity: 3 MITRE: T1218.009 Author: frack113 Status: experimental
Detects the execution of "RegAsm.exe" without a commandline flag or file, which might indicate potential process injection activity. Usually "RegAsm.exe" should point to a dedicated DLL file or call the help with the "/?" flag.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        RegAsm.EXE Execution Without CommandLine Flags or Files
-- Sigma ID:     651f87f7-12db-47f9-84c5-f27b081b94b6
-- Level:        low  |  FSM Severity: 3
-- Status:       experimental
-- MITRE:        T1218.009
-- Author:       frack113
-- Date:         2025-06-04
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_regasm_no_flag_or_dll_execution.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate use of Regasm by developers.
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%RegAsm' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%RegAsm.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%RegAsm.exe"' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%RegAsm.exe''))
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\RegAsm.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'RegAsm.exe')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Legitimate use of Regasm by developers.

Potentially Suspicious Execution Of Regasm/Regsvcs With Uncommon Extension

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: T1218.009 Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects potentially suspicious execution of the Regasm/Regsvcs utilities with an uncommon extension.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potentially Suspicious Execution Of Regasm/Regsvcs With Uncommon Extension
-- Sigma ID:     e9f8f8cc-07cc-4e81-b724-f387db9175e4
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1218.009
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2023-02-13
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_regasm_regsvcs_uncommon_extension_execution.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.dat%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.gif%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.jpeg%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.jpg%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.png%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.txt%'))
  AND ((procName LIKE '%\\Regsvcs.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\Regasm.exe'))
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] IN ('RegSvcs.exe', 'RegAsm.exe'))))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Potentially Suspicious Execution Of Regasm/Regsvcs From Uncommon Location

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: T1218.009 Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects potentially suspicious execution of the Regasm/Regsvcs utilities from a potentially suspicious location
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potentially Suspicious Execution Of Regasm/Regsvcs From Uncommon Location
-- Sigma ID:     cc368ed0-2411-45dc-a222-510ace303cb2
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1218.009
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2022-08-25
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_regasm_regsvcs_uncommon_location_execution.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\Microsoft\\Windows\\Start Menu\\Programs\\Startup\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\PerfLogs\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\Users\\Public\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\Windows\\Temp\\%'))
  AND ((procName LIKE '%\\Regsvcs.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\Regasm.exe'))
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] IN ('RegSvcs.exe', 'RegAsm.exe'))))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Exports Critical Registry Keys To a File

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: exfiltration, discovery, T1012 Author: Oddvar Moe, Sander Wiebing, oscd.community Status: test
Detects the export of a crital Registry key to a file.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Exports Critical Registry Keys To a File
-- Sigma ID:     82880171-b475-4201-b811-e9c826cd5eaa
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        exfiltration, discovery | T1012
-- Author:       Oddvar Moe, Sander Wiebing, oscd.community
-- Date:         2020-10-12
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_regedit_export_critical_keys.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Dumping hives for legitimate purpouse i.e. backup or forensic investigation
-- ============================================================
-- UNSUPPORTED_MODIFIER: contains|windash

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (rawEventMsg LIKE '% -E %'
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%hklm%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%hkey\_local\_machine%'))
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\system' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\sam' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\security'))
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\regedit.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'REGEDIT.EXE')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Dumping hives for legitimate purpouse i.e. backup or forensic investigation

Exports Registry Key To a File

low FSM Severity: 3 MITRE: exfiltration, discovery, T1012 Author: Oddvar Moe, Sander Wiebing, oscd.community Status: test
Detects the export of the target Registry key to a file.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Exports Registry Key To a File
-- Sigma ID:     f0e53e89-8d22-46ea-9db5-9d4796ee2f8a
-- Level:        low  |  FSM Severity: 3
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        exfiltration, discovery | T1012
-- Author:       Oddvar Moe, Sander Wiebing, oscd.community
-- Date:         2020-10-07
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_regedit_export_keys.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate export of keys
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND 1=1
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Legitimate export of keys

Imports Registry Key From a File

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: persistence, T1112 Author: Oddvar Moe, Sander Wiebing, oscd.community Status: test
Detects the import of the specified file to the registry with regedit.exe.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Imports Registry Key From a File
-- Sigma ID:     73bba97f-a82d-42ce-b315-9182e76c57b1
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        persistence | T1112
-- Author:       Oddvar Moe, Sander Wiebing, oscd.community
-- Date:         2020-10-07
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_regedit_import_keys.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate import of keys; Evernote
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND 1=1
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Legitimate import of keys; Evernote

Imports Registry Key From an ADS

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: persistence, T1112 Author: Oddvar Moe, Sander Wiebing, oscd.community Status: test
Detects the import of a alternate datastream to the registry with regedit.exe.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Imports Registry Key From an ADS
-- Sigma ID:     0b80ade5-6997-4b1d-99a1-71701778ea61
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        persistence | T1112
-- Author:       Oddvar Moe, Sander Wiebing, oscd.community
-- Date:         2020-10-12
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_regedit_import_keys_ads.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================
-- UNSUPPORTED_MODIFIER: contains|windash

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND NOT ((rawEventMsg LIKE '% -e %' OR rawEventMsg LIKE '% -a %' OR rawEventMsg LIKE '% -c %'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Regedit as Trusted Installer

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1548 Author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects a regedit started with TrustedInstaller privileges or by ProcessHacker.exe
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Regedit as Trusted Installer
-- Sigma ID:     883835a7-df45-43e4-bf1d-4268768afda4
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1548
-- Author:       Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2021-05-27
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_regedit_trustedinstaller.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unlikely
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] AS parentImage,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\regedit.exe'
    AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\TrustedInstaller.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\ProcessHacker.exe')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unlikely

Suspicious Registry Modification From ADS Via Regini.EXE

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: persistence, T1112 Author: Eli Salem, Sander Wiebing, oscd.community Status: test
Detects the import of an alternate data stream with regini.exe, regini.exe can be used to modify registry keys.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Suspicious Registry Modification From ADS Via Regini.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     77946e79-97f1-45a2-84b4-f37b5c0d8682
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        persistence | T1112
-- Author:       Eli Salem, Sander Wiebing, oscd.community
-- Date:         2020-10-12
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_regini_ads.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((procName LIKE '%\\regini.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'REGINI.EXE'))
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (match(metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')], ':[^ \\]')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Registry Modification Via Regini.EXE

low FSM Severity: 3 MITRE: persistence, T1112 Author: Eli Salem, Sander Wiebing, oscd.community Status: test
Detects the execution of regini.exe which can be used to modify registry keys, the changes are imported from one or more text files.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Registry Modification Via Regini.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     5f60740a-f57b-4e76-82a1-15b6ff2cb134
-- Level:        low  |  FSM Severity: 3
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        persistence | T1112
-- Author:       Eli Salem, Sander Wiebing, oscd.community
-- Date:         2020-10-08
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_regini_execution.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate modification of keys
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((procName LIKE '%\\regini.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'REGINI.EXE'))
  AND NOT (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (match(metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')], ':[^ \\]'))))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Legitimate modification of keys

DLL Execution Via Register-cimprovider.exe

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: persistence, T1574 Author: Ivan Dyachkov, Yulia Fomina, oscd.community Status: test
Detects using register-cimprovider.exe to execute arbitrary dll file.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        DLL Execution Via Register-cimprovider.exe
-- Sigma ID:     a2910908-e86f-4687-aeba-76a5f996e652
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        persistence | T1574
-- Author:       Ivan Dyachkov, Yulia Fomina, oscd.community
-- Date:         2020-10-07
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_registry_cimprovider_dll_load.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\register-cimprovider.exe'
    AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%-path%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%dll%'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Enumeration for 3rd Party Creds From CLI

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: T1552.002 Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects processes that query known 3rd party registry keys that holds credentials via commandline
