-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Enumeration for 3rd Party Creds From CLI
-- Sigma ID:     87a476dc-0079-4583-a985-dee7a20a03de
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1552.002
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2022-06-20
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_registry_enumeration_for_credentials_cli.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\Software\\Aerofox\\Foxmail\\V3.1%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\Software\\Aerofox\\FoxmailPreview%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\Software\\DownloadManager\\Passwords%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\Software\\FTPWare\\COREFTP\\Sites%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\Software\\IncrediMail\\Identities%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\Software\\Martin Prikryl\\WinSCP 2\\Sessions%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\Software\\Mobatek\\MobaXterm\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\Software\\OpenSSH\\Agent\\Keys%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\Software\\OpenVPN-GUI\\configs%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\Software\\ORL\\WinVNC3\\Password%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\Software\\Qualcomm\\Eudora\\CommandLine%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\Software\\RealVNC\\WinVNC4%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\Software\\RimArts\\B2\\Settings%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\Software\\SimonTatham\\PuTTY\\Sessions%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\Software\\SimonTatham\\PuTTY\\SshHostKeys\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\Software\\Sota\\FFFTP%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\Software\\TightVNC\\Server%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\Software\\WOW6432Node\\Radmin\\v3.0\\Server\\Parameters\\Radmin%'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Registry Export of Third-Party Credentials

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1552.002 Author: Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems) Status: experimental
Detects the use of reg.exe to export registry paths associated with third-party credentials. Credential stealers have been known to use this technique to extract sensitive information from the registry.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Registry Export of Third-Party Credentials
-- Sigma ID:     cc1abf27-78a3-4ac5-a51c-f3070b1d8e40
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       experimental
-- MITRE:        T1552.002
-- Author:       Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2025-05-22
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_registry_export_of_thirdparty_creds.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\Software\\Aerofox\\Foxmail\\V3.1%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\Software\\Aerofox\\FoxmailPreview%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\Software\\DownloadManager\\Passwords%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\Software\\FTPWare\\COREFTP\\Sites%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\Software\\IncrediMail\\Identities%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\Software\\Martin Prikryl\\WinSCP 2\\Sessions%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\Software\\Mobatek\\MobaXterm%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\Software\\OpenSSH\\Agent\\Keys%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\Software\\OpenVPN-GUI\\configs%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\Software\\ORL\\WinVNC3\\Password%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\Software\\Qualcomm\\Eudora\\CommandLine%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\Software\\RealVNC\\WinVNC4%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\Software\\RimArts\\B2\\Settings%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\Software\\SimonTatham\\PuTTY\\Sessions%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\Software\\SimonTatham\\PuTTY\\SshHostKeys%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\Software\\Sota\\FFFTP%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\Software\\TightVNC\\Server%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\Software\\WOW6432Node\\Radmin\\v3.0\\Server\\Parameters\\Radmin%'))
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%save%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%export%'))
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\reg.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'reg.exe')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

IE ZoneMap Setting Downgraded To MyComputer Zone For HTTP Protocols Via CLI

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: execution Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects changes to Internet Explorer's (IE / Windows Internet properties) ZoneMap configuration of the "HTTP" and "HTTPS" protocols to point to the "My Computer" zone. This allows downloaded files from the Internet to be granted the same level of trust as files stored locally.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        IE ZoneMap Setting Downgraded To MyComputer Zone For HTTP Protocols Via CLI
-- Sigma ID:     10344bb3-7f65-46c2-b915-2d00d47be5b0
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        execution
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2023-09-05
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_registry_ie_security_zone_protocol_defaults_downgrade.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Internet Settings\\ZoneMap\\ProtocolDefaults%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%http%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% 0%')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Suspicious Debugger Registration Cmdline

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: persistence, T1546.008 Author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), oscd.community, Jonhnathan Ribeiro Status: test
Detects the registration of a debugger for a program that is available in the logon screen (sticky key backdoor).
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Suspicious Debugger Registration Cmdline
-- Sigma ID:     ae215552-081e-44c7-805f-be16f975c8a2
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        persistence | T1546.008
-- Author:       Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), oscd.community, Jonhnathan Ribeiro
-- Date:         2019-09-06
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_registry_install_reg_debugger_backdoor.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\CurrentVersion\\Image File Execution Options\\%')
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%sethc.exe%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%utilman.exe%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%osk.exe%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%magnify.exe%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%narrator.exe%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%displayswitch.exe%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%atbroker.exe%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%HelpPane.exe%')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Potential Persistence Via Logon Scripts - CommandLine

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: persistence, T1037.001 Author: Tom Ueltschi (@c_APT_ure) Status: test
Detects the addition of a new LogonScript to the registry value "UserInitMprLogonScript" for potential persistence
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potential Persistence Via Logon Scripts - CommandLine
-- Sigma ID:     21d856f9-9281-4ded-9377-51a1a6e2a432
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        persistence | T1037.001
-- Author:       Tom Ueltschi (@c_APT_ure)
-- Date:         2019-01-12
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_registry_logon_script.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate addition of Logon Scripts via the command line by administrators or third party tools
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%UserInitMprLogonScript%')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Legitimate addition of Logon Scripts via the command line by administrators or third party tools

Potential Credential Dumping Attempt Using New NetworkProvider - CLI

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1003 Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects when an attacker tries to add a new network provider in order to dump clear text credentials, similar to how the NPPSpy tool does it
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potential Credential Dumping Attempt Using New NetworkProvider - CLI
-- Sigma ID:     baef1ec6-2ca9-47a3-97cc-4cf2bda10b77
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1003
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2022-08-23
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_registry_new_network_provider.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Other legitimate network providers used and not filtred in this rule
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\System\\CurrentControlSet\\Services\\%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\NetworkProvider%')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Other legitimate network providers used and not filtred in this rule

Python Function Execution Security Warning Disabled In Excel

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1562.001 Author: @Kostastsale Status: test
Detects changes to the registry value "PythonFunctionWarnings" that would prevent any warnings or alerts from showing when Python functions are about to be executed. Threat actors could run malicious code through the new Microsoft Excel feature that allows Python to run within the spreadsheet.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Python Function Execution Security Warning Disabled In Excel
-- Sigma ID:     023c654f-8f16-44d9-bb2b-00ff36a62af9
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1562.001
-- Author:       @Kostastsale
-- Date:         2023-08-22
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_registry_office_disable_python_security_warnings.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\Microsoft\\Office\\%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\Excel\\Security%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%PythonFunctionWarnings%')
    AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% 0%'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Potential Privilege Escalation via Service Permissions Weakness

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: persistence, T1574.011 Author: Teymur Kheirkhabarov Status: test
Detect modification of services configuration (ImagePath, FailureCommand and ServiceDLL) in registry by processes with Medium integrity level
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potential Privilege Escalation via Service Permissions Weakness
-- Sigma ID:     0f9c21f1-6a73-4b0e-9809-cb562cb8d981
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        persistence | T1574.011
-- Author:       Teymur Kheirkhabarov
-- Date:         2019-10-26
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_registry_privilege_escalation_via_service_key.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'integrityLevel')] AS integrityLevel,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'integrityLevel') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'integrityLevel')] IN ('Medium', 'S-1-16-8192'))
    AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%ControlSet%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%services%')
    AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\ImagePath%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\FailureCommand%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\ServiceDll%')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Potential Provisioning Registry Key Abuse For Binary Proxy Execution

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1218 Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel Status: test
Detects potential abuse of the provisioning registry key for indirect command execution through "Provlaunch.exe".
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potential Provisioning Registry Key Abuse For Binary Proxy Execution
-- Sigma ID:     2a4b3e61-9d22-4e4a-b60f-6e8f0cde6f25
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1218
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel
-- Date:         2023-08-08
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_registry_provlaunch_provisioning_command.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Provisioning\\Commands\\%')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Potential PowerShell Execution Policy Tampering - ProcCreation

high FSM Severity: 7 Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects changes to the PowerShell execution policy registry key in order to bypass signing requirements for script execution from the CommandLine
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potential PowerShell Execution Policy Tampering - ProcCreation
-- Sigma ID:     cf2e938e-9a3e-4fe8-a347-411642b28a9f
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        (none)
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2023-01-11
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_registry_set_unsecure_powershell_policy.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\ShellIds\\Microsoft.PowerShell\\ExecutionPolicy%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\Policies\\Microsoft\\Windows\\PowerShell\\ExecutionPolicy%'))
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%Bypass%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%RemoteSigned%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%Unrestricted%')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Hiding User Account Via SpecialAccounts Registry Key - CommandLine

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: T1564.002 Author: @Kostastsale, TheDFIRReport Status: test
Detects changes to the registry key "HKLM\Software\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Winlogon\SpecialAccounts\Userlist" where the value is set to "0" in order to hide user account from being listed on the logon screen.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Hiding User Account Via SpecialAccounts Registry Key - CommandLine
-- Sigma ID:     9ec9fb1b-e059-4489-9642-f270c207923d
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1564.002
-- Author:       @Kostastsale, TheDFIRReport
-- Date:         2022-05-14
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_registry_special_accounts_hide_user.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    System administrator activities
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\reg.exe'
    AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\SOFTWARE\\Microsoft\\Windows NT\\CurrentVersion\\Winlogon\\SpecialAccounts\\UserList%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%add%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%/v%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%/d 0%'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: System administrator activities

Persistence Via TypedPaths - CommandLine

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: persistence Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects modification addition to the 'TypedPaths' key in the user or admin registry via the commandline. Which might indicate persistence attempt
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Persistence Via TypedPaths - CommandLine
-- Sigma ID:     ec88289a-7e1a-4cc3-8d18-bd1f60e4b9ba
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        persistence
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2022-08-22
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_registry_typed_paths_persistence.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\Software\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Explorer\\TypedPaths%')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Potential Regsvr32 Commandline Flag Anomaly

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: T1218.010 Author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects a potential command line flag anomaly related to "regsvr32" in which the "/i" flag is used without the "/n" which should be uncommon.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potential Regsvr32 Commandline Flag Anomaly
-- Sigma ID:     b236190c-1c61-41e9-84b3-3fe03f6d76b0
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1218.010
-- Author:       Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2019-07-13
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_regsvr32_flags_anomaly.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Administrator typo might cause some false positives
-- ============================================================
-- UNSUPPORTED_MODIFIER: contains|windash

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\regsvr32.exe'
    AND rawEventMsg LIKE '% -i:%')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Administrator typo might cause some false positives

Potentially Suspicious Regsvr32 HTTP IP Pattern

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1218.010 Author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects regsvr32 execution to download and install DLLs located remotely where the address is an IP address.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potentially Suspicious Regsvr32 HTTP IP Pattern
-- Sigma ID:     2dd2c217-bf68-437a-b57c-fe9fd01d5de8
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1218.010
-- Author:       Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2022-01-11
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_regsvr32_http_ip_pattern.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    FQDNs that start with a number such as "7-Zip"
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((procName LIKE '%\\regsvr32.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'REGSVR32.EXE'))
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% /i:http://1%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% /i:http://2%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% /i:http://3%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% /i:http://4%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% /i:http://5%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% /i:http://6%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% /i:http://7%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% /i:http://8%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% /i:http://9%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% /i:https://1%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% /i:https://2%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% /i:https://3%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% /i:https://4%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% /i:https://5%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% /i:https://6%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% /i:https://7%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% /i:https://8%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% /i:https://9%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% -i:http://1%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% -i:http://2%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% -i:http://3%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% -i:http://4%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% -i:http://5%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% -i:http://6%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% -i:http://7%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% -i:http://8%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% -i:http://9%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% -i:https://1%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% -i:https://2%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% -i:https://3%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% -i:https://4%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% -i:https://5%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% -i:https://6%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% -i:https://7%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% -i:https://8%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% -i:https://9%')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: FQDNs that start with a number such as "7-Zip"

Potentially Suspicious Regsvr32 HTTP/FTP Pattern

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: T1218.010 Author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects regsvr32 execution to download/install/register new DLLs that are hosted on Web or FTP servers.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potentially Suspicious Regsvr32 HTTP/FTP Pattern
-- Sigma ID:     867356ee-9352-41c9-a8f2-1be690d78216
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1218.010
-- Author:       Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2023-05-24
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_regsvr32_network_pattern.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% /i%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% -i%'))
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\regsvr32.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'REGSVR32.EXE'))
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%ftp%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%http%')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Suspicious Regsvr32 Execution From Remote Share

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1218.010 Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects REGSVR32.exe to execute DLL hosted on remote shares
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Suspicious Regsvr32 Execution From Remote Share
-- Sigma ID:     88a87a10-384b-4ad7-8871-2f9bf9259ce5
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1218.010
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2022-10-31
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_regsvr32_remote_share.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% \\\\\\\\%')
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\regsvr32.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = '\REGSVR32.EXE')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Potentially Suspicious Child Process Of Regsvr32

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1218.010 Author: elhoim, Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects potentially suspicious child processes of "regsvr32.exe".
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potentially Suspicious Child Process Of Regsvr32
-- Sigma ID:     6f0947a4-1c5e-4e0d-8ac7-53159b8f23ca
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1218.010
-- Author:       elhoim, Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2022-05-05
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_regsvr32_susp_child_process.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unlikely, but can rarely occur. Apply additional filters accordingly.
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] AS parentImage,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\regsvr32.exe')
    AND (procName LIKE '%\\calc.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\cscript.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\explorer.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\mshta.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\net.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\net1.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\nltest.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\notepad.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\powershell.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\pwsh.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\reg.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\schtasks.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\werfault.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\wscript.exe'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unlikely, but can rarely occur. Apply additional filters accordingly.

Regsvr32 Execution From Potential Suspicious Location

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: T1218.010 Author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects execution of regsvr32 where the DLL is located in a potentially suspicious location.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Regsvr32 Execution From Potential Suspicious Location
-- Sigma ID:     9525dc73-0327-438c-8c04-13c0e037e9da
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1218.010
-- Author:       Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2023-05-26
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_regsvr32_susp_exec_path_1.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Some installers might execute "regsvr32" with DLLs located in %TEMP% or in %PROGRAMDATA%. Apply additional filters if necessary.
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%:\\ProgramData\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%:\\Temp\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%:\\Users\\Public\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%:\\Windows\\Temp\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\AppData\\Roaming\\%'))
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\regsvr32.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'REGSVR32.EXE')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Some installers might execute "regsvr32" with DLLs located in %TEMP% or in %PROGRAMDATA%. Apply additional filters if necessary.

Regsvr32 Execution From Highly Suspicious Location

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1218.010 Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects execution of regsvr32 where the DLL is located in a highly suspicious locations
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Regsvr32 Execution From Highly Suspicious Location
-- Sigma ID:     327ff235-94eb-4f06-b9de-aaee571324be
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1218.010
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2023-05-26
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_regsvr32_susp_exec_path_2.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unlikely
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((procName LIKE '%\\regsvr32.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'REGSVR32.EXE'))
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%:\\PerfLogs\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%:\\Temp\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\Windows\\Registration\\CRMLog%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\Windows\\System32\\com\\dmp\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\Windows\\System32\\FxsTmp\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\Windows\\System32\\Microsoft\\Crypto\\RSA\\MachineKeys\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\Windows\\System32\\spool\\drivers\\color\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\Windows\\System32\\spool\\PRINTERS\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\Windows\\System32\\spool\\SERVERS\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\Windows\\System32\\Tasks\_Migrated\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\Windows\\System32\\Tasks\\Microsoft\\Windows\\SyncCenter\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\Windows\\SysWOW64\\com\\dmp\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\Windows\\SysWOW64\\FxsTmp\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\Windows\\SysWOW64\\Tasks\\Microsoft\\Windows\\PLA\\System\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\Windows\\SysWOW64\\Tasks\\Microsoft\\Windows\\SyncCenter\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\Windows\\Tasks\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\Windows\\Tracing\\%')))
  OR ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% "C:\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% C:\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% 'C:\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%D:\\%'))
  AND NOT ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%C:\\Program Files (x86)\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%C:\\Program Files\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%C:\\ProgramData\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%C:\\Users\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% C:\\Windows\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% "C:\\Windows\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% 'C:\\Windows\\%'))))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unlikely

Regsvr32 DLL Execution With Suspicious File Extension

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1218.010 Author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), frack113 Status: test
Detects the execution of REGSVR32.exe with DLL files masquerading as other files
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Regsvr32 DLL Execution With Suspicious File Extension
-- Sigma ID:     089fc3d2-71e8-4763-a8a5-c97fbb0a403e
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1218.010
-- Author:       Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), frack113
-- Date:         2021-11-29
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_regsvr32_susp_extensions.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unlikely
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.bin' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.bmp' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.cr2' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.dat' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.eps' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.gif' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.ico' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.jpeg' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.jpg' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.log' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.nef' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.orf' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.png' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.raw' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.rtf' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.sr2' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.temp' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.tif' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.tiff' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.tmp' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.txt'))
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\regsvr32.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'REGSVR32.EXE')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unlikely

Scripting/CommandLine Process Spawned Regsvr32

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: T1218.010 Author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects various command line and scripting engines/processes such as "PowerShell", "Wscript", "Cmd", etc. spawning a "regsvr32" instance.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Scripting/CommandLine Process Spawned Regsvr32
-- Sigma ID:     ab37a6ec-6068-432b-a64e-2c7bf95b1d22
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1218.010
-- Author:       Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2023-05-26
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_regsvr32_susp_parent.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate ".bat", ".hta", ".ps1" or ".vbs" scripts leverage legitimately often. Apply additional filter and exclusions as necessary; Some legitimate Windows services
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] AS parentImage,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\cmd.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\cscript.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\mshta.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\powershell\_ise.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\powershell.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\pwsh.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\wscript.exe'))
    AND procName LIKE '%\\regsvr32.exe')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Legitimate ".bat", ".hta", ".ps1" or ".vbs" scripts leverage legitimately often. Apply additional filter and exclusions as necessary; Some legitimate Windows services

Regsvr32 DLL Execution With Uncommon Extension

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: persistence, execution, T1574 Author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects a "regsvr32" execution where the DLL doesn't contain a common file extension.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Regsvr32 DLL Execution With Uncommon Extension
-- Sigma ID:     50919691-7302-437f-8e10-1fe088afa145
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        persistence, execution | T1574
-- Author:       Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2019-07-17
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_regsvr32_uncommon_extension.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Other legitimate extensions currently not in the list either from third party or specific Windows components.
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\regsvr32.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'REGSVR32.EXE'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Other legitimate extensions currently not in the list either from third party or specific Windows components.

Remote Access Tool - AnyDesk Execution

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: T1219.002 Author: frack113 Status: test
An adversary may use legitimate desktop support and remote access software, such as Team Viewer, Go2Assist, LogMein, AmmyyAdmin, etc, to establish an interactive command and control channel to target systems within networks. These services are commonly used as legitimate technical support software, and may be allowed by application control within a target environment. Remote access tools like VNC, Ammyy, and Teamviewer are used frequently when compared with other legitimate software commonly used by adversaries. (Citation: Symantec Living off the Land)
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Remote Access Tool - AnyDesk Execution
-- Sigma ID:     b52e84a3-029e-4529-b09b-71d19dd27e94
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1219.002
-- Author:       frack113
-- Date:         2022-02-11
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_remote_access_tools_anydesk.yml
-- Unmapped:     Description
-- False Pos:    Legitimate use
-- ============================================================
-- UNMAPPED_FIELD: Description

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'product')] AS product,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'company')] AS company,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((procName LIKE '%\\AnyDesk.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\AnyDeskMSI.exe'))
  OR (rawEventMsg = 'AnyDesk')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'product') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'product')] = 'AnyDesk'))
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'company') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'company')] = 'AnyDesk Software GmbH'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Legitimate use

Remote Access Tool - AnyDesk Piped Password Via CLI

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: T1219.002 Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects piping the password to an anydesk instance via CMD and the '--set-password' flag.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Remote Access Tool - AnyDesk Piped Password Via CLI
-- Sigma ID:     b1377339-fda6-477a-b455-ac0923f9ec2c
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1219.002
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2022-09-28
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_remote_access_tools_anydesk_piped_password_via_cli.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate piping of the password to anydesk; Some FP could occur with similar tools that uses the same command line '--set-password'
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%/c %' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%echo %' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.exe --set-password%')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Legitimate piping of the password to anydesk; Some FP could occur with similar tools that uses the same command line '--set-password'

Remote Access Tool - AnyDesk Execution With Known Revoked Signing Certificate

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: execution Author: Sai Prashanth Pulisetti, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects the execution of an AnyDesk binary with a version prior to 8.0.8. Prior to version 8.0.8, the Anydesk application used a signing certificate that got compromised by threat actors. Use this rule to detect instances of older versions of Anydesk using the compromised certificate This is recommended in order to avoid attackers leveraging the certificate and signing their binaries to bypass detections.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Remote Access Tool - AnyDesk Execution With Known Revoked Signing Certificate
-- Sigma ID:     41f407b5-3096-44ea-a74f-96d04fbc41be
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        execution
-- Author:       Sai Prashanth Pulisetti, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2024-02-08
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_remote_access_tools_anydesk_revoked_cert.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unlikely
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND 1=1
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unlikely

Remote Access Tool - AnyDesk Silent Installation

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1219.002 Author: Ján Trenčanský Status: test
Detects AnyDesk Remote Desktop silent installation. Which can be used by attackers to gain remote access.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Remote Access Tool - AnyDesk Silent Installation
-- Sigma ID:     114e7f1c-f137-48c8-8f54-3088c24ce4b9
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1219.002
-- Author:       Ján Trenčanský
-- Date:         2021-08-06
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_remote_access_tools_anydesk_silent_install.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate deployment of AnyDesk
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%--install%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%--start-with-win%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%--silent%')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Legitimate deployment of AnyDesk

Remote Access Tool - Anydesk Execution From Suspicious Folder

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1219.002 Author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems) Status: test
An adversary may use legitimate desktop support and remote access software, such as Team Viewer, Go2Assist, LogMein, AmmyyAdmin, etc, to establish an interactive command and control channel to target systems within networks. These services are commonly used as legitimate technical support software, and may be allowed by application control within a target environment. Remote access tools like VNC, Ammyy, and Teamviewer are used frequently when compared with other legitimate software commonly used by adversaries. (Citation: Symantec Living off the Land)
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Remote Access Tool - Anydesk Execution From Suspicious Folder
-- Sigma ID:     065b00ca-5d5c-4557-ac95-64a6d0b64d86
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1219.002
-- Author:       Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2022-05-20
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_remote_access_tools_anydesk_susp_exec.yml
-- Unmapped:     Description
-- False Pos:    Legitimate use of AnyDesk from a non-standard folder
-- ============================================================
-- UNMAPPED_FIELD: Description

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'product')] AS product,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'company')] AS company,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (((procName LIKE '%\\AnyDesk.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\AnyDeskMSI.exe'))
  OR (rawEventMsg = 'AnyDesk')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'product') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'product')] = 'AnyDesk'))
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'company') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'company')] = 'AnyDesk Software GmbH'))
  AND NOT ((procName LIKE '%\\AppData\\%' OR procName LIKE '%Program Files (x86)\\AnyDesk%' OR procName LIKE '%Program Files\\AnyDesk%')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Legitimate use of AnyDesk from a non-standard folder

Remote Access Tool - GoToAssist Execution

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: T1219.002 Author: frack113 Status: test
An adversary may use legitimate desktop support and remote access software, such as Team Viewer, Go2Assist, LogMein, AmmyyAdmin, etc, to establish an interactive command and control channel to target systems within networks. These services are commonly used as legitimate technical support software, and may be allowed by application control within a target environment. Remote access tools like VNC, Ammyy, and Teamviewer are used frequently when compared with other legitimate software commonly used by adversaries. (Citation: Symantec Living off the Land)
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Remote Access Tool - GoToAssist Execution
-- Sigma ID:     b6d98a4f-cef0-4abf-bbf6-24132854a83d
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1219.002
-- Author:       frack113
-- Date:         2022-02-13
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_remote_access_tools_gotoopener.yml
-- Unmapped:     Description
-- False Pos:    Legitimate use
-- ============================================================
-- UNMAPPED_FIELD: Description

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'product')] AS product,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'company')] AS company,
  rawEventMsg

WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (rawEventMsg = 'GoTo Opener')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'product') > 0

  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'company') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'company')] = 'LogMeIn, Inc.'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Legitimate use

Remote Access Tool - LogMeIn Execution

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: T1219.002 Author: frack113 Status: test
An adversary may use legitimate desktop support and remote access software, such as Team Viewer, Go2Assist, LogMein, AmmyyAdmin, etc, to establish an interactive command and control channel to target systems within networks. These services are commonly used as legitimate technical support software, and may be allowed by application control within a target environment. Remote access tools like VNC, Ammyy, and Teamviewer are used frequently when compared with other legitimate software commonly used by adversaries. (Citation: Symantec Living off the Land)
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Remote Access Tool - LogMeIn Execution
-- Sigma ID:     d85873ef-a0f8-4c48-a53a-6b621f11729d
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1219.002
-- Author:       frack113
-- Date:         2022-02-11
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_remote_access_tools_logmein.yml
-- Unmapped:     Description
-- False Pos:    Legitimate use
-- ============================================================
-- UNMAPPED_FIELD: Description

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'product')] AS product,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'company')] AS company,

WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')

  AND (rawEventMsg = 'LMIGuardianSvc')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'product') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'product')] = 'LMIGuardianSvc'))
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'company') > 0

Remote Access Tool - Potential MeshAgent Execution - Windows

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: T1219.002 Author: Norbert Jaśniewicz (AlphaSOC) Status: experimental
Detects potential execution of MeshAgent which is a tool used for remote access. Historical data shows that threat actors rename MeshAgent binary to evade detection. Matching command lines with the '--meshServiceName' argument can indicate that the MeshAgent is being used for remote access.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Remote Access Tool - Potential MeshAgent Execution - Windows
-- Sigma ID:     2fbbe9ff-0afc-470b-bdc0-592198339968
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       experimental
-- MITRE:        T1219.002
-- Author:       Norbert Jaśniewicz (AlphaSOC)
-- Date:         2025-05-19
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_remote_access_tools_meshagent_arguments.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Environments that legitimately use MeshAgent
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%--meshServiceName%')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Environments that legitimately use MeshAgent

Remote Access Tool - MeshAgent Command Execution via MeshCentral

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: T1219.002 Author: @Kostastsale Status: test
Detects the use of MeshAgent to execute commands on the target host, particularly when threat actors might abuse it to execute commands directly. MeshAgent can execute commands on the target host by leveraging win-console to obscure their activities and win-dispatcher to run malicious code through IPC with child processes.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Remote Access Tool - MeshAgent Command Execution via MeshCentral
-- Sigma ID:     74a2b202-73e0-4693-9a3a-9d36146d0775
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1219.002
-- Author:       @Kostastsale
-- Date:         2024-09-22
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_remote_access_tools_meshagent_exec.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    False positives can be found in environments using MeshAgent for remote management, analysis should prioritize the grandparent process, MeshAgent.exe, and scrutinize the resulting child processes triggered by any suspicious interactive commands directed at the target host.
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] AS parentImage,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\meshagent.exe')
    AND (procName LIKE '%\\cmd.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\powershell.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\pwsh.exe'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: False positives can be found in environments using MeshAgent for remote management, analysis should prioritize the grandparent process, MeshAgent.exe, and scrutinize the resulting child processes triggered by any suspicious interactive commands directed at the target host.

Remote Access Tool - NetSupport Execution

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: T1219.002 Author: frack113 Status: test
An adversary may use legitimate desktop support and remote access software, such as Team Viewer, Go2Assist, LogMein, AmmyyAdmin, etc, to establish an interactive command and control channel to target systems within networks. These services are commonly used as legitimate technical support software, and may be allowed by application control within a target environment. Remote access tools like VNC, Ammyy, and Teamviewer are used frequently when compared with other legitimate software commonly used by adversaries. (Citation: Symantec Living off the Land)
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Remote Access Tool - NetSupport Execution
-- Sigma ID:     758ff488-18d5-4cbe-8ec4-02b6285a434f
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1219.002
-- Author:       frack113
-- Date:         2022-09-25
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_remote_access_tools_netsupport.yml
-- Unmapped:     Description
-- False Pos:    Legitimate use
-- ============================================================
-- UNMAPPED_FIELD: Description

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'product')] AS product,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'company')] AS company,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (rawEventMsg = 'NetSupport Client Configurator')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'product') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'product')] = 'NetSupport Remote Control'))
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'company') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'company')] = 'NetSupport Ltd'))
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'PCICFGUI.EXE'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Legitimate use

Remote Access Tool - NetSupport Execution From Unusual Location

medium FSM Severity: 5 Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects execution of client32.exe (NetSupport RAT) from an unusual location (outside of 'C:\Program Files')
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Remote Access Tool - NetSupport Execution From Unusual Location
-- Sigma ID:     37e8d358-6408-4853-82f4-98333fca7014
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        (none)
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2022-09-19
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_remote_access_tools_netsupport_susp_exec.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'product')] AS product,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'hashMD5')] AS hashes,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((procName LIKE '%\\client32.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'product') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'product')] LIKE '%NetSupport Remote Control%'))
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] LIKE '%client32.exe%'))
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'hashMD5') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'hashMD5')] LIKE '%IMPHASH=a9d50692e95b79723f3e76fcf70d023e%'))
  AND NOT ((procName LIKE 'C:\\Program Files\\%' OR procName LIKE 'C:\\Program Files (x86)\\%')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Remote Access Tool - Renamed MeshAgent Execution - Windows

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1219.002, T1036.003 Author: Norbert Jaśniewicz (AlphaSOC) Status: experimental
Detects the execution of a renamed instance of the Remote Monitoring and Management (RMM) tool, MeshAgent. RMM tools such as MeshAgent are commonly utilized by IT administrators for legitimate remote support and system management. However, malicious actors may exploit these tools by renaming them to bypass detection mechanisms, enabling unauthorized access and control over compromised systems.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Remote Access Tool - Renamed MeshAgent Execution - Windows
-- Sigma ID:     b471f462-eb0d-4832-be35-28d94bdb4780
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       experimental
-- MITRE:        T1219.002, T1036.003
-- Author:       Norbert Jaśniewicz (AlphaSOC)
-- Date:         2025-05-19
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_remote_access_tools_renamed_meshagent_execution.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%--meshServiceName%'))
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] LIKE '%meshagent%'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Remote Access Tool - RURAT Execution From Unusual Location

medium FSM Severity: 5 Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects execution of Remote Utilities RAT (RURAT) from an unusual location (outside of 'C:\Program Files')
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Remote Access Tool - RURAT Execution From Unusual Location
-- Sigma ID:     e01fa958-6893-41d4-ae03-182477c5e77d
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        (none)
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2022-09-19

-- Unmapped:     (none)

-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'product')] AS product,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (((procName LIKE '%\\rutserv.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\rfusclient.exe'))
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'product') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'product')] = 'Remote Utilities'))
  AND NOT ((procName LIKE 'C:\\Program Files\\Remote Utilities%' OR procName LIKE 'C:\\Program Files (x86)\\Remote Utilities%')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
