  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND procName LIKE '%\\svchost.exe'
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Permission Check Via Accesschk.EXE

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: discovery, T1069.001 Author: Teymur Kheirkhabarov (idea), Mangatas Tondang, oscd.community, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects the usage of the "Accesschk" utility, an access and privilege audit tool developed by SysInternal and often being abused by attacker to verify process privileges
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Permission Check Via Accesschk.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     c625d754-6a3d-4f65-9c9a-536aea960d37
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        discovery | T1069.001
-- Author:       Teymur Kheirkhabarov (idea), Mangatas Tondang, oscd.community, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2020-10-13
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_sysinternals_accesschk_check_permissions.yml
-- Unmapped:     Description
-- False Pos:    System administrator Usage
-- ============================================================
-- UNMAPPED_FIELD: Description

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'product')] AS product,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%uwcqv %' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%kwsu %' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%qwsu %' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%uwdqs %'))
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'product') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'product')] LIKE '%AccessChk'))
  OR (rawEventMsg LIKE '%Reports effective permissions%')
  OR ((procName LIKE '%\\accesschk.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\accesschk64.exe'))
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'accesschk.exe')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: System administrator Usage

Active Directory Database Snapshot Via ADExplorer

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: discovery, T1087.002, T1069.002, T1482 Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects the execution of Sysinternals ADExplorer with the "-snapshot" flag in order to save a local copy of the active directory database. This can be used by attackers to extract data for Bloodhound, usernames for password spraying or use the meta data for social engineering. The snapshot doesn't contain password hashes but there have been cases, where administrators put passwords in the comment field.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Active Directory Database Snapshot Via ADExplorer
-- Sigma ID:     9212f354-7775-4e28-9c9f-8f0a4544e664
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        discovery | T1087.002, T1069.002, T1482
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2023-03-14
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_sysinternals_adexplorer_execution.yml
-- Unmapped:     Description
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================
-- UNMAPPED_FIELD: Description

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'product')] AS product,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%snapshot%')
  AND ((procName LIKE '%\\ADExp.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\ADExplorer.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\ADExplorer64.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\ADExplorer64a.exe'))
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'AdExp'))
  OR (rawEventMsg = 'Active Directory Editor')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'product') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'product')] = 'Sysinternals ADExplorer')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Suspicious Active Directory Database Snapshot Via ADExplorer

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: discovery, T1087.002, T1069.002, T1482 Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects the execution of Sysinternals ADExplorer with the "-snapshot" flag in order to save a local copy of the active directory database to a suspicious directory. This can be used by attackers to extract data for Bloodhound, usernames for password spraying or use the meta data for social engineering. The snapshot doesn't contain password hashes but there have been cases, where administrators put passwords in the comment field.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Suspicious Active Directory Database Snapshot Via ADExplorer
-- Sigma ID:     ef61af62-bc74-4f58-b49b-626448227652
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        discovery | T1087.002, T1069.002, T1482
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2023-03-14
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_sysinternals_adexplorer_susp_execution.yml
-- Unmapped:     Description
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================
-- UNMAPPED_FIELD: Description

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'product')] AS product,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%snapshot%')
  AND ((procName LIKE '%\\ADExp.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\ADExplorer.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\ADExplorer64.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\ADExplorer64a.exe'))
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'AdExp'))
  OR (rawEventMsg = 'Active Directory Editor')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'product') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'product')] = 'Sysinternals ADExplorer'))
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\Downloads\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\Users\\Public\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\AppData\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\Windows\\Temp\\%')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Potential Execution of Sysinternals Tools

low FSM Severity: 3 MITRE: T1588.002 Author: Markus Neis Status: test
Detects command lines that contain the 'accepteula' flag which could be a sign of execution of one of the Sysinternals tools
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potential Execution of Sysinternals Tools
-- Sigma ID:     7cccd811-7ae9-4ebe-9afd-cb5c406b824b
-- Level:        low  |  FSM Severity: 3
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1588.002
-- Author:       Markus Neis
-- Date:         2017-08-28
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_sysinternals_eula_accepted.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate use of SysInternals tools; Programs that use the same command line flag
-- ============================================================
-- UNSUPPORTED_MODIFIER: contains|windash

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND rawEventMsg LIKE '% -accepteula%'
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Legitimate use of SysInternals tools; Programs that use the same command line flag

Potential Memory Dumping Activity Via LiveKD

medium FSM Severity: 5 Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects execution of LiveKD based on PE metadata or image name
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potential Memory Dumping Activity Via LiveKD
-- Sigma ID:     a85f7765-698a-4088-afa0-ecfbf8d01fa4
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        (none)
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2023-05-15
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_sysinternals_livekd_execution.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Administration and debugging activity (must be investigated)
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((procName LIKE '%\\livekd.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\livekd64.exe'))
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'livekd.exe'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Administration and debugging activity (must be investigated)

Kernel Memory Dump Via LiveKD

high FSM Severity: 7 Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects execution of LiveKD with the "-m" flag to potentially dump the kernel memory
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Kernel Memory Dump Via LiveKD
-- Sigma ID:     c7746f1c-47d3-43d6-8c45-cd1e54b6b0a2
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        (none)
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2023-05-16
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_sysinternals_livekd_kernel_memory_dump.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unlikely in production environment
-- ============================================================
-- UNSUPPORTED_MODIFIER: contains|windash

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (rawEventMsg LIKE '% -m%'
  AND ((procName LIKE '%\\livekd.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\livekd64.exe'))
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'livekd.exe')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unlikely in production environment

Procdump Execution

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: T1036, T1003.001 Author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects usage of the SysInternals Procdump utility
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Procdump Execution
-- Sigma ID:     2e65275c-8288-4ab4-aeb7-6274f58b6b20
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1036, T1003.001
-- Author:       Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2021-08-16
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_sysinternals_procdump.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate use of procdump by a developer or administrator
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\procdump.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\procdump64.exe')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Legitimate use of procdump by a developer or administrator

Potential SysInternals ProcDump Evasion

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1036, T1003.001 Author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects uses of the SysInternals ProcDump utility in which ProcDump or its output get renamed, or a dump file is moved or copied to a different name
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potential SysInternals ProcDump Evasion
-- Sigma ID:     79b06761-465f-4f88-9ef2-150e24d3d737
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1036, T1003.001
-- Author:       Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2022-01-11
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_sysinternals_procdump_evasion.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    False positives are expected in cases in which ProcDump just gets copied to a different directory without any renaming
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%copy procdump%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%move procdump%'))
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%copy %' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.dmp %')
    AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%2.dmp%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%lsass%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%out.dmp%')))
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%copy lsass.exe\_%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%move lsass.exe\_%'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: False positives are expected in cases in which ProcDump just gets copied to a different directory without any renaming

Potential LSASS Process Dump Via Procdump

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1036, T1003.001 Author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems) Status: stable
Detects potential credential harvesting attempts through LSASS memory dumps using ProcDump. This rule identifies suspicious command-line patterns that combine memory dump flags (-ma, -mm, -mp) with LSASS-related process markers. LSASS (Local Security Authority Subsystem Service) contains sensitive authentication data including plaintext passwords, NTLM hashes, and Kerberos tickets in memory. Attackers commonly dump LSASS memory to extract credentials for lateral movement and privilege escalation.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potential LSASS Process Dump Via Procdump
-- Sigma ID:     5afee48e-67dd-4e03-a783-f74259dcf998
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       stable
-- MITRE:        T1036, T1003.001
-- Author:       Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2018-10-30
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_sysinternals_procdump_lsass.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unlikely, because no one should dump an lsass process memory; Another tool that uses command line flags similar to ProcDump
-- ============================================================
-- UNSUPPORTED_MODIFIER: contains|windash

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((rawEventMsg LIKE '% -ma %' OR rawEventMsg LIKE '% -mm %' OR rawEventMsg LIKE '% -mp %')
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% ls%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% keyiso%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% samss%')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unlikely, because no one should dump an lsass process memory; Another tool that uses command line flags similar to ProcDump

Psexec Execution

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: execution, T1569, T1021 Author: omkar72 Status: test
Detects user accept agreement execution in psexec commandline
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Psexec Execution
-- Sigma ID:     730fc21b-eaff-474b-ad23-90fd265d4988
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        execution | T1569, T1021
-- Author:       omkar72
-- Date:         2020-10-30
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_sysinternals_psexec_execution.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Administrative scripts.
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\psexec.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'psexec.c'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Administrative scripts.

PsExec/PAExec Escalation to LOCAL SYSTEM

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1587.001 Author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects suspicious commandline flags used by PsExec and PAExec to escalate a command line to LOCAL_SYSTEM rights
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        PsExec/PAExec Escalation to LOCAL SYSTEM
-- Sigma ID:     8834e2f7-6b4b-4f09-8906-d2276470ee23
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1587.001
-- Author:       Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2021-11-23
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_sysinternals_psexec_paexec_escalate_system.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Admins that use PsExec or PAExec to escalate to the SYSTEM account for maintenance purposes (rare); Users that debug Microsoft Intune issues using the commands mentioned in the official documentation; see https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/mem/intune/apps/intune-management-extension
-- ============================================================
-- UNSUPPORTED_MODIFIER: contains|windash

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%psexec%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%paexec%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%accepteula%'))
  AND (rawEventMsg LIKE '% -s cmd%' OR rawEventMsg LIKE '% -s -i cmd%' OR rawEventMsg LIKE '% -i -s cmd%' OR rawEventMsg LIKE '% -s pwsh%' OR rawEventMsg LIKE '% -s -i pwsh%' OR rawEventMsg LIKE '% -i -s pwsh%' OR rawEventMsg LIKE '% -s powershell%' OR rawEventMsg LIKE '% -s -i powershell%' OR rawEventMsg LIKE '% -i -s powershell%'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Admins that use PsExec or PAExec to escalate to the SYSTEM account for maintenance purposes (rare); Users that debug Microsoft Intune issues using the commands mentioned in the official documentation; see https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/mem/intune/apps/intune-management-extension

Potential PsExec Remote Execution

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1587.001 Author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects potential psexec command that initiate execution on a remote systems via common commandline flags used by the utility
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potential PsExec Remote Execution
-- Sigma ID:     ea011323-7045-460b-b2d7-0f7442ea6b38
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1587.001
-- Author:       Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2023-02-28
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_sysinternals_psexec_remote_execution.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%accepteula%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% -u %' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% -p %' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% \\\\\\\\%')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

PsExec Service Execution

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: execution Author: Thomas Patzke, Romaissa Adjailia, Florian Roth (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects launch of the PSEXESVC service, which means that this system was the target of a psexec remote execution
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        PsExec Service Execution
-- Sigma ID:     fdfcbd78-48f1-4a4b-90ac-d82241e368c5
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        execution
-- Author:       Thomas Patzke, Romaissa Adjailia, Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2017-06-12
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_sysinternals_psexesvc.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate administrative tasks
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (procName = 'C:\Windows\PSEXESVC.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'psexesvc.exe'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Legitimate administrative tasks

PsExec Service Child Process Execution as LOCAL SYSTEM

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: execution Author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects suspicious launch of the PSEXESVC service on this system and a sub process run as LOCAL_SYSTEM (-s), which means that someone remotely started a command on this system running it with highest privileges and not only the privileges of the login user account (e.g. the administrator account)
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        PsExec Service Child Process Execution as LOCAL SYSTEM
-- Sigma ID:     7c0dcd3d-acf8-4f71-9570-f448b0034f94
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        execution
-- Author:       Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2022-07-21
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_sysinternals_psexesvc_as_system.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Users that debug Microsoft Intune issues using the commands mentioned in the official documentation; see https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/mem/intune/apps/intune-management-extension
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] AS parentImage,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] = 'C:\Windows\PSEXESVC.exe')
    AND (user LIKE '%AUTHORI%' OR user LIKE '%AUTORI%'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Users that debug Microsoft Intune issues using the commands mentioned in the official documentation; see https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/mem/intune/apps/intune-management-extension

Suspicious Use of PsLogList

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: discovery, T1087, T1087.001, T1087.002 Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects usage of the PsLogList utility to dump event log in order to extract admin accounts and perform account discovery or delete events logs
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Suspicious Use of PsLogList
-- Sigma ID:     aae1243f-d8af-40d8-ab20-33fc6d0c55bc
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        discovery | T1087, T1087.001, T1087.002
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2021-12-18
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_sysinternals_psloglist.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Another tool that uses the command line switches of PsLogList; Legitimate use of PsLogList by an administrator
-- ============================================================
-- UNSUPPORTED_MODIFIER: contains|windash

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% security%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% application%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% system%'))
  AND (rawEventMsg LIKE '% -d%' OR rawEventMsg LIKE '% -x%' OR rawEventMsg LIKE '% -s%' OR rawEventMsg LIKE '% -c%' OR rawEventMsg LIKE '% -g%')
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'psloglist.exe'))
  OR ((procName LIKE '%\\psloglist.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\psloglist64.exe')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Another tool that uses the command line switches of PsLogList; Legitimate use of PsLogList by an administrator

Sysinternals PsService Execution

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: discovery, persistence, T1543.003 Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects usage of Sysinternals PsService which can be abused for service reconnaissance and tampering
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Sysinternals PsService Execution
-- Sigma ID:     3371f518-5fe3-4cf6-a14b-2a0ae3fd8a4f
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        discovery, persistence | T1543.003
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2022-06-16
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_sysinternals_psservice.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate use of PsService by an administrator
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'psservice.exe'))
  OR ((procName LIKE '%\\PsService.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\PsService64.exe'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Legitimate use of PsService by an administrator

Sysinternals PsSuspend Execution

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: discovery, persistence, T1543.003 Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects usage of Sysinternals PsSuspend which can be abused to suspend critical processes
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Sysinternals PsSuspend Execution
-- Sigma ID:     48bbc537-b652-4b4e-bd1d-281172df448f
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        discovery, persistence | T1543.003
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2023-03-23
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_sysinternals_pssuspend_execution.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'pssuspend.exe'))
  OR ((procName LIKE '%\\pssuspend.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\pssuspend64.exe'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Sysinternals PsSuspend Suspicious Execution

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1562.001 Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects suspicious execution of Sysinternals PsSuspend, where the utility is used to suspend critical processes such as AV or EDR to bypass defenses
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Sysinternals PsSuspend Suspicious Execution
-- Sigma ID:     4beb6ae0-f85b-41e2-8f18-8668abc8af78
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1562.001
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2023-03-23
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_sysinternals_pssuspend_susp_execution.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unlikely
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%msmpeng.exe%')
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'pssuspend.exe'))
  OR ((procName LIKE '%\\pssuspend.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\pssuspend64.exe')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unlikely

Potential File Overwrite Via Sysinternals SDelete

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: impact, T1485 Author: frack113 Status: test
Detects the use of SDelete to erase a file not the free space
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potential File Overwrite Via Sysinternals SDelete
-- Sigma ID:     a4824fca-976f-4964-b334-0621379e84c4
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        impact | T1485
-- Author:       frack113
-- Date:         2021-06-03
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_sysinternals_sdelete.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'sdelete.exe')
  AND NOT ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% -h%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% -c%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% -z%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% /\\?%'))))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Potential Privilege Escalation To LOCAL SYSTEM

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1587.001 Author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects unknown program using commandline flags usually used by tools such as PsExec and PAExec to start programs with SYSTEM Privileges
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potential Privilege Escalation To LOCAL SYSTEM
-- Sigma ID:     207b0396-3689-42d9-8399-4222658efc99
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1587.001
-- Author:       Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2021-05-22
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_sysinternals_susp_psexec_paexec_flags.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Weird admins that rename their tools; Software companies that bundle PsExec/PAExec with their software and rename it, so that it is less embarrassing
-- ============================================================
-- UNSUPPORTED_MODIFIER: contains|windash

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (rawEventMsg LIKE '% -s cmd%' OR rawEventMsg LIKE '% -s -i cmd%' OR rawEventMsg LIKE '% -i -s cmd%' OR rawEventMsg LIKE '% -s pwsh%' OR rawEventMsg LIKE '% -s -i pwsh%' OR rawEventMsg LIKE '% -i -s pwsh%' OR rawEventMsg LIKE '% -s powershell%' OR rawEventMsg LIKE '% -s -i powershell%' OR rawEventMsg LIKE '% -i -s powershell%')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Weird admins that rename their tools; Software companies that bundle PsExec/PAExec with their software and rename it, so that it is less embarrassing

Sysmon Configuration Update

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: T1562.001 Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects updates to Sysmon's configuration. Attackers might update or replace the Sysmon configuration with a bare bone one to avoid monitoring without shutting down the service completely
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Sysmon Configuration Update
-- Sigma ID:     87911521-7098-470b-a459-9a57fc80bdfd
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1562.001
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2023-03-09
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_sysinternals_sysmon_config_update.yml
-- Unmapped:     Description
-- False Pos:    Legitimate administrators might use this command to update Sysmon configuration.
-- ============================================================
-- UNSUPPORTED_MODIFIER: contains|windash
-- UNMAPPED_FIELD: Description

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (rawEventMsg LIKE '%-c%'
  AND ((procName LIKE '%\\Sysmon64.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\Sysmon.exe'))
  OR (rawEventMsg = 'System activity monitor'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Legitimate administrators might use this command to update Sysmon configuration.

Uninstall Sysinternals Sysmon

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1562.001 Author: frack113 Status: test
Detects the removal of Sysmon, which could be a potential attempt at defense evasion
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Uninstall Sysinternals Sysmon
-- Sigma ID:     6a5f68d1-c4b5-46b9-94ee-5324892ea939
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1562.001
-- Author:       frack113
-- Date:         2022-01-12
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_sysinternals_sysmon_uninstall.yml
-- Unmapped:     Description
-- False Pos:    Legitimate administrators might use this command to remove Sysmon for debugging purposes
-- ============================================================
-- UNSUPPORTED_MODIFIER: contains|windash
-- UNMAPPED_FIELD: Description

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (rawEventMsg LIKE '%-u%'
  AND ((procName LIKE '%\\Sysmon64.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\Sysmon.exe'))
  OR (rawEventMsg = 'System activity monitor'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Legitimate administrators might use this command to remove Sysmon for debugging purposes

Potential Binary Impersonating Sysinternals Tools

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: execution, T1218, T1202, T1036.005 Author: frack113, Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects binaries that use the same name as legitimate sysinternals tools to evade detection. This rule looks for the execution of binaries that are named similarly to Sysinternals tools. Adversary may rename their malicious tools as legitimate Sysinternals tools to evade detection.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potential Binary Impersonating Sysinternals Tools
-- Sigma ID:     7cce6fc8-a07f-4d84-a53e-96e1879843c9
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        execution | T1218, T1202, T1036.005
-- Author:       frack113, Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2021-12-20
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_sysinternals_tools_masquerading.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND 1=1
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Sysprep on AppData Folder

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: execution, T1059 Author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects suspicious sysprep process start with AppData folder as target (as used by Trojan Syndicasec in Thrip report by Symantec)
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Sysprep on AppData Folder
-- Sigma ID:     d5b9ae7a-e6fc-405e-80ff-2ff9dcc64e7e
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        execution | T1059
-- Author:       Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2018-06-22
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_sysprep_appdata.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    False positives depend on scripts and administrative tools used in the monitored environment
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\sysprep.exe'
    AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\AppData\\%'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: False positives depend on scripts and administrative tools used in the monitored environment

Suspicious Execution of Systeminfo

low FSM Severity: 3 MITRE: discovery, T1082 Author: frack113 Status: test
Detects usage of the "systeminfo" command to retrieve information
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Suspicious Execution of Systeminfo
-- Sigma ID:     0ef56343-059e-4cb6-adc1-4c3c967c5e46
-- Level:        low  |  FSM Severity: 3
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        discovery | T1082
-- Author:       frack113
-- Date:         2022-01-01
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_systeminfo_execution.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\systeminfo.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'sysinfo.exe'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Potential Signing Bypass Via Windows Developer Features

high FSM Severity: 7 Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects when a user enable developer features such as "Developer Mode" or "Application Sideloading". Which allows the user to install untrusted packages.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potential Signing Bypass Via Windows Developer Features
-- Sigma ID:     a383dec4-deec-4e6e-913b-ed9249670848
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        (none)
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2023-01-11
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_systemsettingsadminflows_turn_on_dev_features.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%TurnOnDeveloperFeatures%')
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\SystemSettingsAdminFlows.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'SystemSettingsAdminFlows.EXE'))
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%DeveloperUnlock%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%EnableSideloading%')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Suspicious Recursive Takeown

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: T1222.001 Author: frack113 Status: test
Adversaries can interact with the DACLs using built-in Windows commands takeown which can grant adversaries higher permissions on specific files and folders
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Suspicious Recursive Takeown
-- Sigma ID:     554601fb-9b71-4bcc-abf4-21a611be4fde
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1222.001
-- Author:       frack113
-- Date:         2022-01-30
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_takeown_recursive_own.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Scripts created by developers and admins; Administrative activity
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\takeown.exe'
    AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%/f %' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%/r%'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Scripts created by developers and admins; Administrative activity

Tap Installer Execution

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: exfiltration, T1048 Author: Daniil Yugoslavskiy, Ian Davis, oscd.community Status: test
Well-known TAP software installation. Possible preparation for data exfiltration using tunneling techniques
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Tap Installer Execution
-- Sigma ID:     99793437-3e16-439b-be0f-078782cf953d
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        exfiltration | T1048
-- Author:       Daniil Yugoslavskiy, Ian Davis, oscd.community
-- Date:         2019-10-24
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_tapinstall_execution.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate OpenVPN TAP installation
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND procName LIKE '%\\tapinstall.exe'
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Legitimate OpenVPN TAP installation

Compressed File Creation Via Tar.EXE

low FSM Severity: 3 MITRE: collection, exfiltration, T1560, T1560.001 Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), AdmU3 Status: test
Detects execution of "tar.exe" in order to create a compressed file. Adversaries may abuse various utilities to compress or encrypt data before exfiltration.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Compressed File Creation Via Tar.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     418a3163-3247-4b7b-9933-dcfcb7c52ea9
-- Level:        low  |  FSM Severity: 3
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        collection, exfiltration | T1560, T1560.001
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), AdmU3
-- Date:         2023-12-19
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_tar_compression.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Likely
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%-c%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%-r%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%-u%'))
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\tar.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'bsdtar')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Likely

Compressed File Extraction Via Tar.EXE

low FSM Severity: 3 MITRE: collection, exfiltration, T1560, T1560.001 Author: AdmU3 Status: test
Detects execution of "tar.exe" in order to extract compressed file. Adversaries may abuse various utilities in order to decompress data to avoid detection.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Compressed File Extraction Via Tar.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     bf361876-6620-407a-812f-bfe11e51e924
-- Level:        low  |  FSM Severity: 3
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        collection, exfiltration | T1560, T1560.001
-- Author:       AdmU3
-- Date:         2023-12-19
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_tar_extraction.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Likely
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%-x%')
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\tar.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'bsdtar')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Likely

Taskkill Symantec Endpoint Protection

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1562.001 Author: Ilya Krestinichev, Florian Roth (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects one of the possible scenarios for disabling Symantec Endpoint Protection. Symantec Endpoint Protection antivirus software services incorrectly implement the protected service mechanism. As a result, the NT AUTHORITY/SYSTEM user can execute the taskkill /im command several times ccSvcHst.exe /f, thereby killing the process belonging to the service, and thus shutting down the service.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Taskkill Symantec Endpoint Protection
-- Sigma ID:     4a6713f6-3331-11ed-a261-0242ac120002
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1562.001
-- Author:       Ilya Krestinichev, Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2022-09-13
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_taskkill_sep.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%taskkill%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% /F %' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% /IM %' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%ccSvcHst.exe%')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Loaded Module Enumeration Via Tasklist.EXE

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: T1003 Author: Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel Status: test
Detects the enumeration of a specific DLL or EXE being used by a binary via "tasklist.exe". This is often used by attackers in order to find the specific process identifier (PID) that is using the DLL in question. In order to dump the process memory or perform other nefarious actions.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Loaded Module Enumeration Via Tasklist.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     34275eb8-fa19-436b-b959-3d9ecd53fa1f
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1003
-- Author:       Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel
-- Date:         2024-02-12
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_tasklist_module_enumeration.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================
-- UNSUPPORTED_MODIFIER: contains|windash

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (rawEventMsg LIKE '%-m%'
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\tasklist.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'tasklist.exe'))
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%rdpcorets.dll%'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Taskmgr as LOCAL_SYSTEM

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1036 Author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects the creation of taskmgr.exe process in context of LOCAL_SYSTEM
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Taskmgr as LOCAL_SYSTEM
-- Sigma ID:     9fff585c-c33e-4a86-b3cd-39312079a65f
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1036
-- Author:       Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2018-03-18
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_taskmgr_localsystem.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((user LIKE '%AUTHORI%' OR user LIKE '%AUTORI%')
    AND procName LIKE '%\\taskmgr.exe')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

New Process Created Via Taskmgr.EXE

low FSM Severity: 3 MITRE: T1036 Author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects the creation of a process via the Windows task manager. This might be an attempt to bypass UAC
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        New Process Created Via Taskmgr.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     3d7679bd-0c00-440c-97b0-3f204273e6c7
-- Level:        low  |  FSM Severity: 3
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1036
-- Author:       Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2018-03-13
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_taskmgr_susp_child_process.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Administrative activity
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] AS parentImage,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\taskmgr.exe')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Administrative activity

Potentially Suspicious Command Targeting Teams Sensitive Files

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: T1528 Author: @SerkinValery Status: test
Detects a commandline containing references to the Microsoft Teams database or cookies files from a process other than Teams. The database might contain authentication tokens and other sensitive information about the logged in accounts.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potentially Suspicious Command Targeting Teams Sensitive Files
-- Sigma ID:     d2eb17db-1d39-41dc-b57f-301f6512fa75
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1528
-- Author:       @SerkinValery
-- Date:         2022-09-16
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_teams_suspicious_command_line_cred_access.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\Microsoft\\Teams\\Cookies%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\Microsoft\\Teams\\Local Storage\\leveldb%'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

New Virtual Smart Card Created Via TpmVscMgr.EXE

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: execution Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects execution of "Tpmvscmgr.exe" to create a new virtual smart card.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        New Virtual Smart Card Created Via TpmVscMgr.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     c633622e-cab9-4eaa-bb13-66a1d68b3e47
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        execution
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2023-06-15
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_tpmvscmgr_add_virtual_smartcard.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate usage by an administrator
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%create%')
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\tpmvscmgr.exe'
    AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'TpmVscMgr.exe')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Legitimate usage by an administrator

  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR

Detects a suspicious RDP session redirect using tscon.exe

-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Suspicious RDP Redirect Using TSCON
-- Sigma ID:     f72aa3e8-49f9-4c7d-bd74-f8ab84ff9bbb
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1563.002, T1021.001
-- Author:       Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2018-03-17
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_tscon_rdp_redirect.yml

-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% /dest:rdp-tcp#%')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
