Potential RDP Session Hijacking Activity

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: execution Author: @juju4 Status: test
Detects potential RDP Session Hijacking activity on Windows systems
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potential RDP Session Hijacking Activity
-- Sigma ID:     224f140f-3553-4cd1-af78-13d81bf9f7cc
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        execution
-- Author:       @juju4
-- Date:         2022-12-27
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_tscon_rdp_session_hijacking.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Administrative activity
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'integrityLevel')] AS integrityLevel,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((procName LIKE '%\\tscon.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'tscon.exe'))
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'integrityLevel') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'integrityLevel')] IN ('System', 'S-1-16-16384')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Administrative activity

UAC Bypass Using ChangePK and SLUI

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1548.002 Author: Christian Burkard (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects an UAC bypass that uses changepk.exe and slui.exe (UACMe 61)
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        UAC Bypass Using ChangePK and SLUI
-- Sigma ID:     503d581c-7df0-4bbe-b9be-5840c0ecc1fc
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1548.002
-- Author:       Christian Burkard (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2021-08-23
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_uac_bypass_changepk_slui.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] AS parentImage,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'integrityLevel')] AS integrityLevel,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\changepk.exe'
    AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\slui.exe')
    AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'integrityLevel') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'integrityLevel')] IN ('High', 'System', 'S-1-16-16384', 'S-1-16-12288')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

UAC Bypass Using Disk Cleanup

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1548.002 Author: Christian Burkard (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects the pattern of UAC Bypass using scheduled tasks and variable expansion of cleanmgr.exe (UACMe 34)
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        UAC Bypass Using Disk Cleanup
-- Sigma ID:     b697e69c-746f-4a86-9f59-7bfff8eab881
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1548.002
-- Author:       Christian Burkard (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2021-08-30
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_uac_bypass_cleanmgr.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentCommand')] AS parentCommandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'integrityLevel')] AS integrityLevel,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%"\\system32\\cleanmgr.exe /autoclean /d C:')
    AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentCommand') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentCommand')] = 'C:\Windows\system32\svchost.exe -k netsvcs -p -s Schedule')
    AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'integrityLevel') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'integrityLevel')] IN ('High', 'System', 'S-1-16-16384', 'S-1-16-12288')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Bypass UAC via CMSTP

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1548.002, T1218.003 Author: E.M. Anhaus (originally from Atomic Blue Detections, Endgame), oscd.community Status: test
Detect commandline usage of Microsoft Connection Manager Profile Installer (cmstp.exe) to install specially formatted local .INF files
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Bypass UAC via CMSTP
-- Sigma ID:     e66779cc-383e-4224-a3a4-267eeb585c40
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1548.002, T1218.003
-- Author:       E.M. Anhaus (originally from Atomic Blue Detections, Endgame), oscd.community
-- Date:         2019-10-24
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_uac_bypass_cmstp.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate use of cmstp.exe utility by legitimate user
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%/s%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%-s%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%/au%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%-au%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%/ni%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%-ni%'))
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\cmstp.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'CMSTP.EXE')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Legitimate use of cmstp.exe utility by legitimate user

CMSTP UAC Bypass via COM Object Access

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: execution, T1548.002, T1218.003 Author: Nik Seetharaman, Christian Burkard (Nextron Systems) Status: stable
Detects UAC Bypass Attempt Using Microsoft Connection Manager Profile Installer Autoelevate-capable COM Objects (e.g. UACMe ID of 41, 43, 58 or 65)
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        CMSTP UAC Bypass via COM Object Access
-- Sigma ID:     4b60e6f2-bf39-47b4-b4ea-398e33cfe253
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       stable
-- MITRE:        execution | T1548.002, T1218.003
-- Author:       Nik Seetharaman, Christian Burkard (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2019-07-31
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_uac_bypass_cmstp_com_object_access.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate CMSTP use (unlikely in modern enterprise environments)
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] AS parentImage,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentCommand')] AS parentCommandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'integrityLevel')] AS integrityLevel,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\DllHost.exe')
    AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentCommand') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentCommand')] LIKE '% /Processid:{3E5FC7F9-9A51-4367-9063-A120244FBEC7}%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentCommand')] LIKE '% /Processid:{3E000D72-A845-4CD9-BD83-80C07C3B881F}%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentCommand')] LIKE '% /Processid:{BD54C901-076B-434E-B6C7-17C531F4AB41}%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentCommand')] LIKE '% /Processid:{D2E7041B-2927-42FB-8E9F-7CE93B6DC937}%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentCommand')] LIKE '% /Processid:{E9495B87-D950-4AB5-87A5-FF6D70BF3E90}%'))
    AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'integrityLevel') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'integrityLevel')] IN ('High', 'System', 'S-1-16-16384', 'S-1-16-12288')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Legitimate CMSTP use (unlikely in modern enterprise environments)

UAC Bypass Tools Using ComputerDefaults

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1548.002 Author: Christian Burkard (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects tools such as UACMe used to bypass UAC with computerdefaults.exe (UACMe 59)
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        UAC Bypass Tools Using ComputerDefaults
-- Sigma ID:     3c05e90d-7eba-4324-9972-5d7f711a60a8
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1548.002
-- Author:       Christian Burkard (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2021-08-31
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_uac_bypass_computerdefaults.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'integrityLevel')] AS integrityLevel,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] AS parentImage,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'integrityLevel') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'integrityLevel')] IN ('High', 'System', 'S-1-16-16384', 'S-1-16-12288'))
    AND procName = 'C:\Windows\System32\ComputerDefaults.exe')
  AND NOT ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%:\\Windows\\System32%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%:\\Program Files%'))))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

UAC Bypass Using Consent and Comctl32 - Process

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1548.002 Author: Christian Burkard (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects the pattern of UAC Bypass using consent.exe and comctl32.dll (UACMe 22)
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        UAC Bypass Using Consent and Comctl32 - Process
-- Sigma ID:     1ca6bd18-0ba0-44ca-851c-92ed89a61085
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1548.002
-- Author:       Christian Burkard (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2021-08-23
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_uac_bypass_consent_comctl32.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] AS parentImage,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'integrityLevel')] AS integrityLevel,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\consent.exe')
    AND procName LIKE '%\\werfault.exe'
    AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'integrityLevel') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'integrityLevel')] IN ('High', 'System', 'S-1-16-16384', 'S-1-16-12288')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

UAC Bypass Using DismHost

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1548.002 Author: Christian Burkard (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects the pattern of UAC Bypass using DismHost DLL hijacking (UACMe 63)
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        UAC Bypass Using DismHost
-- Sigma ID:     853e74f9-9392-4935-ad3b-2e8c040dae86
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1548.002
-- Author:       Christian Burkard (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2021-08-30
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_uac_bypass_dismhost.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] AS parentImage,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'integrityLevel')] AS integrityLevel,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%C:\\Users\\%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\DismHost.exe%')
    AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'integrityLevel') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'integrityLevel')] IN ('High', 'System', 'S-1-16-16384', 'S-1-16-12288')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

UAC Bypass Using Event Viewer RecentViews

high FSM Severity: 7 Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects the pattern of UAC Bypass using Event Viewer RecentViews
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        UAC Bypass Using Event Viewer RecentViews
-- Sigma ID:     30fc8de7-d833-40c4-96b6-28319fbc4f6c
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        (none)
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2022-11-22
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_uac_bypass_eventvwr_recentviews.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\Event Viewer\\RecentViews%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\EventV~1\\RecentViews%'))
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%>%'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Bypass UAC via Fodhelper.exe

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1548.002 Author: E.M. Anhaus (originally from Atomic Blue Detections, Tony Lambert), oscd.community Status: test
Identifies use of Fodhelper.exe to bypass User Account Control. Adversaries use this technique to execute privileged processes.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Bypass UAC via Fodhelper.exe
-- Sigma ID:     7f741dcf-fc22-4759-87b4-9ae8376676a2
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1548.002
-- Author:       E.M. Anhaus (originally from Atomic Blue Detections, Tony Lambert), oscd.community
-- Date:         2019-10-24
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_uac_bypass_fodhelper.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate use of fodhelper.exe utility by legitimate user
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] AS parentImage,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\fodhelper.exe')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Legitimate use of fodhelper.exe utility by legitimate user

UAC Bypass via Windows Firewall Snap-In Hijack

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: T1548 Author: Tim Rauch, Elastic (idea) Status: test
Detects attempts to bypass User Account Control (UAC) by hijacking the Microsoft Management Console (MMC) Windows Firewall snap-in
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        UAC Bypass via Windows Firewall Snap-In Hijack
-- Sigma ID:     e52cb31c-10ed-4aea-bcb7-593c9f4a315b
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1548
-- Author:       Tim Rauch, Elastic (idea)
-- Date:         2022-09-27
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_uac_bypass_hijacking_firwall_snap_in.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] AS parentImage,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentCommand')] AS parentCommandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\mmc.exe')
    AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentCommand') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentCommand')] LIKE '%WF.msc%'))
  AND NOT (procName LIKE '%\\WerFault.exe'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

UAC Bypass via ICMLuaUtil

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1548.002 Author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Elastic (idea) Status: test
Detects the pattern of UAC Bypass using ICMLuaUtil Elevated COM interface
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        UAC Bypass via ICMLuaUtil
-- Sigma ID:     49f2f17b-b4c8-4172-a68b-d5bf95d05130
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1548.002
-- Author:       Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Elastic (idea)
-- Date:         2022-09-13
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_uac_bypass_icmluautil.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] AS parentImage,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentCommand')] AS parentCommandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\dllhost.exe')
    AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentCommand') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentCommand')] LIKE '%/Processid:{3E5FC7F9-9A51-4367-9063-A120244FBEC7}%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentCommand')] LIKE '%/Processid:{D2E7041B-2927-42FB-8E9F-7CE93B6DC937}%')))
  AND NOT ((procName LIKE '%\\WerFault.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'WerFault.exe'))))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

UAC Bypass Using IDiagnostic Profile

-- ============================================================
-- Title:        UAC Bypass Using IDiagnostic Profile

  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] AS parentImage,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentCommand')] AS parentCommandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'integrityLevel')] AS integrityLevel,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\DllHost.exe')
    AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentCommand') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentCommand')] LIKE '% /Processid:{12C21EA7-2EB8-4B55-9249-AC243DA8C666}%')
    AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'integrityLevel') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'integrityLevel')] IN ('High', 'System', 'S-1-16-16384', 'S-1-16-12288')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;

-- ============================================================
-- Title:        UAC Bypass Using IEInstal - Process

  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'integrityLevel')] AS integrityLevel,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] AS parentImage,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'integrityLevel') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'integrityLevel')] IN ('High', 'System', 'S-1-16-16384', 'S-1-16-12288'))

    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\ieinstal.exe')

UAC Bypass Using MSConfig Token Modification - Process

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1548.002 Author: Christian Burkard (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects the pattern of UAC Bypass using a msconfig GUI hack (UACMe 55)
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        UAC Bypass Using MSConfig Token Modification - Process
-- Sigma ID:     ad92e3f9-7eb6-460e-96b1-582b0ccbb980
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1548.002
-- Author:       Christian Burkard (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2021-08-30
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_uac_bypass_msconfig_gui.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'integrityLevel')] AS integrityLevel,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] AS parentImage,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'integrityLevel') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'integrityLevel')] IN ('High', 'System', 'S-1-16-16384', 'S-1-16-12288'))
    AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\pkgmgr.exe')
    AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] = '"C:\Windows\system32\msconfig.exe" -5'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

UAC Bypass Using NTFS Reparse Point - Process

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1548.002 Author: Christian Burkard (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects the pattern of UAC Bypass using NTFS reparse point and wusa.exe DLL hijacking (UACMe 36)
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        UAC Bypass Using NTFS Reparse Point - Process
-- Sigma ID:     39ed3c80-e6a1-431b-9df3-911ac53d08a7
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1548.002
-- Author:       Christian Burkard (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2021-08-30
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_uac_bypass_ntfs_reparse_point.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'integrityLevel')] AS integrityLevel,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentCommand')] AS parentCommandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '"C:\\Windows\\system32\\wusa.exe"  /quiet C:\\Users\\%')
    AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\update.msu')
    AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'integrityLevel') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'integrityLevel')] IN ('High', 'System', 'S-1-16-16384', 'S-1-16-12288')))
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentCommand') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentCommand')] = '"C:\Windows\system32\dism.exe" /online /quiet /norestart /add-package /packagepath:"C:\Windows\system32\pe386" /ignorecheck')
    AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'integrityLevel') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'integrityLevel')] IN ('High', 'System'))
    AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%C:\\Users\\%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\dismhost.exe {%')
    AND procName LIKE '%\\DismHost.exe')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

UAC Bypass Using PkgMgr and DISM

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1548.002 Author: Christian Burkard (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects the pattern of UAC Bypass using pkgmgr.exe and dism.exe (UACMe 23)

-- ============================================================
-- Title:        UAC Bypass Using PkgMgr and DISM
-- Sigma ID:     a743ceba-c771-4d75-97eb-8a90f7f4844c
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1548.002
-- Author:       Christian Burkard (Nextron Systems)

-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_uac_bypass_pkgmgr_dism.yml

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] AS parentImage,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'integrityLevel')] AS integrityLevel,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\pkgmgr.exe')
    AND procName LIKE '%\\dism.exe'
    AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'integrityLevel') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'integrityLevel')] IN ('High', 'System', 'S-1-16-16384', 'S-1-16-12288')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC

Potential UAC Bypass Via Sdclt.EXE

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: T1548.002 Author: Roberto Rodriguez (Cyb3rWard0g), OTR (Open Threat Research) Status: test
A General detection for sdclt being spawned as an elevated process. This could be an indicator of sdclt being used for bypass UAC techniques.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potential UAC Bypass Via Sdclt.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     40f9af16-589d-4984-b78d-8c2aec023197
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1548.002
-- Author:       Roberto Rodriguez (Cyb3rWard0g), OTR (Open Threat Research)
-- Date:         2020-05-02
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_uac_bypass_sdclt.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'integrityLevel')] AS integrityLevel,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (procName LIKE '%sdclt.exe'
    AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'integrityLevel') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'integrityLevel')] IN ('High', 'S-1-16-12288')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

TrustedPath UAC Bypass Pattern

critical FSM Severity: 9 MITRE: T1548.002 Author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects indicators of a UAC bypass method by mocking directories
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        TrustedPath UAC Bypass Pattern
-- Sigma ID:     4ac47ed3-44c2-4b1f-9d51-bf46e8914126
-- Level:        critical  |  FSM Severity: 9
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1548.002
-- Author:       Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2021-08-27
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_uac_bypass_trustedpath.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (procName LIKE '%C:\\Windows \\System32\\%' OR procName LIKE '%C:\\Windows \\SysWOW64\\%')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

UAC Bypass Abusing Winsat Path Parsing - Process

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1548.002 Author: Christian Burkard (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects the pattern of UAC Bypass using a path parsing issue in winsat.exe (UACMe 52)

-- ============================================================
-- Title:        UAC Bypass Abusing Winsat Path Parsing - Process
-- Sigma ID:     7a01183d-71a2-46ad-ad5c-acd989ac1793
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1548.002
-- Author:       Christian Burkard (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2021-08-30
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_uac_bypass_winsat.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'integrityLevel')] AS integrityLevel,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] AS parentImage,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentCommand')] AS parentCommandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'integrityLevel') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'integrityLevel')] IN ('High', 'System', 'S-1-16-16384', 'S-1-16-12288'))
    AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\system32\\winsat.exe')
    AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentCommand') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentCommand')] LIKE '%C:\\Windows \\system32\\winsat.exe%'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

UAC Bypass Using Windows Media Player - Process

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1548.002 Author: Christian Burkard (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects the pattern of UAC Bypass using Windows Media Player osksupport.dll (UACMe 32)
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        UAC Bypass Using Windows Media Player - Process
-- Sigma ID:     0058b9e5-bcd7-40d4-9205-95ca5a16d7b2
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1548.002
-- Author:       Christian Burkard (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2021-08-23
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_uac_bypass_wmp.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'integrityLevel')] AS integrityLevel,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'integrityLevel') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'integrityLevel')] IN ('High', 'System', 'S-1-16-16384', 'S-1-16-12288'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Bypass UAC via WSReset.exe

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1548.002 Author: E.M. Anhaus (originally from Atomic Blue Detections, Tony Lambert), oscd.community, Florian Roth Status: test
Detects use of WSReset.exe to bypass User Account Control (UAC). Adversaries use this technique to execute privileged processes.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Bypass UAC via WSReset.exe
-- Sigma ID:     d797268e-28a9-49a7-b9a8-2f5039011c5c
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1548.002
-- Author:       E.M. Anhaus (originally from Atomic Blue Detections, Tony Lambert), oscd.community, Florian Roth
-- Date:         2019-10-24
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_uac_bypass_wsreset.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown sub processes of Wsreset.exe
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] AS parentImage,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\wsreset.exe')
  AND NOT ((procName LIKE '%\\conhost.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'CONHOST.EXE'))))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown sub processes of Wsreset.exe

UAC Bypass WSReset

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1548.002 Author: Christian Burkard (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects the pattern of UAC Bypass via WSReset usable by default sysmon-config
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        UAC Bypass WSReset
-- Sigma ID:     89a9a0e0-f61a-42e5-8957-b1479565a658
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1548.002
-- Author:       Christian Burkard (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2021-08-23
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_uac_bypass_wsreset_integrity_level.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'integrityLevel')] AS integrityLevel,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\wsreset.exe'
    AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'integrityLevel') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'integrityLevel')] IN ('High', 'System', 'S-1-16-16384', 'S-1-16-12288')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Use of UltraVNC Remote Access Software

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: T1219.002 Author: frack113 Status: test
An adversary may use legitimate desktop support and remote access software,to establish an interactive command and control channel to target systems within networks
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Use of UltraVNC Remote Access Software
-- Sigma ID:     145322e4-0fd3-486b-81ca-9addc75736d8
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1219.002
-- Author:       frack113
-- Date:         2022-10-02
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_ultravnc.yml
-- Unmapped:     Description
-- False Pos:    Legitimate use
-- ============================================================
-- UNMAPPED_FIELD: Description

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'product')] AS product,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'company')] AS company,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (rawEventMsg = 'VNCViewer')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'product') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'product')] = 'UltraVNC VNCViewer'))
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'company') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'company')] = 'UltraVNC'))
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'VNCViewer.exe'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Legitimate use

Suspicious UltraVNC Execution

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1021.005 Author: Bhabesh Raj Status: test
Detects suspicious UltraVNC command line flag combination that indicate a auto reconnect upon execution, e.g. startup (as seen being used by Gamaredon threat group)
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Suspicious UltraVNC Execution
-- Sigma ID:     871b9555-69ca-4993-99d3-35a59f9f3599
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1021.005
-- Author:       Bhabesh Raj
-- Date:         2022-03-04
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_ultravnc_susp_execution.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%-autoreconnect %' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%-connect %' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%-id:%')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Uninstall Crowdstrike Falcon Sensor

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1562.001 Author: frack113 Status: test
Adversaries may disable security tools to avoid possible detection of their tools and activities by uninstalling Crowdstrike Falcon
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Uninstall Crowdstrike Falcon Sensor
-- Sigma ID:     f0f7be61-9cf5-43be-9836-99d6ef448a18
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1562.001
-- Author:       frack113
-- Date:         2021-07-12
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_uninstall_crowdstrike_falcon.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Administrator might leverage the same command line for debugging or other purposes. However this action must be always investigated
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\WindowsSensor.exe%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% /uninstall%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% /quiet%')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Administrator might leverage the same command line for debugging or other purposes. However this action must be always investigated

User Shell Folders Registry Modification via CommandLine

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: persistence, T1547.001, T1112 Author: Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems) Status: experimental
Detects modifications to User Shell Folders registry values via reg.exe or PowerShell, which could indicate persistence attempts. Attackers may modify User Shell Folders registry values to point to malicious executables or scripts that will be executed during startup. This technique is often used to maintain persistence on a compromised system by ensuring that malicious payloads are executed automatically.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        User Shell Folders Registry Modification via CommandLine
-- Sigma ID:     8f3ab69a-aa22-4943-aa58-e0a52fdf6818
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       experimental
-- MITRE:        persistence | T1547.001, T1112
-- Author:       Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2026-01-05
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_user_shell_folders_registry_modification.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Usage of reg.exe or PowerShell to modify User Shell Folders for legitimate purposes; but rare.
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% add %' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%New-ItemProperty%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%Set-ItemProperty%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%si %'))
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\Software\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Explorer\\Shell Folders%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\Software\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Explorer\\User Shell Folders%'))
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%Startup%')
  AND ((procName LIKE '%\\powershell.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\pwsh.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\reg.exe'))
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] IN ('powershell.exe', 'pwsh.dll', 'reg.exe'))))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Usage of reg.exe or PowerShell to modify User Shell Folders for legitimate purposes; but rare.

Uncommon Userinit Child Process

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: persistence, T1037.001 Author: Tom Ueltschi (@c_APT_ure), Tim Shelton Status: test
Detects uncommon "userinit.exe" child processes, which could be a sign of uncommon shells or login scripts used for persistence.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Uncommon Userinit Child Process
-- Sigma ID:     0a98a10c-685d-4ab0-bddc-b6bdd1d48458
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        persistence | T1037.001
-- Author:       Tom Ueltschi (@c_APT_ure), Tim Shelton
-- Date:         2019-01-12
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_userinit_uncommon_child_processes.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate logon scripts or custom shells may trigger false positives. Apply additional filters accordingly.
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] AS parentImage,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\userinit.exe')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Legitimate logon scripts or custom shells may trigger false positives. Apply additional filters accordingly.

Windows Credential Manager Access via VaultCmd

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: T1555.004 Author: frack113 Status: test
List credentials currently stored in Windows Credential Manager via the native Windows utility vaultcmd.exe
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Windows Credential Manager Access via VaultCmd
-- Sigma ID:     58f50261-c53b-4c88-bd12-1d71f12eda4c
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1555.004
-- Author:       frack113
-- Date:         2022-04-08
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_vaultcmd_list_creds.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%/listcreds:%')
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\VaultCmd.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'VAULTCMD.EXE')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Registry Modification Attempt Via VBScript

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: persistence, execution, T1112, T1059.005 Author: Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems) Status: experimental
Detects attempts to modify the registry using VBScript's CreateObject("Wscript.shell") and RegWrite methods via common LOLBINs. It could be an attempt to modify the registry for persistence without using straightforward methods like regedit.exe, reg.exe, or PowerShell. Threat Actors may use this technique to evade detection by security solutions that monitor for direct registry modifications through traditional tools.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Registry Modification Attempt Via VBScript
-- Sigma ID:     921aa10f-2e74-4cca-9498-98f9ca4d6fdf
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       experimental
-- MITRE:        persistence, execution | T1112, T1059.005
-- Author:       Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2025-08-13
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_vbscript_registry_modification.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%CreateObject%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%Wscript.shell%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.RegWrite%')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Verclsid.exe Runs COM Object

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: T1218 Author: Victor Sergeev, oscd.community Status: test
Detects when verclsid.exe is used to run COM object via GUID
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Verclsid.exe Runs COM Object
-- Sigma ID:     d06be4b9-8045-428b-a567-740a26d9db25
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1218
-- Author:       Victor Sergeev, oscd.community
-- Date:         2020-10-09
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_verclsid_runs_com.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND 1=1
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Virtualbox Driver Installation or Starting of VMs

low FSM Severity: 3 MITRE: T1564.006, T1564 Author: Janantha Marasinghe Status: test
Adversaries can carry out malicious operations using a virtual instance to avoid detection. This rule is built to detect the registration of the Virtualbox driver or start of a Virtualbox VM.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Virtualbox Driver Installation or Starting of VMs
-- Sigma ID:     bab049ca-7471-4828-9024-38279a4c04da
-- Level:        low  |  FSM Severity: 3
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1564.006, T1564
-- Author:       Janantha Marasinghe
-- Date:         2020-09-26
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_virtualbox_execution.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    This may have false positives on hosts where Virtualbox is legitimately being used for operations
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%VBoxRT.dll,RTR3Init%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%VBoxC.dll%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%VBoxDrv.sys%'))
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%startvm%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%controlvm%'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: This may have false positives on hosts where Virtualbox is legitimately being used for operations

Suspicious VBoxDrvInst.exe Parameters

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: persistence, T1112 Author: Konstantin Grishchenko, oscd.community Status: test
Detect VBoxDrvInst.exe run with parameters allowing processing INF file. This allows to create values in the registry and install drivers. For example one could use this technique to obtain persistence via modifying one of Run or RunOnce registry keys
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Suspicious VBoxDrvInst.exe Parameters
-- Sigma ID:     b7b19cb6-9b32-4fc4-a108-73f19acfe262
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        persistence | T1112
-- Author:       Konstantin Grishchenko, oscd.community
-- Date:         2020-10-06
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_virtualbox_vboxdrvinst_execution.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate use of VBoxDrvInst.exe utility by VirtualBox Guest Additions installation process
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\VBoxDrvInst.exe'
    AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%driver%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%executeinf%'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Legitimate use of VBoxDrvInst.exe utility by VirtualBox Guest Additions installation process

Potential Persistence Via VMwareToolBoxCmd.EXE VM State Change Script

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: execution, persistence, T1059 Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects execution of the "VMwareToolBoxCmd.exe" with the "script" and "set" flag to setup a specific script to run for a specific VM state
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potential Persistence Via VMwareToolBoxCmd.EXE VM State Change Script
-- Sigma ID:     7aa4e81a-a65c-4e10-9f81-b200eb229d7d
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        execution, persistence | T1059
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2023-06-14
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_vmware_toolbox_cmd_persistence.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% script %' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% set %')
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\VMwareToolBoxCmd.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'toolbox-cmd.exe')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Suspicious Persistence Via VMwareToolBoxCmd.EXE VM State Change Script

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: execution, persistence, T1059 Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects execution of the "VMwareToolBoxCmd.exe" with the "script" and "set" flag to setup a specific script that's located in a potentially suspicious location to run for a specific VM state
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Suspicious Persistence Via VMwareToolBoxCmd.EXE VM State Change Script
-- Sigma ID:     236d8e89-ed95-4789-a982-36f4643738ba
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        execution, persistence | T1059
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2023-06-14
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_vmware_toolbox_cmd_persistence_susp.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% script %' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% set %')
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\VMwareToolBoxCmd.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'toolbox-cmd.exe'))
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%:\\PerfLogs\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%:\\Temp\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%:\\Windows\\System32\\Tasks\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%:\\Windows\\Tasks\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%:\\Windows\\Temp\\%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\AppData\\Local\\Temp%')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

VMToolsd Suspicious Child Process

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: execution, persistence, T1059 Author: bohops, Bhabesh Raj Status: test
Detects suspicious child process creations of VMware Tools process which may indicate persistence setup
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        VMToolsd Suspicious Child Process
-- Sigma ID:     5687f942-867b-4578-ade7-1e341c46e99a
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        execution, persistence | T1059
-- Author:       bohops, Bhabesh Raj
-- Date:         2021-10-08
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_vmware_vmtoolsd_susp_child_process.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate use by VM administrator
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND 1=1
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Legitimate use by VM administrator

Potentially Suspicious Child Process Of VsCode

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: execution, T1218, T1202 Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects uncommon or suspicious child processes spawning from a VsCode "code.exe" process. This could indicate an attempt of persistence via VsCode tasks or terminal profiles.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potentially Suspicious Child Process Of VsCode
-- Sigma ID:     5a3164f2-b373-4152-93cf-090b13c12d27
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        execution | T1218, T1202
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2023-01-26
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_vscode_child_processes_anomalies.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    In development environment where VsCode is used heavily. False positives may occur when developers use task to compile or execute different types of code. Remove or add processes accordingly
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] AS parentImage,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\code.exe')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: In development environment where VsCode is used heavily. False positives may occur when developers use task to compile or execute different types of code. Remove or add processes accordingly

Visual Studio Code Tunnel Execution

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: T1071.001, T1219 Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), citron_ninja Status: test
Detects Visual Studio Code tunnel execution. Attackers can abuse this functionality to establish a C2 channel
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Visual Studio Code Tunnel Execution
-- Sigma ID:     90d6bd71-dffb-4989-8d86-a827fedd6624
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1071.001, T1219
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), citron_ninja
-- Date:         2023-10-25
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_vscode_tunnel_execution.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate use of Visual Studio Code tunnel
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentCommand')] AS parentCommandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'None')
    AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.exe tunnel'))
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentCommand') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentCommand')] LIKE '% tunnel')
    AND procName LIKE '%\\cmd.exe'
    AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%/d /c %' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\servers\\Stable-%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%code-server.cmd%'))
  OR indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%.exe tunnel%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%--accept-server-license-terms%')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Legitimate use of Visual Studio Code tunnel

Visual Studio Code Tunnel Shell Execution

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: T1071.001 Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects the execution of a shell (powershell, bash, wsl...) via Visual Studio Code tunnel. Attackers can abuse this functionality to establish a C2 channel and execute arbitrary commands on the system.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Visual Studio Code Tunnel Shell Execution
-- Sigma ID:     f4a623c2-4ef5-4c33-b811-0642f702c9f1
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1071.001
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2023-10-25
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_vscode_tunnel_remote_shell_.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate use of Visual Studio Code tunnel and running code from there
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] AS parentImage,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentCommand')] AS parentCommandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\servers\\Stable-%')
    AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\server\\node.exe')
    AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentCommand') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentCommand')] LIKE '%.vscode-server%'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Legitimate use of Visual Studio Code tunnel and running code from there

Renamed Visual Studio Code Tunnel Execution

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1071.001, T1219 Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects renamed Visual Studio Code tunnel execution. Attackers can abuse this functionality to establish a C2 channel

-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Renamed Visual Studio Code Tunnel Execution
-- Sigma ID:     2cf29f11-e356-4f61-98c0-1bdb9393d6da

-- Status:       test
