-- Date:         2023-09-28
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_vscode_tunnel_renamed_execution.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentCommand')] AS parentCommandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentCommand') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentCommand')] LIKE '% tunnel')
    AND procName LIKE '%\\cmd.exe'
    AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%/d /c %' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\servers\\Stable-%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%code-server.cmd%'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Visual Studio Code Tunnel Service Installation

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: T1071.001 Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects the installation of VsCode tunnel (code-tunnel) as a service.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Visual Studio Code Tunnel Service Installation
-- Sigma ID:     30bf1789-379d-4fdc-900f-55cd0a90a801
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1071.001
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2023-10-25
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_vscode_tunnel_service_install.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate installation of code-tunnel as a service
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%tunnel %' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%service%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%internal-run%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%tunnel-service.log%')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Legitimate installation of code-tunnel as a service

Potential Binary Proxy Execution Via VSDiagnostics.EXE

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: T1218 Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects execution of "VSDiagnostics.exe" with the "start" command in order to launch and proxy arbitrary binaries.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potential Binary Proxy Execution Via VSDiagnostics.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     ac1c92b4-ac81-405a-9978-4604d78cc47e
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1218
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2023-08-03
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_vsdiagnostics_execution_proxy.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate usage for tracing and diagnostics purposes
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% /launch:%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% -launch:%'))
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%start%')
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\VSDiagnostics.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'VSDiagnostics.exe')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Legitimate usage for tracing and diagnostics purposes

Proxy Execution via Vshadow

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: T1202 Author: David Faiss Status: experimental
Detects the invocation of vshadow.exe with the -exec parameter that executes a specified script or command after the shadow copies are created but before the VShadow tool exits. VShadow is a command-line tool that you can use to create and manage volume shadow copies. While legitimate backup or administrative scripts may use this flag, attackers can leverage this parameter to proxy the execution of malware.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Proxy Execution via Vshadow
-- Sigma ID:     d7c75059-2901-4578-b209-8837fd31c6a8
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       experimental
-- MITRE:        T1202
-- Author:       David Faiss
-- Date:         2025-05-26
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_vshadow_exec.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    System backup or administrator tools; Legitimate administrative scripts
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%-exec%')
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\vshadow.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'vshadow.exe')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: System backup or administrator tools; Legitimate administrative scripts

Suspicious Vsls-Agent Command With AgentExtensionPath Load

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: T1218 Author: bohops Status: test
Detects Microsoft Visual Studio vsls-agent.exe lolbin execution with a suspicious library load using the --agentExtensionPath parameter
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Suspicious Vsls-Agent Command With AgentExtensionPath Load
-- Sigma ID:     43103702-5886-11ed-9b6a-0242ac120002
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1218
-- Author:       bohops
-- Date:         2022-10-30
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_vslsagent_agentextensionpath_load.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    False positives depend on custom use of vsls-agent.exe
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((procName LIKE '%\\vsls-agent.exe'
    AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%--agentExtensionPath%'))
  AND NOT (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%Microsoft.VisualStudio.LiveShare.Agent.%')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: False positives depend on custom use of vsls-agent.exe

Vulnerable Driver Blocklist Registry Tampering Via CommandLine

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1562.001 Author: Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems) Status: experimental
Detects tampering of the Vulnerable Driver Blocklist registry via command line tools such as PowerShell or REG.EXE. The Vulnerable Driver Blocklist is a security feature that helps prevent the loading of known vulnerable drivers. Disabling this feature may indicate an attempt to bypass security controls, often targeted by threat actors to facilitate the installation of malicious or vulnerable drivers, particularly in scenarios involving Endpoint Detection and Response
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Vulnerable Driver Blocklist Registry Tampering Via CommandLine
-- Sigma ID:     22154f0e-5132-4a54-aa78-cc62f6def531
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       experimental
-- MITRE:        T1562.001
-- Author:       Swachchhanda Shrawan Poudel (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2026-01-26
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_vulnerable_driver_blocklist_registry_tampering.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    It is very unlikely for legitimate activities to disable the Vulnerable Driver Blocklist via command line tools; thus it is recommended to investigate promptly.
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%add %' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%New-ItemProperty %' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%Set-ItemProperty %' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%si %'))
  AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\Control\\CI\\Config%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%VulnerableDriverBlocklistEnable%')
  AND ((procName LIKE '%\\powershell.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\pwsh.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\reg.exe'))
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] IN ('PowerShell.EXE', 'pwsh.dll', 'reg.exe'))))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: It is very unlikely for legitimate activities to disable the Vulnerable Driver Blocklist via command line tools; thus it is recommended to investigate promptly.

Use of W32tm as Timer

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: discovery, T1124 Author: frack113 Status: test
When configured with suitable command line arguments, w32tm can act as a delay mechanism
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Use of W32tm as Timer
-- Sigma ID:     6da2c9f5-7c53-401b-aacb-92c040ce1215
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        discovery | T1124
-- Author:       frack113
-- Date:         2022-09-25
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_w32tm.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate use
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%/stripchart%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%/computer:%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%/period:%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%/dataonly%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%/samples:%')
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\w32tm.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'w32time.dll')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Legitimate use

Wab Execution From Non Default Location

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: execution Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects execution of wab.exe (Windows Contacts) and Wabmig.exe (Microsoft Address Book Import Tool) from non default locations as seen with bumblebee activity
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Wab Execution From Non Default Location
-- Sigma ID:     395907ee-96e5-4666-af2e-2ca91688e151
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        execution
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2022-08-12
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_wab_execution_from_non_default_location.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((procName LIKE '%\\wab.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\wabmig.exe')
  AND NOT ((procName LIKE 'C:\\Windows\\WinSxS\\%' OR procName LIKE 'C:\\Program Files\\Windows Mail\\%' OR procName LIKE 'C:\\Program Files (x86)\\Windows Mail\\%')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Wab/Wabmig Unusual Parent Or Child Processes

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: execution Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects unusual parent or children of the wab.exe (Windows Contacts) and Wabmig.exe (Microsoft Address Book Import Tool) processes as seen being used with bumblebee activity
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Wab/Wabmig Unusual Parent Or Child Processes
-- Sigma ID:     63d1ccc0-2a43-4f4b-9289-361b308991ff
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        execution
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2022-08-12
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_wab_unusual_parents.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] AS parentImage,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\wab.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\wabmig.exe'))
  OR ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\WmiPrvSE.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\svchost.exe' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\dllhost.exe'))
    AND (procName LIKE '%\\wab.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\wabmig.exe'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

All Backups Deleted Via Wbadmin.EXE

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: impact, T1490 Author: frack113, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects the deletion of all backups or system state backups via "wbadmin.exe". This technique is used by numerous ransomware families and actors. This may only be successful on server platforms that have Windows Backup enabled.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        All Backups Deleted Via Wbadmin.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     639c9081-f482-47d3-a0bd-ddee3d4ecd76
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        impact | T1490
-- Author:       frack113, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2021-12-13
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_wbadmin_delete_all_backups.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%delete%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%backup%')
    AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%keepVersions:0%'))
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\wbadmin.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'WBADMIN.EXE')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Windows Backup Deleted Via Wbadmin.EXE

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: impact, T1490 Author: frack113, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects the deletion of backups or system state backups via "wbadmin.exe". This technique is used by numerous ransomware families and actors. This may only be successful on server platforms that have Windows Backup enabled.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Windows Backup Deleted Via Wbadmin.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     89f75308-5b1b-4390-b2d8-d6b2340efaf8
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        impact | T1490
-- Author:       frack113, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2021-12-13
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_wbadmin_delete_backups.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate backup activity from administration scripts and software.
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND 1=1
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Legitimate backup activity from administration scripts and software.

Sensitive File Dump Via Wbadmin.EXE

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1003.003 Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), frack113 Status: test
Detects the dump of highly sensitive files such as "NTDS.DIT" and "SECURITY" hive. Attackers can leverage the "wbadmin" utility in order to dump sensitive files that might contain credential or sensitive information.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Sensitive File Dump Via Wbadmin.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     8b93a509-1cb8-42e1-97aa-ee24224cdc15
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1003.003
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), frack113
-- Date:         2024-05-10
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_wbadmin_dump_sensitive_files.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate backup operation by authorized administrators. Matches must be investigated and allowed on a case by case basis.
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%start%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%backup%'))
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\wbadmin.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'WBADMIN.EXE'))
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\config\\SAM%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\config\\SECURITY%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\config\\SYSTEM%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\Windows\\NTDS\\NTDS.dit%')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Legitimate backup operation by authorized administrators. Matches must be investigated and allowed on a case by case basis.

File Recovery From Backup Via Wbadmin.EXE

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: impact, T1490 Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), frack113 Status: test
Detects the recovery of files from backups via "wbadmin.exe". Attackers can restore sensitive files such as NTDS.DIT or Registry Hives from backups in order to potentially extract credentials.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        File Recovery From Backup Via Wbadmin.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     6fe4aa1e-0531-4510-8be2-782154b73b48
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        impact | T1490
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), frack113
-- Date:         2024-05-10
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_wbadmin_restore_file.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% recovery%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%recoveryTarget%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%itemtype:File%')
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\wbadmin.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'WBADMIN.EXE')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Sensitive File Recovery From Backup Via Wbadmin.EXE

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1003.003 Author: Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), frack113 Status: test
Detects the dump of highly sensitive files such as "NTDS.DIT" and "SECURITY" hive. Attackers can leverage the "wbadmin" utility in order to dump sensitive files that might contain credential or sensitive information.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Sensitive File Recovery From Backup Via Wbadmin.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     84972c80-251c-4c3a-9079-4f00aad93938
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1003.003
-- Author:       Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems), frack113
-- Date:         2024-05-10
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_wbadmin_restore_sensitive_files.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% recovery%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%recoveryTarget%' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%itemtype:File%')
    AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\config\\SAM%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\config\\SECURITY%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\config\\SYSTEM%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\Windows\\NTDS\\NTDS.dit%')))
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\wbadmin.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'WBADMIN.EXE')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Potentially Suspicious WebDAV LNK Execution

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: execution, T1059.001, T1204 Author: Micah Babinski Status: test
Detects possible execution via LNK file accessed on a WebDAV server.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potentially Suspicious WebDAV LNK Execution
-- Sigma ID:     1412aa78-a24c-4abd-83df-767dfb2c5bbe
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        execution | T1059.001, T1204
-- Author:       Micah Babinski
-- Date:         2023-08-21
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_webdav_lnk_execution.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] AS parentImage,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\explorer.exe')
    AND (procName LIKE '%\\cmd.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\cscript.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\mshta.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\powershell.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\pwsh.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\wscript.exe')
    AND indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%\\DavWWWRoot\\%'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Chopper Webshell Process Pattern

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: persistence, discovery, T1505.003, T1018, T1033, T1087 Author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), MSTI (query) Status: test
Detects patterns found in process executions cause by China Chopper like tiny (ASPX) webshells
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Chopper Webshell Process Pattern
-- Sigma ID:     fa3c117a-bc0d-416e-a31b-0c0e80653efb
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        persistence, discovery | T1505.003, T1018, T1033, T1087
-- Author:       Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), MSTI (query)
-- Date:         2022-10-01
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_webshell_chopper.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] AS parentImage,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND ((indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%&ipconfig&echo%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%&quser&echo%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%&whoami&echo%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%&c:&echo%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%&cd&echo%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%&dir&echo%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%&echo [E]%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%&echo [S]%'))
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\w3wp.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\w3wp.exe')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Webshell Hacking Activity Patterns

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: persistence, discovery, T1505.003, T1018, T1033, T1087 Author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects certain parent child patterns found in cases in which a web shell is used to perform certain credential dumping or exfiltration activities on a compromised system
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Webshell Hacking Activity Patterns
-- Sigma ID:     4ebc877f-4612-45cb-b3a5-8e3834db36c9
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        persistence, discovery | T1505.003, T1018, T1033, T1087
-- Author:       Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2022-03-17
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_webshell_hacking.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unlikely
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND 1=1
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unlikely

Webshell Detection With Command Line Keywords

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: persistence, discovery, T1505.003, T1018, T1033, T1087 Author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Jonhnathan Ribeiro, Anton Kutepov, oscd.community, Chad Hudson, Matt Anderson Status: test
Detects certain command line parameters often used during reconnaissance activity via web shells
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Webshell Detection With Command Line Keywords
-- Sigma ID:     bed2a484-9348-4143-8a8a-b801c979301c
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        persistence, discovery | T1505.003, T1018, T1033, T1087
-- Author:       Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Jonhnathan Ribeiro, Anton Kutepov, oscd.community, Chad Hudson, Matt Anderson
-- Date:         2017-01-01
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_webshell_recon_commands_and_processes.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND 1=1
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Suspicious Process By Web Server Process

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: persistence, T1505.003, T1190 Author: Thomas Patzke, Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Zach Stanford @svch0st, Tim Shelton, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects potentially suspicious processes being spawned by a web server process which could be the result of a successfully placed web shell or exploitation
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Suspicious Process By Web Server Process
-- Sigma ID:     8202070f-edeb-4d31-a010-a26c72ac5600
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        persistence | T1505.003, T1190
-- Author:       Thomas Patzke, Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Zach Stanford @svch0st, Tim Shelton, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2019-01-16
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_webshell_susp_process_spawned_from_webserver.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Particular web applications may spawn a shell process legitimately
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\arp.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\at.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\bash.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\bitsadmin.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\certutil.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\cmd.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\cscript.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\dsget.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\hostname.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\nbtstat.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\net.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\net1.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\netdom.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\netsh.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\nltest.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\ntdsutil.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\powershell\_ise.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\powershell.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\pwsh.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\qprocess.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\query.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\qwinsta.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\reg.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\rundll32.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\sc.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\sh.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\wmic.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\wscript.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\wusa.exe')
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Particular web applications may spawn a shell process legitimately

Webshell Tool Reconnaissance Activity

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: persistence, T1505.003 Author: Cian Heasley, Florian Roth (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects processes spawned from web servers (PHP, Tomcat, IIS, etc.) that perform reconnaissance looking for the existence of popular scripting tools (perl, python, wget) on the system via the help commands
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Webshell Tool Reconnaissance Activity
-- Sigma ID:     f64e5c19-879c-4bae-b471-6d84c8339677
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        persistence | T1505.003
-- Author:       Cian Heasley, Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2020-07-22
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_webshell_tool_recon.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%perl --help%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%perl -h%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%python --help%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%python -h%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%python3 --help%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%python3 -h%' OR metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '%wget --help%'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Potential Credential Dumping Via WER

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1003.001 Author: @pbssubhash , Nasreddine Bencherchali Status: test
Detects potential credential dumping via Windows Error Reporting LSASS Shtinkering technique which uses the Windows Error Reporting to dump lsass
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potential Credential Dumping Via WER
-- Sigma ID:     9a4ccd1a-3526-4d99-b980-9f9c5d3a6ff3
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1003.001
-- Author:       @pbssubhash , Nasreddine Bencherchali
-- Date:         2022-12-08
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_werfault_lsass_shtinkering.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Windows Error Reporting might produce similar behavior. In that case, check the PID associated with the "-p" parameter in the CommandLine.
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND 1=1
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Windows Error Reporting might produce similar behavior. In that case, check the PID associated with the "-p" parameter in the CommandLine.

Potential ReflectDebugger Content Execution Via WerFault.EXE

medium FSM Severity: 5 MITRE: execution, T1036 Author: X__Junior (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects execution of "WerFault.exe" with the "-pr" commandline flag that is used to run files stored in the ReflectDebugger key which could be used to store the path to the malware in order to masquerade the execution flow
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Potential ReflectDebugger Content Execution Via WerFault.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     fabfb3a7-3ce1-4445-9c7c-3c27f1051cdd
-- Level:        medium  |  FSM Severity: 5
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        execution | T1036
-- Author:       X__Junior (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2023-06-30
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_werfault_reflect_debugger_exec.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% -pr %')
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\WerFault.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'WerFault.exe')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

PPL Tampering Via WerFaultSecure

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1562.001, T1003.001 Author: Jason (https://github.com/0xbcf) Status: experimental
Detects potential abuse of WerFaultSecure.exe to dump Protected Process Light (PPL) processes like LSASS or to freeze security solutions (EDR/antivirus). This technique is used by tools such as EDR-Freeze and WSASS to bypass PPL protections and access sensitive information or disable security software. Distinct command line patterns help identify the specific tool: - WSASS usage typically shows: "WSASS.exe WerFaultSecure.exe [PID]" in ParentCommandLine - EDR-Freeze usage typically shows: "EDR-Freeze_[version].exe [PID] [timeout]" in ParentCommandLine Legitimate debugging operations using WerFaultSecure are rare in production environments and should be investigated.
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        PPL Tampering Via WerFaultSecure
-- Sigma ID:     1f0b4cac-9c81-41f4-95d0-8475ff46b3e2
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       experimental
-- MITRE:        T1562.001, T1003.001
-- Author:       Jason (https://github.com/0xbcf)
-- Date:         2025-09-23
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_werfaultsecure_abuse.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Legitimate usage of WerFaultSecure for debugging purposes
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] AS commandLine,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] AS originalFileName,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'command') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% /h %' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% /pid %' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% /tid %' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% /encfile %' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% /cancel %' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% /type %' AND metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'command')] LIKE '% 268310%')
  AND (procName LIKE '%\\WerFaultSecure.exe')
  OR (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'originalFileName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'originalFileName')] = 'WerFaultSecure.exe')))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Legitimate usage of WerFaultSecure for debugging purposes

Suspicious Child Process Of Wermgr.EXE

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: T1055, T1036 Author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects suspicious Windows Error Reporting manager (wermgr.exe) child process
View on GitHub ↗ 
-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Suspicious Child Process Of Wermgr.EXE
-- Sigma ID:     396f6630-f3ac-44e3-bfc8-1b161bc00c4e
-- Level:        high  |  FSM Severity: 7
-- Status:       test
-- MITRE:        T1055, T1036
-- Author:       Florian Roth (Nextron Systems)
-- Date:         2022-10-14
-- GitHub:       https://github.com/SigmaHQ/sigma/blob/main/rules/windows/process_creation/proc_creation_win_wermgr_susp_child_process.yml
-- Unmapped:     (none)
-- False Pos:    Unknown
-- ============================================================

SELECT
  phRecvTime,
  reptDevName,
  reptDevIpAddrV4,
  user,
  procName,
  metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] AS parentImage,
  rawEventMsg
FROM fsiem.events
WHERE eventType IN ('Win-Sysmon-1-Create-Process', 'Win-Security-4688')
  AND phRecvTime >= now() - INTERVAL 24 HOUR
  AND (indexOf(metrics_string.name, 'parentProcName') > 0
    AND (metrics_string.value[indexOf(metrics_string.name,'parentProcName')] LIKE '%\\wermgr.exe')
    AND (procName LIKE '%\\cmd.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\cscript.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\ipconfig.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\mshta.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\net.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\net1.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\netstat.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\nslookup.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\powershell\_ise.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\powershell.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\pwsh.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\regsvr32.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\rundll32.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\systeminfo.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\whoami.exe' OR procName LIKE '%\\wscript.exe'))
ORDER BY phRecvTime DESC
LIMIT 1000;
False Positives: Unknown

Suspicious Execution Location Of Wermgr.EXE

high FSM Severity: 7 MITRE: execution Author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems) Status: test
Detects suspicious Windows Error Reporting manager (wermgr.exe) execution location.

-- ============================================================
-- Title:        Suspicious Execution Location Of Wermgr.EXE
